从我们的全加载组件库 Material UI 开始，或者将您自己的设计系统带入我们的生产就绪组件。

在最后一项之前

事实上，我已经包含了我们在构建网站时需要的大部分资源。 可能有我的不足，忘记的东西，也可能有更好的，你可以在评论中指出，我会很高兴，我可以和他们一起写后续文章😇

但除此之外，还有最后一个项目我想和你谈谈。 你写了一个标题说它会让我们的生活更轻松，但你可能会说将所有这些添加到一个项目中会让我们的生活更轻松。 此时，我们遇到了“refine”💪

精炼是一个基于 React 的框架，用于快速构建内部工具。 它是辅助钩子、组件和提供者的集合。 它们都与您的 UI 组件和业务逻辑分离，因此它们永远不会阻止您自定义 UI 或编写自己的流程。

精炼为快速开发提供了许多开箱即用的功能，而不会影响极端的可定制性。 用例包括但不限于管理面板、B2B 应用程序和仪表板。

如果您正在寻找一个框架，可以让您的数据密集型应用程序以极快的速度运行并具有极高的可定制性，那么您只需优化即可。 这个基于 React 的无头系统专为提高速度而设计 - 允许用户带来自己的 UI 并通过refine 的强大界面进行更新！

原文:

There are so many ways to make web development more efficient, and I found some amazing resources that will help you save time⌛

Table of Contents

1. Lorem Ipsum and Lorem Picsum

Lorem Ipsum is defined as a block of imitation writing.

If you need dummy photos for your content, Lorem Picsum is the place for you.

2. Squoosh

Squoosh compressing your images will almost unnoticeable quality loss, and you can edit their size or how much is lost making it a must if optimizing the image for web use.

3. JSON Formatter

JSON Formatter and JSON Validator help to format and validate your JSON text. It also provides a tree view that helps to navigate your formatted JSON data.

We create our dummy content. Now, How do we store them? Let's keep reading 👇

4. Supabase

Create a backend with Supabase in less than 2 minutes. Start your project with a Postgres Database, Authentication, instant APIs, Realtime subscriptions and Storage.

Now it's time to show our amazing content on the website 🎉

5. Tailwind

Tailwind CSS works by scanning all of your HTML files, JavaScript components, and any other templates for class names, generating the corresponding styles and then writing them to a static CSS file.

It's fast, flexible, and reliable — with zero-runtime.

6. Ant Design

Ant Design is a design system for enterprise-level products. Create an efficient and enjoyable work experience.

7. Material UI

Material UI offers a comprehensive suite of UI tools to help you ship new features faster. Start with Material UI, our fully-loaded component library, or bring your own design system to our production-ready components.

8. auth0

The Auth0 identity platform provides a secure, streamlined way to manage your users with ease.

9. Cerbos

Cerbos provides complete authorization for your product while enabling collaborative management of permissions.

Before the last item

In fact, I've included most of the resources we'll need when building a website. There may be my shortcomings, things I forgot, or there may be better ones, you can specify them in the comments, I would be very happy, I can write a follow-up article with them 😇

But apart from all these, there is one last item that I want to talk to you about. You wrote a title saying it will make our life easier, but you may say how adding all these to a project will make our life easier. At this point, we come across "refine" 💪

10. refine

refine is a React-based framework for rapid building of internal tools. It's is a a collection of helper hooks, components and providers. They are all decoupled from your UI components and business logic, so they never keep you from customizing your UI or coding your own flow.

refine offers lots of out-of-the box functionality for rapid development, without compromising extreme customizability. Use-cases include, but are not limited to admin panels, B2B applications and dashboards.

If you're looking for a framework that will let your data- intensive application run at top speeds with extreme customizability, then look no further than refine. This React based headless system has been designed specifically for speed - allowing users to bring their own UI and update it via refine's powerful interface!

Key features ​

🔥 Headless : So you can bring your own UI and fuel it with Refine for top speed development.

Items.Title: 为您的下一个应用程序尝试的最佳开源无头 CMS | Best Open-Source Headless CMS to Try for Your Next Application
Items.Description:
一个可以快速开发的无头CMS系统，包含您需要的许多功能，并且与传统系统相比，可以帮助您非常快速地生产产品。

什么是无头 CMS？

没有前端系统，因此得名“headless”。

它允许您创建、存储、管理和修改您的内容。 即使您没有任何编码或技术专长，您也可以使用 WordPress、Wix、Joomla 等优秀的 CMS 来创建网站并立即开始发布内容。

然而，随着技术的发展和需求的变化，传统的系统已经变成了繁琐、难以开发的系统。

在本文中，我将讨论您可以考虑在下一个项目中使用的无头开源 cms。

斯特拉皮

它是继Strapi之后适用于您的应用程序的领先开源无头 CMS。 它是可定制的，并且 100% JavaScript 使得构建高性能、可定制和自托管的内容 API 变得容易和简单。

自定义 API 和管理面板很容易。 它已通过插件变得更加强大。 它具有先进的面向开发人员的 SDK。

功能有限的免费服务。 从每月 9 美元起的付费计划中获得更多好处。

直连

它是使用Directus将 SQL 数据库变成漂亮的无代码应用程序和 API 的最佳开放数据平台之一。 用创新的解决方案加强您的项目。

您可以在自己的服务器上免费使用 Directus。 使用云解决方案，您只需每月支付 25 美元即可轻松使用它。

将Netlify CMS 与静态站点构建器结合使用，构建灵活、更快的 Web 项目。 它还有助于在 Git 中处理多线程和处理内容更新。

它被设计成一个单页的 React 应用程序； 因此您可以添加 UI 小部件、编辑器插件和后端来支持各种 Git 平台 API。

鬼

使用Ghost开始您的无头 CMS 之旅，并学习如何创建精美的独立出版物。 从本地环境到产品开发，按照安装指南轻松安装软件。 您可以使用本指南设计自己的模板。 通过了解 Ghost 的配置和架构，您可以创建可以创建的新网站、应用程序等。

基石

使用Keystone构建更快的内容。 只需描述您的架构并获得用于数据和内容的出色管理 UI 和 GraphQL API。

在不牺牲后端灵活性的情况下使用 API 提交代码。 它提供多种字段类型、会话管理、自定义模式、访问控制、数据库迁移、强大的过滤器、关系数据、事件挂钩、自动化 CRUD 等等。

细化

如果您正在寻找一个可以为您的应用程序提供极端可定制性的框架， refine将是一个非常好的选择。 结论

因此，如果您正在寻找开源无头 CMS 系统，请根据您的需要查看上述选项。



原文:

A headless CMS system, which can be developed quickly, contains many features you need, and helps you to produce products very quickly compared to traditional systems.

What is a headless CMS?

Headless CMS are basically systems that distribute content with a database and restfull API. There is no front-end system, hence the name “headless”.

It allows you to create, store, manage and modify your content. Even if you don't have any coding or technical expertise, you can create a website and start publishing content right away using a good CMS like WordPress, Wix, Joomla.

However, with the developing technology and changing needs, traditional systems have turned into cumbersome, difficult to develop systems.

In this article, I will talk about headless open source cms that you can consider using in your next project.

Strapi

It is the leading open source headless CMS for your applications after Strapi. It is customizable and 100% JavaScript that makes it easy and simple to build a performant, customizable and self-hosted content API.

Strapi is an open source platform and has many contributors. It is easy to customize the API and admin panel. It has become more powerful with plugins. It has an advanced and developer-oriented SDK.

Free service with limited features. Get more benefits with the paid plan starting at $9/month.

Directus

It is one of the best Open Data Platforms for turning SQL databases into beautiful no-code apps and APIs with Directus. Strengthens your project with innovative solutions.

You can use Directus for free on your own server. With the Cloud solution, you can use it effortlessly by paying $25/month.

Netlify CMS

Build flexible and faster web projects using Netlify CMS in combination with the static site builder. Netlify CMS stores your content in a Git repository for easier and simpler versioning. It also helps with multi-threading and handling content updates within Git.

It is designed as a one-page React app; so you can add UI widgets, editor plugins and backends to support various Git platform APIs.

Ghost

Start your headless CMS journey with Ghost and learn how to create beautiful standalone publications. From local environment to product development, follow the installation guide and install the software easily. You can design your own templates using this guide. By understanding Ghost's configuration and architecture, you can create new websites, apps, etc. you can create.

Keystone

Build faster content with the Keystone. Just describe your schema and get awesome Management UI and GraphQL API for data and content.

Submit the code with APIs without sacrificing the flexibility of the backend. It offers multiple field types, session management, custom schema, access control, database migrations, powerful filters, relational data, event hooks, automated CRUD, and more.

Refine

If you are looking for a framework that will provide extreme customizability to your application, refine will be a very good alternative. Refine doesn't have any back-end support yet, but if you have a rest-full API or GraphQL api it allows you to build very flexible applications.

Refine comes with Ant Design and can also be used headless if you prefer.

Conclusion

Headless CMS systems offer a modern and efficient approach to creating and distributing content across multiple channels with high performance, flexibility, scalability and a host of useful features.

So, if you are looking for an open source headless CMS system, check out the above options based on your needs.
Items.Author: yildirayunlu

Items.Title: C#中Array和List的区别（内存使用+性能） | The difference between Array and List in C# (Memory Usage + Performance)
Items.Description:
为了有效地使用它们，您需要了解它们之间的差异，以便您可以根据自己的目的选择合适的。

首先让我们快速回顾一下什么是 Array和什么是 List的基本概念：

什么是数组？

数组是元素的集合或序列，可以是不同的类型（字符串、双精度、整数、浮点等），但它们必须是单一类型。 它允许在单个变量中存储许多相同类型的变量。

假设我们有一个存储holidays的日历，然后，我们将多个假期存储在一个变量中。

 string [] holidays = { "christmas" , "easter" , "new year" };

它们的主要特征之一是它们始终具有定义的长度，该长度等于适合内部的元素数量。

什么是列表？

让我们看看 List 中的假期示例是什么样子的：

 var holidays = new List < string >(); days . Add ( "christmas" ); days . Add ( "easter" ); days . Add ( "new year" );

现在你会告诉我，我来这里只是为了解释这种差异，但不是，让我们慢慢开始：

什么是内存使用情况以及有哪些类型？

以非常基本的方式解释的内存是应用程序“记住”在一段时间内使用的信息或数据的空间。 它就像人类的记忆，它保留了事物。

当我们谈论内存使用时，它经常与它在磁盘上使用的内存大小相混淆，这就是产生疑问的地方。

让我们快速回顾一下存在的 2 种内存类型：

  • 静态内存：在编译时定义的内存类型。 此内存空间是为变量保留的，在运行时不能更改。

  • 动态内存：运行时使用的内存类型。 这个内存空间也是为变量保留的，但在这种情况下，它可以在运行时修改。

或多或少地清楚静态内存和动态内存之间的区别。 对于 Array 和 List 之间的比较，让我们关注我们现在感兴趣的一个：动态内存。

解释动态内存（数组）

假设我们在动态内存中有空间，让我们考虑单元格，每个单元格都存储一个值，现在，假设我们已经有几个单元格被某些东西占用了。 让我们拿出油漆：

现在，让我们假设我们想要将我们之前的数组添加到内存中，即带有假期的数组：

 string [] holidays = { "christmas" , "easter" , "new year" };

结果将是这样的：

好吧，一切都很好，但是……如果我想在数组中添加另一个元素怎么办？ 例如法利亚斯（这是一个典型的西班牙假期）。

如果一开始你认为它会进入下一个单元格（在第一个 X 之后，圣诞节之后），你失败了。

这是 Array 的主要特点，它在内存中必须始终是连续的。 即 Array 的所有元素必须彼此相邻，不能分开。

因此，如果我们添加法利亚斯假期，结果将如下所示：

现在，已经清楚了 Array 在内存中的工作方式，让我们看看 List 在内存中的工作方式。

解释动态内存（列表）

列表与节点一起工作，一个节点由一个值和一个指向下一个节点的指针组成。 还有 start 和 end 指针，这意味着如果我想要new year的值（以假期为例），我会简单地跟随指针直到我得到它：

 var holidays = new List < string >(); days . Add ( "christmas" ); days . Add ( "easter" ); days . Add ( "new year" );

让我们想象在这个 Lists 是如何工作的方案中，我们想要添加fallas的假期。 好吧，最后一个元素（即new year ）的指针将指向一个新元素（即fallas ），而最后一个元素的指针将指向 final 指针。 让我们看看这个例子：

现在，在列表的内存中添加一个新元素（ fallas ）会是什么感觉？ 让我们记住我们的方案：

在这种情况下，是的，因为列表元素使用指向下一个元素的指针，新元素可以进入下一个空闲单元格。 他们不必一个接一个地去。 这就是它的样子：

列表的元素不需要全部一起排成一行。

现在，您知道 Array 和 List 在内存方面的区别，但是性能呢？ 我想你已经有了一个想法，但让我们再深入一点。

性能基准：数组与列表

让我们从实践开始吧！ 我们将有一个类，它将在循环中加载一组记录，除此之外几乎没有：

我们将对列表和数组进行相同的测试：

  • <int>
  • <object>
  • <long>
  • <ulong>

您也可以运行测试。 我将留下 Gist 链接，因此您也可以尝试一下（我没有嵌入要点，因为它很长）：🚀 Array vs List benchmark

在运行测试时，我们发现：

瞧，数组可以比列表快 97% （显然，这是一个综合测试，在实际测试中差异会更小）。

我希望解释清楚，现在您知道 Array 和 List 在内存和性能方面的区别！ 如果您喜欢它，请通过喜欢和保存帖子向我展示，以便我可以为您提供更好的内容。

我们要感谢Dotnetsafer 抽出宝贵时间阅读本文。

请记住：现在您可以免费试用我们的.NET 混淆器。 您还可以使用.NET Obfuscator for Visual Studio直接从 Visual Studio 保护您的应用程序。



原文:

What are the differences between an Array and List in C#? An Array and List both serve as collections of values, but they differ in how they store their contents in memory and how they can be accessed. In order to use either effectively, you'll need to understand the differences between them so that you can pick the right one for your purposes.

First let's quickly review the basic concepts of what is an Array and what is a List:

What is an Array?

An Array is a set or sequence of elements and can be of different types (string, double, int, float, etc…) but they have to be of a single type. It allows to store many variables of the same type inside a single variable.

Let's imagine that we have a calendar that stores the holidays, then, we are storing several holidays in a single variable.

string[] holidays = {"christmas", "easter", "new year"};

One of their main characteristics is that they always have a defined length, which is equal to the number of elements that fit inside.

What is an List?

A List is a group of objects and can be of different types (they do not have to be all of the same type as in the Array).

Let's see what the example of holidays in a List would look like:

var holidays = new List<string>(); days.Add("christmas"); days.Add("easter"); days.Add("new year");

The main characteristic of a List (difference with an Array) is that its length does not have to be defined, they can have a variable length.

Now you will tell me that I have only come here to explain that difference but no, let's start slowly:

What is memory usage and what types are there?

Memory explained in a very basic way, is the space in which the application "remembers" information or data that is used over a period of time. It is like the human memory, which retains things.

When we talk about memory usage, it is often confused with the size of the memory it uses on the disk and this is where doubts arise.

Let's quickly review the 2 types of memory that exist:

  • Static memory: The type of memory that is defined at compile time. This memory space is reserved for a variable and cannot be changed at runtime.

  • Dynamic memory: The type of memory used at runtime. This memory space is also reserved for a variable, but in this case it can be modified at run time.

Being more or less clear about the difference between static memory and dynamic memory. For this comparison between Array and List, let's focus on the one we are interested in right now: Dynamic memory.

Explaining dynamic memory (Array)

Suppose we have space in dynamic memory, let's think in cells and each cell stores a value and now, imagine that we already have several cells occupied by something. Let's take out Paint:

Now, let's imagine that we want to add to the memory our previous Array, the one with the holidays:

string[] holidays = {"christmas", "easter", "new year"};

The result would be something like this:

Well, all good but… what if I wanted to add another element to the Array? For example fallas (it's a typical Spanish holiday).

If at first you think it would go in the next cell (after the first X, below Christmas), you failed.

This is the main characteristic of the Array, it must always be continuous in memory. That is, all the elements of the Array must be next to each other, they cannot be separated.

So, if we add the fallas holiday, the result would be as follows:

📚Remember: The Array will always look for the required number of free cells next to each other to place their values there.

Now, having clear how an Array works in memory, let's see how a List works in memory.

Explaining dynamic memory (List)

Lists work with nodes, a node consists of a value and a pointer to the next node. There are also start and end pointers, which means that if I want the value new year (taking the example of holidays), I would simply follow the pointers until I get to it:

var holidays = new List<string>(); days.Add("christmas"); days.Add("easter"); days.Add("new year");

Let's imagine in this scheme of how the Lists work, we want to add the holiday of fallas. Well, simply the pointer of the last element (that is new year) will point to a new element (that will be fallas) and the pointer of this last one, will point to the final pointer. Let's see the example:

Now, what would it be like to add a new element (fallas) in memory of a list? Let's remember our scheme:

And in this case, yes, as list elements work with pointers pointing to the next element, the new element could go in the next free cell. They do not have to go one after the other. This is how it would look like:

📚Remember: The list can continue in the next free cell. The elements of a list do not need to be all together in a row.

Now, you know the differences between an Array and a List in terms of memory, but what about performance? I think you already have an idea but let's go a little deeper.

Performance benchmark: Array vs List

Let's start with practice! We are going to have a class that is going to load a set of records inside a loop, little else:

The tests we will do will be the same for lists and arrays:

  • <int>
  • <object>
  • <long>
  • <ulong>

You can also run the test. I'm going to leave the Gist link so you can try it too (I don't embed the gist because it gets long): 🚀Array vs List benchmark

When running the test we found this:

Voilà, Arrays can be up to 97% faster than Lists (obvious, this is a synthetic test and in real tests the difference will be smaller).

I hope the explanation was clear, now you know the differences between an Array and a List both in terms of memory and performance! If you liked it, show it to me with liking and saving post so I can give you better content.

Items.Title: 什么技能造就了一个伟大的软件工程师？ | What Skills Makes a Great Software Engineer?
Items.Description:
在我的职业生涯中，我曾与来自世界各地、有着不同背景和经验的人一起工作🌎。 其中一些我很喜欢与他们一起工作，而其中一些我没有。

最近我一直在思考为什么我更喜欢和一些人一起工作。 我喜欢与具备以下技能的工程师一起工作：

  • 如您所见，这些都是软技能。 我宁愿和一个不是那么优秀的程序员合作，而是努力成为一个团队成员，而不是一个不听别人意见的“摇滚明星程序员”。

    我看到很多关于如何提高技术技能的文章和博客文章，它们很有用，但软技能至少同样重要，甚至更重要！ 技术技能很容易学习，但软技能更难（从个人经验谈起）。

    所以现在我很好奇，你认为什么技能可以造就一个伟大的软件工程师？ 最初发布于https://prplcode.dev



原文:

In my career I've worked with people from different parts of the world with diverse backgrounds and experiences 🌎. Some of them I've enjoyed working with, and some of them I have not.

Recently I've been thinking about why I like working with some more than others. I enjoy working with engineers who have these skills:

  • 🙂 Friendly and easy to communicate with
  • 👂 Listen to others and try to see things from other perspectives
  • ✍️ Thorough in their work
  • 👫 Team player, striving to put the team first
  • 💪🤙 Strong opinions, loosely held
  • 👩‍🏫 Wants to learn
  • 🙋 Asks a lot of questions

As you can see these are all soft skills. I'd rather work with someone who's not that great of a programmer, but working to be, and is a team player, than someone who's a "rockstar programmer" who doesn't listen to others.

I see many articles and blog posts about how to improve your technical skills, and they are useful, but the soft skills are at least as important, if not more! Technical skills are easy to learn but the soft skills are harder (talking from personal experience).

So now I'm curios, what skills do you think makes a great software engineer? 😊

Originally published at https://prplcode.dev
Items.Author: simeg

Items.Title: 大辩论！？ | The big debate!?
Items.Description:
娄威洛比

发表于我经常看到和听到这一点，HTML 和 CSS 不被认为是“真正的”语言。

我理解这是因为它们只确定网站的结构，而不像其他语言那样给出一套说明。 但是，当您在 Google 上搜索时，会有许多不同的文章，每篇文章都对这个主题发表了自己的看法。 这一切实际上是一个意见问题，还是有一个直接的事实可以说是或否？

随着时间的推移，我在社交媒体上看到了很多关于这个的模因和争论，但从来没有真正考虑过，只是当我偶然发现有人在网上发布他们的简历/作品集网站时说他们知道 HTML 和 CSS，而有人告诉他们不知道到，这让我质疑为什么？ 为什么不能有人自豪地说他们知道 HMTL 和 CSS，这是您作为开发人员学习的技能，甚至可能是您实际上学习的第一个技能。

它们可能是基本的，但您仍然必须知道如何正确编写它们，原因之一是可访问性和正确的网站结构。 我知道有一些框架可以为你做很多这样的事情，但如果你正在编写香草，你需要了解你的东西。

你有什么想法？ 迈克尔霍夫曼 - Apr 28 4月28日



原文:

Lou Willoughby

Posted on May 2

I see and hear this a lot, HTML and CSS are not considered ”real” languages.

I understand this is because they only determine the structure of a website and don’t give a set of instructions per say like other languages. When you google this however there are many different articles each giving their own opinion on the subject. Is this all actually a matter of opinion or is there a straight up fact to say yes or no?

I’ve seen many memes about this over time and arguments on social media but never gave it a real thought, it was only when I stumbled across someone posting their CV/portfolio website online that said they know HTML and CSS and someone told them not to, this made me question why? Why can’t someone proudly say they know HMTL and CSS, it’s a skill you learn as a developer, maybe even the first you learn in fact.

They may be basic but you still have to know how to write them correctly, for one reason accessibility and yes correct structure of your website. CSS is also another kettle of fish, there’s so much to learn about CSS! I know there’s frameworks out there that do a lot of this for you but if you’re writing vanilla you need to know your stuff.

What’s your thoughts? 👀🤔

Michael Hoffmann - Apr 28
Items.Author: louiseann93

Items.Title: 为电子商务平台构建应用程序的问题以及我们构建通用忍者的原因 | The Problems With Building Apps for E-Commerce Platforms and Why We Built Common Ninja
Items.Description:
让我们从底线开始：

为电子商务平台构建应用程序很困难。

背景

电子商务平台（例如 Shopify、BigCommerce、Wix、WooCommerce 等）非常适合想要在线建立和管理商店的人。 它们为店主提供了管理库存、定制店面以及开始从在线订单中赚取收入的最佳工具。

但归根结底，这些平台是有限的，这就是为什么它们允许开发人员利用他们的 API 和数据在它们之上开发应用程序的原因。

这些应用程序正在被商家及其客户使用，而且种类繁多。 从提高转化率和增强用户体验的店面小部件，到收集数据以改善库存管理的应用程序，再到社交证明应用程序和轻松的直接运输。

电子商务应用程序被在线商家广泛使用，并变得非常流行。 有多受欢迎？ 事情是...

如果您问任何有为电子商务平台构建应用程序经验的开发人员“您的经验如何？” 你会得到同样的答案：“这简直是一场噩梦”。

开发者电子商务平台的问题

当您第一次开始构建应用程序时，您正在进入一个痛苦的世界。

它从文档开始。 为了构建一个应用程序，您需要阅读。 很多。 虽然有些平台在将开发人员加入他们的平台并记录所有内容方面做得很好，但其他平台却没有。

一旦您在无尽的文档中找到了自己的方式，并了解了每个平台允许您构建的不同类型的应用程序，接下来的事情就是处理身份验证。

令人惊讶的是，尽管大多数平台都在使用OAuth ，但每个平台都以不同的方式实现了身份验证流程，因此您需要在使用隧道时分别准确了解它在每个平台上的工作原理（并且在某些情况下在生产环境中调试代码，因为您必须联系平台才能更改身份验证端点）。

我希望你还在这里，因为还有更多！

接下来是API。 我们以Product资源为例。

在 Shopify 中，产品对象如下所示：

 interface IShopifyProduct { id?: string; title?: string; body_html?: string; vendor?: string; product_type?: string; created_at?: string; updated_at?: string; handle?: string; variants?: { id?: string; product_id?: string; price?: string; // no unit supported in api weight?: number; weight_unit?: string; }[] images?: IShopifyProductImage[]; status?: 'active' | 'archived' | 'draft'; tags?: string; } interface IShopifyProductImage { product_id?: number; id?: number; title?: string; position?: number; created_at?: string; updated_at?: string; alt?: string; weight?: number; weight_unit?: 'kg' | 'lb'; price?: number | string; src?: string; variant_ids?: string[]; admin_graphql_api_id?: string; }

在 BigCommerce 中是这样的：

 interface IBigCommerceProduct { id?: number; name?: string; description?: string; date_created?: string; date_modified?: string; custom_url?: { url?: string; }; type?: 'physical' | 'digital'; weight?: number; price?: number; primary_image?: IBigCommerceProductImage; images?: IBigCommerceProductImage[]; } interface IBigCommerceProductImage { image_file?: string; is_thumbnail?: boolean; product_id?: number; id?: number; description?: string; sort_order?: number; date_modified?: string; url_standard?: string; url_thumbnail?: string; url_tiny?: string; }

在 Wix 中，它是：

 interface IWixProduct { id: string; name: string; slug: string; visible: boolean; weight: number; description: string; sku: string; productType: 'physical'; priceData?: { currency?: string; price: number; }; media?: IWixProductMedia; lastUpdated?: string; }

没有标准化，每个平台看到的东西都不一样，这没关系，除非你想构建一个支持多个平台的应用程序。

最后——货币化。

如果您正在为自己或为您工作的公司构建应用程序，您的最终目标显然是从中获得收入。 然而，虽然 Shopify 和 Wix 等一些平台为您提供了一些工具，可以在不同的商业模式中通过您的应用获利，但所有其他平台都没有。 这意味着，除了集成支付解决方案所需的一切之外，您还需要自己开发这些集成。

我们非常了解这些问题，因为在过去的 10 年中，我们为网站建设和电子商务平台开发了十几个应用程序。

在这些年里，我们开发了很多工具，帮助我们将电子商务平台应用程序的开发时间减少了 80%，这促使我们建立了 Common Ninja 并将这些工具提供给那里的任何开发者🎉

我们的主要产品是“一个 API 来统治它们”。

无论您的用户/商家使用什么平台，您都将使用单一 API 来消费电子商务资源，例如产品、订单、客户等。 你总是知道你得到了什么，如果一个电子商务平台正在改变他们的 API，我们会在幕后处理它，所以你不必这样做。

此外，使用 Common Ninja，您将能够完美地通过您的应用获利，创建基于订阅的计划，并将您的应用连接到流行的支付提供商，如 Stripe 和 PayPal。

难以置信？ 大概。 但这就是我们，这就是我们所做的🙂

想了解更多？ 到这里：
如果您有任何反馈、问题或意见，我们在这里。



原文:

Let's start with the bottom line:

Building apps for e-commerce platforms is hard.

Background

E-Commerce platforms (such as Shopify, BigCommerce, Wix, WooCommerce, etc.) are great for people who want to build and manage shops online. They provide the best tools for shop owners to manage inventory, customize their storefront, and start making revenues from online orders.

But at the end, these platforms are limited, and this is why they allow developers to leverage their APIs and data to develop apps on top them.

These apps are being used by merchants and their customers and they are quite varied. From storefront widgets that increase conversions and enhance the user experience, to apps that collects data to improve inventory management, to social proof apps, and easy drop shipping.

E-Commerce apps are widely used by online merchants, and become very popular. How popular? Shopify says that in 2020 the average merchant installed 6 apps on his store, and that developer partners earned $233M in 2020 alone.

The thing is...

If you ask any developer who had an experience with building apps for e-commerce platforms "How was your experience?" you'll get the same answer: "It was a hell of a nightmare".

The Problems With E-Commerce Platforms For Developers

When you first start building an app you're entering a world of pain.

It starts with the documentation. In order to build an app you'll need to read. A lot. And while some platforms did a nice job with onboarding developers to their platform and documenting everything, others don't.

Once you find your way in the endless docs, and understand the different types of apps each platform allows you to build, the next thing will be to handle authentication.

Surprisingly, although most of the platforms are using OAuth, each one of them implemented the authentication flow differently so you'll need to learn exactly how it works on each platform separately while using tunnels (and in some cases to debug the code in production because you have to contact the platforms in order to change the auth endpoints).

I hope you're still here because there's more!

The next thing would be APIs. Let's take the Product resource as an example.

In Shopify a product object looks like that:

interface IShopifyProduct {
  id?: string;
  title?: string;
  body_html?: string;
  vendor?: string;
  product_type?: string;
  created_at?: string;
  updated_at?: string;
  handle?: string;
  variants?: {
    id?: string;
    product_id?: string;
    price?: string; // no unit supported in api
    weight?: number;
    weight_unit?: string;
  }[]
  images?: IShopifyProductImage[];
  status?: 'active' | 'archived' | 'draft';
  tags?: string;
}

interface IShopifyProductImage {
  product_id?: number;
  id?: number;
  title?: string;
  position?: number;
  created_at?: string;
  updated_at?: string;
  alt?: string;
  weight?: number;
  weight_unit?: 'kg' | 'lb';
  price?: number | string;
  src?: string;
  variant_ids?: string[];
  admin_graphql_api_id?: string;
}

And in BigCommerce like this:

interface IBigCommerceProduct {
  id?: number;
  name?: string;
  description?: string;
  date_created?: string;
  date_modified?: string;
  custom_url?: {
    url?: string;
  };
  type?: 'physical' | 'digital';
  weight?: number;
  price?: number;
  primary_image?: IBigCommerceProductImage;
  images?: IBigCommerceProductImage[];
}

interface IBigCommerceProductImage {
  image_file?: string;
  is_thumbnail?: boolean;
  product_id?: number;
  id?: number;
  description?: string;
  sort_order?: number;
  date_modified?: string;
  url_standard?: string;
  url_thumbnail?: string;
  url_tiny?: string;
}

While in Wix it's:

interface IWixProduct {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  slug: string;
  visible: boolean;
  weight: number;
  description: string;
  sku: string;
  productType: 'physical';
  priceData?: {
    currency?: string;
    price: number;
  };
  media?: IWixProductMedia;
  lastUpdated?: string;
}

There's no standardization, each platform sees things differently which is ok, unless you want to build an app that supports multiple platforms.

And finally - monetization.

If you're building an app, either for yourself or for a company you're working for, your end goal is obviously to see revenues out of it. However, while some platforms like Shopify and Wix are giving you some tools to monetize your app in different business models, all of the other platforms don't. Which means, that in addition to everything else you'll need to integrate payment solutions as well, and develop these integrations yourself.

Common Ninja: A New Era Of E-Commerce Apps Development

We know these problems very closely, since in the past 10 years we developed dozen of apps for website building and e-commerce platforms.

During these years we developed a lot of tools that helped us to decrease the development time of e-commerce platforms apps in 80%, which led us to establish Common Ninja and offer these tools to any developer out there 🎉

Common Ninja is a platform for building & monetizing e-commerce apps.

Our main offering is "One API to rule them all".

You'll work with a single API for consuming e-commerce resources such as products, orders, customers, etc. regardless the platform your user/merchant is using. You always know what you get, and if an e-commerce platform is changing their API, we take care of it behind the scenes so you won't have to.

In addition, with Common Ninja you'll be able to monetize your app flawlessly, create subscriptions based plans, and connect your app to popular payment providers like Stripe and PayPal.

Too good to be true? Probably. But this is us, and that's what we do 🙂

Want to learn more? go here:
https://www.commoninja.com/developers

If you have any feedback, question or comment, we're here.
Items.Author: dsternlicht

Items.Title: 每个开发人员都应该知道的顶级 React 组件库和框架 | Top React Component Libraries and Frameworks Every Developer Should Know
Items.Description:
它易于使用、灵活且功能强大，您可以使用它构建从简单应用程序到复杂 Web 应用程序的任何东西！ 在本文中，我们正在研究一些 UI 组件框架，它们通过提供按钮或导航栏等现成的组件来简化您的下一个项目的开发——在快速开始时节省设计创建和编码时间。

材质界面

在GitHub 上有近 77k+ 的 star，MUI 的受欢迎程度相当明显。 这意味着他们会处理样式和文档，以使它们看起来很完美； 它是许多人每天喜爱和使用的一款！

有无数的免费资源可以帮助您入门。 登陆页面模板、博客设计模型和投资组合样式都可用。 如果你找不到你需要的东西，可能有人已经创建了它。

特征

  • 即用型、永久免费且可访问的组件列表。
  • 用于复杂用例的先进且强大的组件。
  • 完全构建的、开箱即用的应用程序模板、登录页面等。

蚂蚁设计

它是GitHub 上最受欢迎的组件库，拥有超过 79k 颗星。

它有很棒的文档，包括示例和变体。 在详细定制现有组件和主题方面，它也是一个强大的平台。 此外，它还免费提供了一套开箱即用的高质量 React 组件。

与其他 React 库相比，Ant Design 的缺点之一是它具有较大的包大小。

特征

  • 专为 Web 应用程序设计的企业级 UI。
  • 用 TypeScript 编写，具有可预测的静态类型。
  • 国际化支持。
  • 每个细节的强大主题定制。

脉轮用户界面

它是一个简单、模块化且可访问的组件库，为您提供构建 React 应用程序所需的构建块。 在GitHub 上拥有超过 22.5k 颗星。

简单是描述 Chakra UI 时想到的术语之一。 顾名思义，它的库强调开发过程，而不仅仅是关注代码。 它具有出色的文档肯定会对此有所帮助。

特征

  • 通过传递 props 可以轻松地设置组件的样式。
  • 建立在 React UI Primitive 之上的灵活且可组合的 wicth。
  • 所有组件均可访问，并且遵循 WAI-ARIA 指南规范。
  • 大多数组件都兼容暗模式。

曼汀

在GitHub 上有超过 10,000 颗星。

它以其简单的结构和模块化从其他 UI 设计系统中脱颖而出。

特征

  • 核心组件非常广泛，并提供了诸如颜色选择器、日期范围选择器和时间线等利基组件。
  • 高级主题自定义和暗模式支持。
  • 一组使组件易于使用的钩子。
  • 与您的主题无缝集成的全功能通知系统。

故事书

在GitHub 上有超过 7 万颗星。

它通过隔离组件使开发更快、更容易。 这允许您一次处理一个组件。 您可以开发整个 UI，而无需启动复杂的开发堆栈、强制将某些数据输入数据库或在应用程序中导航。

特征

  • 用于独立构建 UI 的沙箱，因此您可以开发难以到达的状态和边缘情况。
  • 无需担心数据、API 或业务逻辑即可实现组件和页面。
  • 将用例保存为纯 JavaScript 中的故事，以便在开发、测试和 QA 期间重新访问。
  • 使用插件自定义您的工作流程、自动化测试并与您喜欢的工具集成。

细化

优化基于 React 的框架，用于快速构建内部工具。 它为快速开发提供了许多开箱即用的功能，而不会影响极端的可定制性。 用例包括但不限于管理面板、B2B 应用程序和仪表板。 尽管它最近刚刚发布，但在Github上有超过 2.1k 的星星。

此外，还支持开箱即用的 Ant Design。

它有许多帮助您入门的示例以及文档。 此外，它与 Next.js 的服务器端渲染完全兼容。

特征

  • 无头：适用于任何 UI 框架
  • 零配置：使用superplate的一条线设置。 启动一个项目只需不到一分钟。
  • 开箱即用：路由、网络、身份验证、状态管理、i18n 和 UI。
  • 后端不可知：连接到任何自定义后端。
  • 强大的 UI ：与集成的Ant Design无缝协作（它也可以与任何 UI 框架一起使用）。

👉 Showcase 👉展示



原文:

React is the most popular JavaScript library for building user interfaces. It's easy to use, flexible, and powerful enough that you can build anything from simple apps to complex web applications with it! In this article, we're looking at some UI component frameworks which make developing your next project easier by providing ready-to-go components like buttons or navigation bars - saving time in both design creation as well as coding when getting started quickly.

Material UI

MUI offers a wide range of high-quality components that have helped us ship features more quickly. With nearly 77k+ stars on GitHub, the popularity of MUI is rather clear. The Google design system has been integrated into an extended React library with great success.

MUI is a brand, not just an open-source project. This means that they take care of the style and the documentation so that it all looks perfect; it's one that many people love and use daily!

The best part of MUI is how many people use it. There are countless free resources to help you get started. Landing page templates, mockups of blog designs, and portfolio styles are all available. If you can't find what you need, someone has probably already created it.

Features

  • A list of components that ready-to-use, free forever, and has accessibility.
  • Advance and powerful components for complex use-cases.
  • Fully built, out-of-the-box, templates for apps, landing pages, and more.

👉 Showcase

Ant Design

Ant Design is a UI library and design system for enterprise applications. It is the most popular components library on GitHub with over 79k stars.

It has great documentation that includes examples and variants. It is also a robust platform when it comes to customizing the existing components and themes in detail. Furthermore, It has a set of high-quality React components out of the box for free.

One of the drawbacks of Ant Design is that it has a large bundle size, when compared to other React libraries.

Features

  • Enterprise-class UI designed for web applications.
  • Written in TypeScript with predictable static types.
  • Internationalization support.
  • Powerful theme customization in every detail.

👉 Showcase

Chakra UI

Chakra UI is one of the fastest-growing React UI libraries. It is a simple, modular and accessible component library that gives you the building blocks you need to build your React applications. With over 22.5k stars on GitHub.

Simplicity is one of the terms that come to mind when describing Chakra UI. Its library, as its name suggests, emphasizes the development process rather than just focusing on code. It has excellent documentation will certainly aid with that.

Features

  • Makes it easy to style your components by passing props.
  • Flexible and composable wicth built on top of React UI Primitive.
  • All of components are accessible and its follow the WAI-ARIA guidelines specifications.
  • Most components are dark mode compatible.

👉 Showcase

Mantine

Mantine is a full-featured React component library with 100+ customizable components and 30+ hooks that allows you to create fully functional and accessible web applications quickly. Mantine has a lot of packages and components that are ready-to-use and can be used in any project. With over 10k stars on GitHub.

It stands out from other UI design systems with its simple structure and modularity.

Features

  • The core components are extremely extensive and provides niche components like color picker, date-range picker, and timelines.
  • Advanced theme customization and dark mode support.
  • A set of hooks that make it easy to use the components.
  • A full-featured notification system that integrates seamlessly with your theme.

👉 Showcase

Storybook

Storybook is a JavaScript tool that allows developers to create organized UI systems making both the building process and documentation more efficient and easier to use. With over 70k stars on GitHub.

It makes development faster and easier by isolating components. This allows you to work on one component at a time. You can develop entire UIs without needing to start up a complex dev stack, force certain data into your database, or navigate around your application.

Features

  • A sandbox to build UIs in isolation so you can develop hard-to-reach states and edge cases.
  • Implement components and pages without needing to fuss with data, APIs, or business logic.
  • Save use cases as stories in plain JavaScript to revisit during development, testing, and QA.
  • Use addons to customize your workflow, automate testing, and integrate with your favorite tools.

👉 Showcase

Refine

Refine A React-based framework for building internal tools, rapidly. It provides lots of out-of-the box functionality for rapid development, without compromising extreme customizability. Use-cases include, but are not limited to admin panels, B2B applications and dashboards. With over 2.1k stars on Github even though it has been released recently.

Refine is a headless React framework, which means all out-of-the-box features(Routing, Networking, Authentication, Authorization, State Management, Realtime, i18n, etc.), it can be used without being tied to any UI elements or framework. Also, Ant Design as out-of-the-box is supported.

It has many examples to help you get started, as well as documentation. Also it is fully compatible with server side rendering with Next.js.

Features

  • Headless: Works with any UI framework
  • Zero-configuration: One-line setup with superplate. It takes less than a minute to start a project.
  • Out-of-the-box: Routing, networking, authentication, state management, i18n and UI.
  • Backend Agnostic: Connects to any custom backend.
  • Powerful UI: Works seamlessly with integrated Ant Design(It can also be used with any UI Framework).

👉 Showcase

Items.Title: 90% 的软件工程都在集成 janky API，我喜欢它 | 90% of software engineering is integrating janky APIs, and I love it
Items.Description:
我今天偶然发现了这个 twitter 线程和相应的 Hacker News 评论：今天完成的 90% 的软件工程都在集成文档记录不佳的 API | 黑客新闻。 这个镜头根本没有引起我的共鸣。 我实际上认为这种态度是有毒的。

没有人说：“90% 的桥梁建造都是用不同质量的水泥制作形状”。 众所周知，土木工程涉及使用哪种水泥、设计合理的建筑、计算结构应力、组织大量劳动力、制定项目计划。 这是艰难的，具有挑战性的和有益的。 水泥的质量总是很差，因为它不是 unobtainium 可以神奇地塑造成指定的桥梁。

软件工程和我们自己的数字水泥也是如此：API。 事实上，结合粗糙的 API 来解决问题是如此具有挑战性和成就感，以至于我无法想象做其他任何事情。

两种类型的软件工程

这些天来，我正在编写一个 CRUD 电子商务应用程序，使用 PHP、wordpress 和 mysql。 在过去的 20 年里，我从事的工作从裸机嵌入式系统到量子密码学以太网桥，再到搜索引擎和“非谷歌规模”的数据工程。 这告诉我有两种截然不同的软件工程。

软件工程作为复制字节

一种是我所说的“复制字节”。 你得到输入字节，以某种方式与业务相关。 作为软件工程师，您的工作是将这些输入字节复制到指定的目的地。

例如，您收到客户的订单（行项目 + 送货地址），您必须将这些行项目转发给履行部门，并在此过程中记录一些日志条目。 您可能会在此过程中计算一些税或应用优惠券，稍微修改这些字节，但大致而言，您只是按照上帝设计的方式发送它们。

软件工程作为“发明”字节

另一种软件工程是我所说的“发明字节”，或“以非常复杂的方式转换字节”。 这是时髦算法、数据科学、数学和复杂事物的领域。 例如：获取输入的 3D 模型并输出一些 CAM 指令或在复杂的索引结构中搜索字符串。 通常，艰苦的思考是由大学或研究实验室的人完成的。

编写代码通常是工作中最微小的部分。 人们可能想知道这些“字节发明者”到底是不是软件工程师。 或许我们应该称他们为“3D模型转换工程师”，或者“搜索索引数据结构工程师”。 通常，他们的代码需要由知道如何缩进文件的人重写（或者说，将其从 hacky matlab 移植到 java）。

发明字节的 API 很奇怪。 坦率地说，我并没有真正涉足发明的字节，所以我不能对这些 API 的文档说太多。

您实际上可能会在整个职业生涯中复制字节

如果您的业务不是思考聪明的想法或制作电子游戏等漂亮的东西，而是从事赚钱的业务，那么您可能属于软件工程的“复制字节”类别。

要复制哪些字节，何时，何地，沿途添加哪些日志条目，如何扩展复制，如何确保复制的字节与其他复制的字节在事务上对齐，这些东西很复杂。 但它一直是乌龟，一个用于复制字节的 API 建立在另一个之上。 最终，您遇到了硬件，而男孩是用于复制字节的硬件 API，这完全是胡说八道。

您无需编写有趣的代码即可成为工程师

你可能认为这很愚蠢：你学会了如何平衡树，如何安排流程，以及如何做一些微分曲线几何； 如何编写编译器，如何设计数据库引擎，如何实现分布式共识； 你为什么不把那些漂亮的算法写成你日常工作的一部分呢？ 事情是，聪明的人已经这样做了，而且他们经常利用它做生意。 现在你可以为他们聪明的想法和勤奋的工作付钱，希望他们确实比你更聪明，然后再调用 API 来复制刚刚计算的任何字节。

人们出于各种不同的原因，以不同的方式，在不同的约束条件下想要传输数据。 这意味着即使是最好的书面 API 文档也必须具有足够的通用性，以涵盖其构建的用例，并且有一定的机会涵盖人们使用它的目的。 它很少完全适合您的用例。 因此，文档将不完美，并且可能根本无法涵盖您要查找的内容。

实际上，我认为 API 文档比以往任何时候都好很多。 我最近必须处理的所有 API 都带有 15 种语言的 copypasta、一个快速的可搜索索引、大量的教程、文章和视频，以及一个活跃的 github。 这比必须猜测放置在 PC-NFSD 假脱机上的文件需要多少空白填充才能让 COBOL 解析器满意要好得多。

通过结合糟糕的 API 解决问题是我做的最有成就感的事情

但这就是为什么我发现我的工作使用低于标准的 API 复制字节非常令人满意：

复制字节并不容易，尤其是在当今的分布式世界中。 弄清楚如何可靠、安全和大规模地做到这一点需要很多思考。 弄清楚将哪些数据传输给需要业务理解、人员知识和系统思维的人。 构建和维护解决方案需要产品和项目规划、管理团队、处理不断变化的需求。 它需要保持代码库的健康，指导其他工程师，与利益相关者保持一致，处理遗留代码。

我最终使用的 API 是我遇到的最少的问题。 有些很好，有些很糟糕，有些确实有问题，但它们与我工作的“工程”部分没有太大关系。 工程部分是关于用方框和箭头绘制图片。 方框是其他人的系统，箭头是其他人的 API。 我画了哪些箭头，它们指向哪里，沿着它们的小气泡包含什么，以及我选择使用哪些盒子，这就是我作为一名工程师所做的事情。

一个具体的例子：检查订单

作为一个难题的具体示例，通过将劣质 API 粘合在一起完全实现：在电子商务商店上完成订单。

  • 使用浏览器中的流畅 UX 向客户无缝显示支付过程和结帐状态（API：一些伪劣的 REST、一些 janky web socket 或其他、一些被黑客入侵的反应和一些可疑的 CSS。最终只是将状态代码从支付处理器铲到用户的浏览器，可能会添加一些花括号并转义一两个字符串。）
  • 记录事务并向业务和操作提供指标和分析（一些无意义的像素数据，一些腐烂的跟踪 JS，一些伪劣的 SQL，也许一些 janky JSON 进入 SQS 队列）
  • 处理付款信息、订单信息并将其显示在客户帐户部分/后端 CRM/fedex 中（我什至不打算在这里列出 API 的低劣程度）
  • 将送货地址转换为带有条形码的正确送货标签（与斑马和 ZPL 一起玩）
  • 将行项目分派到所需的履行中心，向仓库工作人员提供拣货信息，包括跟踪、指标、库存处理……

设计这样的东西，使其正确、高效地工作，具有可见性、可靠性、可扩展性，并满足业务需求，并为每个相关人员带来欢乐——现在这就是实际的工程。

你认为将 API 粘合在一起是工程吗？

你认为今天的软件工程很无聊吗？ 你讨厌将 API 粘合在一起吗？ 你认为什么构成了“真正的”软件工程？ 软件工程师理想的日常工作是什么？ 请务必在下方发表评论！



原文:

(Title photo: Ophelia photos - Creative Commons)

I stumbled upon this twitter thread and corresponding Hacker News comments today: 90% of software engineering done today is integrating poorly documented APIs | Hacker News. This take doesn’t resonate with me at all. I actually think this attitude is toxic.

Nobody says: "90% of building a bridge is making shapes out of cement of varying quality". It is well understood that civil engineering is about which cement to use, designing a sound architecture, computing structural stresses, organizing a large workforce, establishing a project plan. It is hard, challenging and rewarding. Cement will always be of poor quality, because it is not unobtainium that can magically be molded into the specified bridge.

The same goes for software engineering and our own digital cement: APIs. In fact, combining cruddy APIs to solve problems is so challenging and fulfilling that I can’t imagine doing anything else.

Two types of software engineering

These days, I am in the business of writing a CRUD e-commerce application, using PHP, wordpress and mysql of all things. In the past 20 years I've worked on anything from bare-metal embedded systems to quantum cryptography ethernet bridges to search engines and "not google-scale" data engineering. This has taught me that there are two very separate kinds of software engineering.

Software engineering as copying bytes

One is what I call "copying bytes". You get input bytes, relevant to the business in some way. As a software engineer, your job is copy these input bytes to their specified destinations.

For example, you receive an order from the customer (line items + shipping address), and you have to forward these line items to the fulfillment department, recording a few log entries along the way. You might calculate some tax or apply a coupon along the way, slightly modifying those bytes, but roughly, you are just sending them along the way god designed them.

Software engineering as “inventing” bytes

The other kind of software engineering is what I call "inventing bytes", or "transforming bytes in really complicated ways". This is the realm of funky algorithms, data science, mathematics, complicated stuff. For example: taking an input 3D model and outputting some CAM instructions or searching for a string in complicated index structures. Often, the hard thinking is done by people at universities or research labs.

Writing code is usually the tiniest part of the undertaking. One could wonder if these “byte inventors” are software engineers at all. Maybe we should call them "3D model transformation engineers", or "search index data structure engineers". Often, their code needs to be rewritten by someone who knows how to indent files (or say, port it from hacky matlab to java).

The APIs for inventing bytes are weird. Frankly, I don't really dabble in invented bytes, so I can't say too much as to the documentation of these APIs.

You might actually copy bytes your entire career

If you are not in the business of thinking clever ideas or making pretty things like video games, but in the business of making money, you probably fall into the "copying bytes" category of software engineering.

Which bytes to copy, when, where, what log entries to add along the way, how to scale the copying, how to ensure the copied bytes are transactionally aligned with other copied bytes, that stuff is complicated. But it's turtles all the way down, one API to copy bytes building upon another. Ultimately you hit the hardware, and boy are the hardware APIs to copy bytes utterly nonsense.

You don’t need to write interesting code to be an engineer

You might think it's inane: you learned how to balance trees, how to schedule processes, and how to do some differential curve geometry; how to write compilers, how design database engines and how to implement distributed consensus; why don't you get to write all those beautiful algorithms as part of your day job? Thing is, clever people have already done so, and often they made a business out of it. Now you can pay them for their clever thinking and their diligent work, hoping that they were indeed more clever than you, and go back to calling APIs to copy whatever bytes have just been computed.

People want to transfer data for all kind of different reasons, in different ways, with different constraints. That means that even the best written API documentation will have to be generic enough to cover the use case it was built for, and with some chance, what people are using it for. It will rarely exactly fit your use case. The documentation will thus not be perfect, and might not cover what you are looking for at all.

I actually think API documentation at large is miles better than it ever was. All the APIs I had to deal with lately come with copypasta for 15 languages, a snappy searchable index, a plethora of tutorials and articles and videos, an active github. This is miles better than having to guess how much whitespace padding a file placed on a PC-NFSD spool needs for the COBOL parser to be happy.

Solving problems by combining crappy APIs is the most fulfilling thing I do

But here's why I find my job copying bytes with subpar APIs singularly fulfilling:

Copying bytes is not easy, especially in today's distributed world. Figuring out how to do it reliably, securely and at scale requires a lot of thought. Figuring out which data to transfer to whom needs business understanding, knowledge of people and systems thinking. Building and maintaining a solution requires product and project planning, managing teams, dealing with changing requirements. It requires keeping a codebase healthy, mentoring other engineers, aligning with stakeholders, dealing with legacy code.

The APIs I ultimately use are the least of my problems. Some are nice, some are terrible, some are really problematic, but they don't have much to do with the "engineering" part of my job. The engineering part is all about drawing pictures with boxes and arrows. The boxes are other people's systems, the arrows are other people's APIs. What arrows I draw and where they point to and what the little bubbles along them contain, and which boxes I chose to use, that's what I do as an engineer.

A concrete example: checking out an order

As a concrete example of a hard problem, fully implemented by glueing together shoddy APIs: fulfilling an order on an e-commerce store.

  • seamlessly show payment process and checkout status to the customer using a slick UX in their browser (APIs: some shoddy REST, some janky web socket or whatever, some hacked react and some dubious CSS. Ultimately just shoveling the status code from the payment processor to the user's browser, potentially adding some curly braces and escaping a string or two.)
  • log the transaction and provide metrics and analytics to business and ops (some inane pixel data, some putrid tracking JS, some shoddy SQL, maybe some janky JSON going to a SQS queue)
  • process the payment information, order information and display it in the customers account section / backend CRM / fedex (I'm not even going to list how subpar the APIs are here)
  • transform the shipping address into a proper shipping label with barcode (have fun with zebra and ZPL)
  • dispatches the line items to the required fulfillment centers, provides picking information to the warehouse workers, with tracking, metrics, inventory handling, ...

Designing something like this so that it works correctly, efficiently, with visibility, reliability, scalability, and fulfills business needs, and sparks joy for every human involved – now that's actual engineering.

Do you think glueing together APIs is engineering?

Do you think software engineering today is boring? Do you hate glueing APIs together? What do you think constitutes "real" software engineering? What is the ideal day-to-day of a software engineer? Please be sure to comment below!
Items.Author: wesen

Items.Title: ⚛️ React Intersection Observer 👀 Hook 🪝。 | ⚛️ React Intersection Observer 👀 Hook 🪝.
Items.Description:
亚历克斯纸

发表于正如MDN所说， Intersection Observer API提供了一种异步观察的方法
目标元素与祖先元素或顶级文档的交集的变化。

看起来很复杂，但是在 React 中你可以使用一个简单的钩子来实现交集
唯一要记住的是，您需要使用 useRef() 将“目标元素”传递给观察者

 const targetMiddle = React.useRef(); const targetTop = React.useRef();
 <h3 ref={targetTop}>TOP</h3> <h3 ref={targetMiddle}>Middle</h3>

钩

钩子非常简单，您需要导出一个接受目标元素作为参数的函数

默认情况下，阈值设置为 0，但您可以根据需要使用选项

使用 _isIntersecting _entry 属性设置状态
export function useObserver(ref) { const [isIntersecting, setIsIntersecting] = React.useState(false); // OPTIONS const options = { // root: target.current, rootMargin: '0px', threshold: 0.5, // A threshold of 1.0 means that when 100% of the target is visible within the element specified by the root option, the callback is invoked. }; // Observer const observer = new IntersectionObserver(([entry]) => setIsIntersecting(entry.isIntersecting),options); // Use Effect React.useEffect(() => { observer.observe(ref.current) // DISCONNECT return () => observer.disconnect() }, []); // Return return isIntersecting };

在 useEffect 中，您可以调用 _disconnect _ 方法来阻止它观察目标元素并最终返回可以为真或假的状态，并且基于此值您可以更改 vDom，例如通过显示或隐藏视频

当目标元素可见时，极限就是你的想象力！

在这个视频指南中你可以看到一个超级简单的实现，很好的观察👀 ...

视频指南
悉达多文卡特什—— Apr 29 4月29日

IT-Recruiter-USA - IT-Recruiter-USA - Apr 21 4月21日



原文:

alexpaper

Posted on May 2

As MDN says the Intersection Observer API provides a way to asynchronously observe
changes in the intersection of a target element with an ancestor element or with a top-level document's.

It seems complicated but in React you can achieve intersection using a simple hook
the only thing to keep in mind is that you need to pass a 'target element' to the observer using useRef()

const targetMiddle = React.useRef();
const targetTop = React.useRef();


<h3 ref={targetTop}>TOP</h3>
<h3 ref={targetMiddle}>Middle</h3>

Hook

The hook is very simple you need to export a function that accept the target element as argument

By default the threshold is set to 0 but you can play around with the options based on your needs

set the state using _isIntersecting _entry property 
export function useObserver(ref) {

    const [isIntersecting, setIsIntersecting] = React.useState(false);

     // OPTIONS
     const options = {
        // root: target.current,
        rootMargin: '0px',
        threshold: 0.5, // A threshold of 1.0 means that when 100% of the target is visible within the element specified by the root option, the callback is invoked.
    };
    // Observer
    const observer = new IntersectionObserver(([entry]) => setIsIntersecting(entry.isIntersecting),options);

    // Use Effect
    React.useEffect(() => {

        observer.observe(ref.current)
        // DISCONNECT 
        return () => observer.disconnect()

    }, []);
    // Return
    return isIntersecting
  };

inside useEffect you can call the _disconnect _ method to stop it from observing the target element and finally return the state that can be true or false, and based on this value you can change the vDom, for example by showing or hiding a video

when the target element is visible, the limit is your imagination!

In this video guide you can see a super simple implementation, good observation 👀 ...

Video Guide
🆗 👋

Siddharth Venkatesh - Apr 29

IT-Recruiter-USA - Apr 21
Items.Author: alexpaper

Items.Title: 盖茨比中的代码拆分 | Code splitting in Gatsby
Items.Description:
凯齐奥斯的阿德里安·盖里

发表于这篇文章对于 Gatsby 的大型项目特别有趣：+1,000 页。
此外，如果您的项目使用 React18 或 +，请改用此方法： https ://youtu.be/lypEGNEIRKE

这对于 SEO 和 UX 来说非常重要。

随着 Gatsby 静态页面的生成，TTFB（到第一个字节的时间）和 FCP（第一个内容绘制）与在 react pure 中开发的 CSR 站点（客户端渲染）相比非常减少。 并且随着图片加载的优化，通过 Gatsby Image，LCP 也在那里进行了优化。

而这一切几乎都是用 Gatsby 自动完成的！ 极好的 ！ 没有什么可做的吗？ 不是真的😅这太容易了。

当您的项目增长时，您可能会在站点的某些页面中加载过多的 JS 包。 如果您使用 PageSpeed Insights ( pagespeed.web.dev ) 执行测试，您可能会得到以下建议/机会来优化您的页面：

当您加载执行测试的页面未使用的 javascript 代码时，就会出现这种优化机会。

有两个主要原因：

  • 错误的代码拆分
  • 第三方脚本：GTM、Facebook 像素等。

在本文中，我们将看到如何解决此问题的主要原因，即错误的代码拆分。 我将在第二篇文章中解释通过第三方脚本修复未使用的 javascript 的解决方案。

最初，Gatsby 将您的 JS 捆绑包分为 3 类：

  • 应用程序级块（与所有页面共享），
  • 模板级块（与同一模板的所有页面共享），
  • 页面级数据导入

让我们以一个新闻网站为例，该网站为每个新闻项目生成文章，有些还有视频。

我们的网站有几个页面模板：

  • 主页模板
  • 新闻页面模板（根据新闻可能包含视频）
  • 新闻类别页面模板（按类别列出新闻）

我们将专注于网站上最重要的新闻页面。 我们新闻页面的特殊性在于它们可能包含视频。
所以我们需要一个能够很好地管理这些视频的库。 让我们以Video.js为例。 这个库非常完美，它允许我们播放多种格式的视频（MP4、Webm、HLS、DASH 等），还可以轻松自定义我们的视频播放器。

这个库的问题在于它很重，573.6kb MINIFIED 和 159.1 kB MINIFIED + GZIPPED。

根据bundlephobia的说法，在慢速 3G 中加载最多可能需要 3.18 秒。 这会严重破坏我们网站的体验，该网站旨在高效和快速加载（这就是我们决定与 Gatsby 一起开发它的原因）。

现在我们将检查没有视频的文章包，看看它们是否也导入了 Video.js。

如何分析 Gatsby 中每个页面的 bundle？

感谢使用此命令安装的gatsby-plugin-perf-budgets和gatsby-plugin-webpack-bundle-analysis-v2插件：

 npm i gatsby-plugin-webpack-bundle-analyser-v2 -D npm i gatsby-plugin-perf-budgets -D

我们可以看到我们的视频库 ( Video.js ) 已加载到所有文章页面中，即使它们没有视频。

对没有视频的页面文章进行捆绑分析：

我们导入 457 kb 的 JS ：

我简化了分析的渲染以使其更具可读性（是的，我是 Photoshop 大师）

因此，我们将通过拆分VideoPlayer组件的导入来执行我们所谓的“代码拆分”，该组件本身会导入并使用我们的Video.js库。

如何使用 Gatsby 进行代码拆分？

在通过分析页面包确定我们应该对什么进行代码拆分后，我们将能够对包含我们只想在包含视频的页面上导入的包的组件进行代码拆分： VideoPlayer组件。

要使用 Gatsby 进行代码拆分，我们需要安装几个包：

  • @loadable/babel-plugin
  • @loadable/component
  • @loadable/webpack-plugin
  • gatsby-plugin-loadable-components-ssr

并将我们刚刚安装的gatsby-plugin-loadable-components-ssr插件添加到gatsby-config.js中的插件列表中。

然后我们只需要使用@loadable/component来导入我们的 VideoPlayer 组件：

 import loadable from " @loadable/component " const VideoPlayer = loadable ( () => import ( " ~/components/VideoPlayer/VideoPlayer " ) )

然后确保仅在文章有视频时有条件地使用VideoPlayer组件：

 { article . video && ( < VideoPlayer src = { article . video . url } title = { article . video . intro } /> ) }

因此，现在我们没有视频的页面不再包含Video.js库：

现在我们导入 355kb 的 JS 文件，我们节省了 2 个请求！

瞧，您刚刚看到了如何使用 Gatbsy 创建代码拆分。

但是您应该知道，使用 React 18 和React.lazy() ，现在可以本地创建拆分代码！ 没有gatsby-plugin-loadable-components-ssr插件！ 所以迁移到 React 18 也是一个很好的解决方案。

如果您有兴趣，我在这里列出了一些非常有趣的资源，以加深我们刚刚共同看到的内容：



原文:

Adrian Guery for Kezios

Posted on May 2

This article will be especially interesting for large Gatsby projects: +1,000 pages.
Also if your project uses React18 or +, use this method instead : https://youtu.be/lypEGNEIRKE

Gatsby is a very powerful meta framework for developing very fast loading sites. Which is very important for SEO and for UX.

With the generation of static pages of Gatsby, the TTFB (time to first byte) and the FCP (first contentful paint) are very reduced compared to a CSR site (client side rendering) as for example developed in react pure. And with the optimization of picture loading, via Gatsby Image, the LCP is also optimized there.

And all this is done almost automatically with Gatsby! Incredible ! Nothing more to do? Not really 😅 It would have been too easy.

When your project grows, you risk loading too many JS bundles in some pages of your site. If you perform a test using PageSpeed Insights (pagespeed.web.dev) you may get this advice / opportunity to optimize your page :

This optimization opportunity occurs when you load javascript code that is not used by the page on which you are performing the test.

There are two main causes:

  • bad code splitting
  • third party scripts: GTM, Facebook pixels, etc.

In this article we will see how to fix the main cause of this problem, bad code splitting. I will explain the solution to fix unused javascript by third party scripts cause in a second article.

How does Gatsby split your code natively?

Initially Gatsby organize your JS bundles into 3 categories:

  • application-level chunks (shared with all pages),
  • template-level chunks (shared with all pages of the same template),
  • page-level data imports

Let's take the example of a news site that produces articles for each news item and some have videos.

Our site has several page templates:

  • Home page template
  • News page template (potentially with videos depending on the news)
  • News categories page template (which lists news according to categories)

We will focus on the news pages, which are the most important on the site. The specificity of our news pages is that they potentially have videos.
So we need a library allowing a good management of these videos. Let's take Video.js for example. This library is perfect, it allows us to play videos in many formats (MP4, Webm, HLS, DASH, etc.) and also to easily customize our video player.

The problem with this library is that it’s heavy, 573.6kb MINIFIED, and 159.1 kB MINIFIED + GZIPPED.

According to bundlephobia, it can take up to 3.18 seconds to load in slow 3G. This can seriously spoil the experience of our site, which aims to be efficient and quick to load (which is why we decided to develop it with Gatsby).

Now we will check the bundles of our articles without videos, to see if they have also the Video.js imported.

How to analyze the bundles of each pages in Gatsby ?

Thanks to the gatsby-plugin-perf-budgets and gatsby-plugin-webpack-bundle-analyse-v2 plugins which are installed using this command:

npm i gatsby-plugin-webpack-bundle-analyser-v2 -D
npm i gatsby-plugin-perf-budgets -D

We can see that our video library (Video.js) is loaded in all article pages, even if they don't have videos.

Bundle analyze of a page article without video :

And we import 457 kb of JS :

I simplified the rendering of the analysis to make it more readable (Yes I'm a photoshop master)

So, we will do what we call “code splitting” by splitting the import of our VideoPlayer component which itself imports and uses our Video.js library.

How to do code splitting with Gatsby?

After having determined what we should code split by analyzing the bundles of the pages, we will be able to code split the components containing the packages that we want to import only on page with videos : The VideoPlayer component.

To do code splitting with Gatsby we will need to install several packages:

  • @loadable/babel-plugin
  • @loadable/component
  • @loadable/webpack-plugin
  • gatsby-plugin-loadable-components-ssr

And add the gatsby-plugin-loadable-components-ssr plugin that we just installed to our plugin list in the gatsby-config.js.

Then we just have to use @loadable/component to import our VideoPlayer component:

import loadable from "@loadable/component"
const VideoPlayer = loadable( () => import("~/components/VideoPlayer/VideoPlayer")
)

And then make sure to conditionally use the VideoPlayer component only if the article has a video:

{ article.video && ( <VideoPlayer src={article.video.url} title={article.video.intro} /> )
}

And so, now our pages without videos no longer have the Video.js library in their bundles:

(Always the same photoshop talents)
Now we import 355kb of JS files, and we economize 2 requests!

Voilà, you have just seen how to create code splitting with Gatbsy.

But you should know that with React 18 and React.lazy(), it is now possible to create splitting code natively! Without the gatsby-plugin-loadable-components-ssr plugin ! So moving to React 18 can also be a good solution.

I put here a list of very interesting sources to deepen what we have just seen together if you are interested:

Items.Title: 使用智能测试自动化简化 Oracle EBS 测试 | Streamlining Oracle EBS Testing with Smart Test Automation
Items.Description:
普雷娜·巴特

发表于此外，保持 EBS 应用程序的质量非常重要，因为企业的关键功能取决于它。 因此，不能破坏 EBS 的持续测试。

但是，在实施期间或在频繁的应用程序更新期间测试 EBS 应用程序可能是一项具有挑战性的任务，尤其是在Oracle EBS 测试不是自动化的情况下。 手动测试不能被视为可行的解决方案，因为您根本无法跟上频繁的 Oracle EBS 更新。 此外，EBS 的测试需要测试对您的业务至关重要的多个复杂应用程序，例如 ERP、HCM、SCM、CRM 等。 内部业务用户或专门的测试人员需要点击每个关键业务流程并记录结果。 因此，测试可能需要数周甚至数月才能完成。

每当推出更新时，这些集成或定制可能会中断，从而产生测试需求。 然而，测试关键集成和定制更难，而且通常会耗费大量时间。 所有这些都会导致应用关键补丁和升级的时间很长。

另请参阅如何为您的模型获得完美的背景

通过引入 Oracle EBS 测试自动化，企业将能够应对这些挑战。 测试自动化将使他们能够测试整个端到端业务流程，这些流程通常超出 Oracle EBS，包括多个平台、第三方应用程序、大型机、设备类型和 API。 测试自动化也将完美地解决过度测试和测试不足的问题。

有时，QA 团队在不知道更新对 Oracle 应用程序的影响的情况下继续测试所有业务流程，从而导致时间和金钱的浪费。 有时，测试是在“猜测”的基础上进行的，关键点未经测试，使您的业务面临不必要的风险。 借助 Oracle 测试自动化，QA 团队可以执行基于风险的测试，从而节省大量时间和金钱，同时避免业务风险。

我们可以使用 Selenium 来测试 Oracle EBS 的自动化吗？
毫无疑问，Selenium 是最流行的测试自动化工具之一。 但是，对于 Oracle EBS，由于某些限制，它不能被视为可行的解决方案。 由于 Selenium 无法在屏幕上定位 EBS 元素，因此它不能用作 Oracle EBS 测试工具。

除此之外，每当由于更新（即新屏幕、按钮或用户流）而直接对 Oracle 应用程序进行更改时，Selenium 测试脚本往往会中断，因为它们无法自动合并这些更改。 无法合并更改会导致测试失败、不稳定/脆弱的测试、构建失败等。

另请参阅您的公司是否对网络威胁免疫！ 我们应该使用哪个 Oracle EBS 测试自动化工具？

由于市场上有太多的测试自动化工具可用，要找到能够完美处理 Oracle EBS 用例的合适工具确实令人生畏。 自我修复：具有自我修复功能的 Oracle 测试工具解决了包括测试脚本维护在内的主要难题。 此类自动化工具会自动识别对象属性（名称、ID、Xpath、CSS 等）的变化，并自动修复测试自动化脚本，无需人工干预。 自我修复将节省测试人员的时间和精力，最终节省成本。

测试建议：测试一切将消耗大量时间和资源，而基于猜测的测试将使您的关键应用程序面临风险。 选择识别和推荐与更新相关的测试的自动化框架。 有了这个，QA 团队可以避免运行整个测试套件，从而节省大量宝贵的时间。

测试数据管理：为“采购到付款”和“订单到现金”等流程提供合格的测试数据可能是一项艰巨的任务。 选择可提供最佳测试数据覆盖率的 Oracle 测试自动化平台。 这可以加快测试执行周期并缩短上市时间。

最后的想法：拥有智能Oracle 测试平台将为企业提供他们一直在寻找的保持领先地位的敏捷性。 使用人工智能、机器学习和自然过程语言等智能技术优化测试不仅可以确保更快的测试执行周期，还可以确保足够的覆盖率。

标题：使用智能测试自动化简化 Oracle EBS 测试￼ 描述：在本博客中，我们将研究使用智能测试自动化简化 Oracle EBS 测试￼

Joao Carlos Sousa do Vale - Joao Carlos Sousa do Vale - May 2 5月2日



原文:

Prerna Bhatt

Posted on May 2

Oracle E-business Suite (EBS) is widely used across the globe to streamline critical business functions such as financials, budgeting, supply chain, human capital, etc. Enterprises that are looking to implement or patch Oracle E-Business Suite (EBS) need to ensure that their EBS systems continue to function as expected so that business continuity remains intact. Furthermore, it is really important to maintain the quality of EBS apps as the enterprise’s critical functions depend on it. So, continuous testing of EBS cannot be undermined.

However, testing EBS apps during implementation or during frequent application updates can be a challenging task, especially if Oracle EBS testing isn’t automated. Manual testing cannot be considered as a viable solution as you simply cannot keep pace with the frequent Oracle EBS updates. Furthermore, testing of EBS requires testing of multiple complex applications like ERP, HCM, SCM, CRM, etc that are critical for your business. Internal business users or dedicated testers need click through each critical business process and document the outcomes. So, testing can take weeks, or even months to get completed.

Often EBS is integrated with different third-party apps or has been customized to meet the unique needs of an enterprise. Whenever an update is rolled out, there are chances that these integrations or customizations might break, cropping up the need of testing. However, it is harder to test critical integrations and customizations and often consumes lot of time. All this result in long delays in applying critical patches and upgrades.

See also How to Get the Perfect Background for Your Mockup

Oracle EBS Test Automation – Stay Ahead of the Game

By bringing in Oracle EBS test automation, enterprises will stay on top of these challenges. Test automation will provide them the ability to test entire end-to-end business processes that often extend beyond Oracle EBS including multiple platforms, third-party apps, mainframes, device types and APIs. Test automation will also perfectly address the issues of over-testing and under testing.

At times, QA teams keep on testing all the business processes without knowing the impact of updates on Oracle apps, leading to wastage of time and money. At times, testing is performed on the basis of “guesses”, leaving critical points untested, exposing your business to unnecessary risks. With Oracle test automation, QA teams can perform risk-based testing, saving ample time and money, while keeping business risks at bay.

Can we use Selenium to test automate Oracle EBS?
There is no iota of doubt that Selenium is one of the most popular test automation tool. However, in case of Oracle EBS, it cannot be considered as a viable solution owing to certain limitations. Oracle EBS Apps are complex and contain dynamic elements that don’t have fixed ID, CSS, Name, or Class. Since Selenium cannot locate EBS elements on a screen, itcannot be used as an Oracle EBS testing tool.

Apart from this, whenever changes are made directly to Oracle appsdue to updates i.e. new screens, buttons, or user flows, Selenium test scripts tend to break as they fail to incorporate those changes automatically. The inability to incorporate changes leads to test failures, flaky/brittle tests, build failures, and more.

See also Are your firms immune to Cyberthreats !! Which Oracle EBS test Automation tool should we use?

With too many test automation tools available in the market, it is really daunting to find the right one that can perfectly handle Oracle EBS use-case. Givebelow are certain features that you should look for in Oracle EBS test automation platform.

Self-healing: Oracle testing tools that come with self-healing capabilities solve major pains including test script maintenance. Such automation tools automatically identify changes in the object property (Name, ID, Xpath, CSS etc.) and automatically fix the test automation scripts without requiring human intervention. Self-healing will save testers’ time and efforts, eventually leading to savings on costs.

Test Recommendations: Test everything will consume lot of time and resources while testing things based on guesses will expose your critical apps to risks. Opt for automation framework that identifies and recommends tests that are relevant to the updates. With this, QA teams can avoid running the entire test suite, saving lots of precious hours.

Test Data Management: Having qualiﬁed test data for processes such as Procure to Pay and Order to Cash can be a daunting task. Opt for Oracle test automation platform that provides optimal test data coverage. This leads to faster test execution cycles and improved time to market.

Final Thought: Having smart Oracle testing platform will provide enterprises the agility which they’ve been looking for to stay ahead of the game. Optimize testing with intelligent technologies like Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, and Natural Process Language will not only ensure faster test execution cycles but also adequate coverage.

Title: Streamlining Oracle EBS Testing with Smart Test Automation￼ Description: In this blog, we will study Streamlining Oracle EBS Testing with Smart Test Automation￼

Joao Carlos Sousa do Vale - May 2

Items.Title: 你可以用容器做什么？ | What can you do with containers?
Items.Description:
容器并不是什么新鲜事物——它们已经成为几年来的热门话题。 尽管有这些事实，但我们发现在我们的客户群和我们自己的研发团队中，容器的采用速度相当缓慢。 考虑到该技术比标准 VM 具有如此多的明显优势，这很奇怪。 原因可能在于所有技术人员大多坚持熟悉的做法。 许多人想利用容器技术，但它被理解为需要改变我们做基础设施的方式以及新的工具集、新的开发流程、新的监控流程等。突然间，门槛变得太高了，许多人们不知道从哪里开始。 感觉就像一个人必须先花一两个月才能弄清楚这项技术。

就像在生活中一样，我们可以在不知道每个细节的情况下使用某些东西。 许多人驾驶汽车，却不知道内燃机是如何工作的，也不知道变速箱究竟是如何为车轮提供牵引力的。 我们不在乎它，只要它足够可用——首先，它足以解锁、加油、换档和使用方向盘。 以类似的方式，本篇文章将提出一些小的实用示例，说明如何在已知环境中使用容器，以便每个人都能在熟悉技术的同时获得小胜利。

虚拟机与容器

每个人都使用虚拟机，并且容器通常被视为他们的下一代。 让我们简单检查一下多个类别的差异。

尺寸

图 1 显示了容器结构和 VM 结构之间的运行时差异。 注意每个虚拟机带来的开销——它有一个厚厚的操作系统层，它通过容器抽象以更优化的方式共享主机操作系统。 这样，容器在内存、硬盘分配和更快的启动时间方面的占用空间要小得多。 他们的表现也更好，更持久。

目的

与普通计算机一样，可以运行的应用程序数量没有限制或最佳实践。

容器单独使用宿主操作系统。 它小而快，但根据最佳实践，它只运行一个应用程序。 此外，这个应用程序只能是无头的，作为容器，与虚拟机相反，并且没有 UI 控制台来处理桌面应用程序。 持久性

虚拟机是普通计算机的抽象，因此，它们与运行在其中的进程编写的所有内容保持一致。 我们可以恢复到虚拟机的基于状态的快照，但重新启动时不会丢失任何数据。 与此相反，容器是不可变的。 在容器的进程空间中生成的每个数据都会在重启时丢失。 重新启动后需要保留的数据必须保留在映射到主机操作系统的卷中。

分享、感动、封装

虚拟机并非旨在在开发人员之间共享。 虽然发生这种情况，并且每个人都有一个名为“dev setup virtual machine revision 32”之类的项目 VM，但它始终是一个繁琐的过程，涉及通过硬盘驱动器、网络共享等进行复制。与此相反，容器被设计为共享. 共享容器的机制是通过容器注册表——这是一个服务器组件，类似于可以容纳许多类型和版本的容器的 VCS。 与源代码一样，这些容器可以被推送和拉取并使用版本标记，最终实现开发人员之间的共享或推广到测试或生产等环境。 与代码不同，并且与 VM 很相似，容器提供了一个完全打包的解决方案，其中包含所有依赖项——操作系统设置、环境变量、工具、库等。总而言之——容器提供了“它在我的机器上工作的问题”的解决方案” 但没有强加共享或推广大型虚拟机的问题。

轻松启动

那么，使用 webMethods 的容器有哪些速成方法呢？ 让我在 Software AG 技术社区的原始帖子中提出多种方案——有些很简单，有些更高级。

路线图和进一步阅读

很快，我们将使用定期更新的容器镜像启动我们自己的公共容器注册表。 您还应该监视我们的官方GitHub帐户，以获取有关 CI/CD 推广和容器编排的任何示例。

要更深入地了解如何使用 webMethods 部署容器解决方案，请查看有关容器和容器编排的更全面的博客文章。 以下网络研讨会也讨论了该主题。

本文是 TECHniques 时事通讯博客的一部分 - Software AG 社区的技术提示和技巧。 订阅以接收我们的季度更新或阅读最新一期。



原文:

Containers are not a new thing – they’ve been a hot topic for a couple of years. Docker, the company that became a synonym for the technology and now has a huge community worldwide, was founded 14 years ago. Despite those facts, we see a rather slow adoption of the containers both in our customer base and in our own R&D teams. This is rather strange considering the technology has so many clear advantages over the standard VMs. The reason may lie in the fact that all techies mostly stick to familiar practices. Many would like to leverage the container technology, but it is understood as something that requires a change in the way we do infrastructure along with a new toolset, new development processes, new monitoring processes, etc. Suddenly the bar gets too high, and many people don’t know where to start. It feels like one has to spend a month or two figuring out the technology first.

As often in life, we could use something without knowing each detail about it. Many drive a car and have no knowledge of how the combustion engine works or how exactly the gearbox gives traction to the wheels. We don’t care about it, as long it is usable enough – for one, it is enough to unlock, put gasoline, switch into gear and use the steering wheel. In a similar manner, this read will suggest small and practical examples of how to use the containers in a known context so that everybody can achieve small wins while getting familiar with the technology.

VMs vs containers

Everybody uses VMs and often the containers are seen as their next generation. Let’s briefly check the differences in multiple categories.

Size

_ Figure 1: Source: Docker.com_

Figure 1 shows the runtime differences between a container structure and a VM structure. Notice the overhead every VM brings – it has a thick layer of an operating system instead, and it shares the host operating systems via the container abstraction in a much more optimal way. By this, the containers have a much smaller footprint in terms of memory, hard disk allocation and much faster boot-up time. Their performance is also better and more persistent.

Purpose

VM is a general-purpose computer – you can run desktop and server applications in one. As in a normal computer, there is no limitation or best practice for the number of applications that one can run.

A container uses the host operation system in isolation. It is small and fast, but per best practice, it runs only a single application. Further, this app can only be headless, as the container, contrary to the VM, and does not have a UI console to work with desktop applications. Headless means that the application will be accessible only via the network interface – TCP endpoints, REST endpoints, web UI, web services, etc. The two latter facts explain why often the containers are used in the microservices architecture – they contain a single service type application.

Persistence

VMs are an abstraction of a normal computer and as such, they persist with everything that is written by the processes running inside it. We could revert back to a state-based snapshot of the virtual machine, but no data gets lost on restart. Contrary to this, the containers are immutable. Every data generated in the process space of the container gets lost on restart. The data that needs to survive a reboot has to persist in a volume mapped to the host operating system.

Sharing, moving, encapsulating

Virtual machines are not designed to be shared between developers. While this happens and everybody had a project VM called something like “dev setup virtual machine revision 32,” it is always a cumbersome process involving copying via hard drives, network shares, etc. In contrast to this, the containers are designed to be shared. The mechanism for sharing containers is through a container registry – this is a server component, similar to a VCS that can hold many types and versions of a container. Like source code, those containers can be pushed and pulled and tagged with a version, eventually enabling sharing between developers or promoting to an environment like test or production. Unlike code, and much like VMs, the containers provide a fully packaged solution that contains all dependencies – OS settings, environment variables, tools, libraries, etc. In summary – the containers are providing a solution of the “it worked on my machine problem” but without imposing the problem of sharing or promoting huge virtual machines.

Easy start

So, what are the quick wins for the containers with webMethods? Let me propose multiple scenarios in the original post on the Software AG Tech Community – some are easy, some are more advanced.

Road map and further readings

Software AG is going to bet more and more on containers for the delivery of its classical on-premises products. Soon, we will launch our own public container registry with regularly updated container images. You should also monitor our official GitHub account for any samples around the CI/CD promotion and container orchestration.

For a deeper understanding of how to deploy container solutions with webMethods, take a look at the more comprehensive blog post on containers and container orchestration. The topic is also discussed in the following webinar.

This article is part of the TECHniques newsletter blog - technical tips and tricks for the Software AG community. Subscribe to receive our quarterly updates or read the latest issue.
Items.Author: techcomm_sag

Items.Title: 使用 AWX-operator 在 Kubernetes 中安装 AWX v21.0.0 | Install AWX v21.0.0 in Kubernetes with AWX-operator
Items.Description:
更新ความความความมาเป็นไปของของของอย่างอย่างอย่างๆก็สักปีครับครับหยิบหยิบหยิบมามาดูดูดูดูว่าว่ามีอะไรอะไรใหม่ใหม่ใหม่ๆๆๆไหมไหมไหมไหมแต่แต่แต่แต่ส่วนส่วนส่วนใหญ่ใหญ่ผมผมผมเรียนเรียนรู้รู้งานงานงาน ลองเปลี่ยนใหม่ หยิบมาลงบน K8s ซะหน่อย
节点แต่ละ节点มีมีความจำจำ8 GB（ครั้งครั้งทดลองที่ที่ที่ที่ที่ที่ที่ไม่ไม่ไม่สามารถทำงานได้ได้）

  • 印度


    kubectl explain awx --recursive=true

    • ก้าวแรกกับ AWX ก้าวแรกกับ AWX
    มูลค่าความสุข มูลค่าความสุข


    • 原文:

    ผมมักจะ update ความเป็นมาเป็นไปของ AWX อย่างน้อย ๆ ก็สักปีละครับ หยิบมาดูว่ามีอะไรใหม่ ๆ ไหม แต่ส่วนใหญ่ผมเรียนรู้การใช้งาน AWX อยู่ในรูปแบบ container แบบเครื่องเดียวมาตลอด ปีนี้ลองเปลี่ยนใหม่ หยิบมาลงบน K8s ซะหน่อย
    ตอนแรกก็คิดว่าจะใช้เวลาหลายวัน คงลองผิดลองถูกติดตั้งเสร็จ ปรากฎว่า ไม่ยากอย่างที่คิดนะ แปร๊บบบบบบเดียวก็ได้ใช้แล้ว เสียเวลาตรงที่ node เรามี memory ไม่พอ ติดตั้งรอบแรกเสร็จก็เลยยังใช้ไม่ได้ มาดูครับ ไม่ยากเลย

    • Kubernetes 1.23.6
    • node แต่ละ node มีหน่วยความจำ 8 GB (ครั้งแรกทดลองที่ 2 GB ไม่สามารถทำงานได้)
    • awx-operator 0.20.2
    • ใช้ Longhorn เป็น Storageฃ
    • เครื่องที่ใช้ในการติดตั้งต้องมีข้อมูล kubeconfig เรียบร้อยแล้ว
    • เครืื่องที่ใช้ในการติดตั้งมี kubectl ให้พร้อมใช้งาน

    • เครื่องที่ใช้ในการติดตั้งมีระบบปฏิบัติการเป็น macOS version 12.3.1

Items.Title: 提交时自动进行代码审查 | Automating code review on commit
Items.Description:
想象一下每天花费数小时进行代码审查的忙碌。 代码审查自动化可以节省无数浪费的努力。

本文可能会引导您逐步了解如何添加可在每次代码提交期间执行的自动代码审查。

使用 Codacy，您可以在每次提交和拉取请求中获得静态分析、圈复杂度、重复和代码单元测试覆盖率变化。

将您的项目与 Codacy 集成的步骤

您可以使用您的 Git 提供商（例如 GitHub、GitLab 或 Bitbucket）来注册 Codacy。 这会将您的 Codacy 用户与您的 Git 提供者用户链接起来，从而更容易将存储库添加到 Codacy 并邀请您的团队成员。

现在您将需要加入包含您的存储库的组织

笔记：

示例 如果您的存储库是“github.com/Fruitsandvegies”。 然后您的组织名称将是“Fruitsandvegies”。

通过在步骤 3 中选择组织，codency 将列出所有可用的相关存储库。 选择“添加”选项以添加存储库以供审核。 笔记：

仅当您有权访问该存储库时，才能将存储库添加到 Codancy。

分析完成查看结果后，选择“转到存储库”。

详细分析结果

The initial analysis from Codancy will be based on the default configuration. Codancy 的初始分析将基于默认配置。 Codacy provides tweaks to allow you to set up a custom configuration such as files to ignore, code pattern, and adjust quality settings. Codacy 提供调整以允许您设置自定义配置，例如要忽略的文件、代码模式和调整质量设置。



原文:

Imagine the hustle of spending hours on code review every day. Code review automation may save countless wasted efforts.

This Article may walk you through steps on how to add automated code reviews that may be executed during every code commit.

Codacy is one of the tools that may be used to achieve this.

Codacy is an automated code review tool that helps developers ship better software, faster. With Codacy, you get static analysis, cyclomatic complexity, duplication, and code unit test coverage changes in every commit and pull request.

Steps to integrate your project with Codacy

1. Sign up

You may use your Git provider such as GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket to sign up to Codacy. This links your Codacy user with your Git provider user, making it easier to add repositories to Codacy and invite your teammates.

2. Grant access

Codacy will request access to your Git provider and will require you to provide a few details regarding your organization. (this helps them on use case evaluation).

3. Choose organization

Now you will be required to join the organizations that contain your repositories

Note:

codacy name the organizations similar to your git repository user name.

Example If your repository is “github.com/Fruitsandvegies”. Then the name of your organization will be “Fruitsandvegies”.

4. Adding repository

By selecting the organization on step 3, codency will list all related repositories available. Select the “add” option to add the repository for review. Codancy will immediately set up everything so as to be ready to analyze on the next commit.

Note:

You can add the repository to Codancy only if you have permission to that repository.

5. Review result of the analysis

Select “Go to repository” once the analysis has completed reviewing the results.

Detailed analysis result

The initial analysis from Codancy will be based on the default configuration. Codacy provides tweaks to allow you to set up a custom configuration such as files to ignore, code pattern, and adjust quality settings.

Items.Title: 在 Django 中加载样式和静态文件 | Loading styles and static files in Django
Items.Description:
我喜欢你是 Django 的新手。 是的，我有点喜欢这个框架，并对它可以做什么以及它向前发展的强大程度感到有点兴奋。 但是我正在处理的项目需要能够加载自定义样式表并覆盖一些引导程序位，现在有很多小教程，但它们似乎都是针对旧版本的框架。 所以我想我会很快列出它在当前版本中是如何完成的：

在项目的主文件夹中创建一个静态文件夹。 在里面添加一个 CSS 文件夹和你的 style.css 样式表。 应该看起来像这样

  • 管理.py
  • 我的应用
  • 静止的
  • 模板

所以现在转到您的 base.html 并将其添加到页面顶部

然后在

部分添加到我们的 css 的链接
<link rel= "stylesheet" href= "{% static 'css/styles.css' %}" >

最后在我们的 settings.py 文件中添加

# Static file route STATICFILES_DIRS = [ os . path . join ( BASE_DIR , 'static' ), ]

就是这样！ 我们应该都在用油做饭并加载样式，如果您想要加载 JS 或任何其他静态文件，您可以将它们全部添加到静态文件夹中，超级干净超级简单！



原文:

I like you am a newbie when it comes to Django. Yes I am slightly in love with the Framework and slightly excited about what it can do and how powerful it can be moving forward. But the project I'm working on I needed to be able to load a custom stylesheet in and overwrite some bootstrap bits, now there are plenty of little tutorials out there but they all seem to be for older versions of the framework. So I thought I'd quickly lay out how its being done in the current version:

Create a static folder in the main folder of your project. The one where the manage.py folder is. Inside there add a CSS folder and your style.css stylesheet. Should look like this

  • manage.py
  • myapp
  • static
  • templates

So now head over to your base.html and add this to the top of the page

And then in the

portion add a link to our css
<link rel="stylesheet" href="{% static 'css/styles.css' %}">

And finally in our settings.py file add

# Static file route
STATICFILES_DIRS = [ os.path.join(BASE_DIR, 'static'),
]

And thats it! We should be all cooking on gas and have the styles loading in, also if you want JS or any other static file to load you can add it all to the static folder, super clean and super easy!
