Items.Title: 我如何使用 Airtable 和 Gumroad 为我的时事通讯管理广告 | How I Manage Ads For My Newsletter With Airtable And Gumroad
Items.Description:
我的时事通讯Design Insight现在设有分类广告部分。 人们可以付费购买我的时事通讯中的广告位来宣传他们的产品或服务。

为了使这个过程更易于管理，我设置了Gumroad列表并将其与Airtable基础相结合。 通过这种设置，我已经将大部分流程自动化到只需要每周检查一次的程度。

本文介绍了我如何设置此过程，包括如何创建 Airtable 基础、发布用户提交表单、添加自动化以及设置 Gumroad 列表。

用户流

以下是购买广告位的用户流程：

  • 用户导航到 Gumroad 列表。
  • 他们阅读了有关广告位的信息，并从嵌入式 Airtable 视图中查看了可用的广告位。
  • 他们通过 Gumroad 购买广告位，然后被重定向到填写与广告位相关的详细信息的表格。
  • 他们填写表格并按提交。
  • 如果用户选择在他们的广告投放时收到通知，我会向他们发送一封电子邮件，让他们知道。

我首先设置了一个新的 Airtable 帐户。 我目前正在使用免费帐户并且没有遇到任何限制。

然后，我创建了一个新的基础来管理广告位数据。 我称之为设计洞察分类。

我知道我想要两张桌子，一张用来管理广告位的可用性，另一张用来管理预订细节。

表：分类可用性

领域：

  • 日期 - 通讯版本的日期（日期字段）
  • 广告位 1 - 我在每个版本中都有 2 个广告位的空间，因此每个广告位都在此处说明（多选 - 可用 + 已预订）
  • 插槽 2 - 同上。
  • 已完全预订 - 此字段表示新闻通讯版本的两个广告位都已预订（复选框字段）
  • 广告预订 - 这是一个链接字段，链接到下面的分类预订表。 当分类预订中的记录与此表中的日期字段（分类可用性）匹配时，它将包含在此字段中。 这会告诉我每周安排哪些广告预订。

表：分类预订

领域：

  • 标题 - 这是用于新闻通讯中广告的标题（文本字段）
  • 说明 - 这是简报中用于广告的主要副本（长文本字段）
  • 链接 - 这是通讯中广告资源的 URL（URL 字段）
  • 广告位可用性 - 我想让人们选择何时希望广告出现在时事通讯中。 此字段链接到另一个表（分类可用性）中的日期字段。 这意味着该表中包含的任何日期都将出现在该表中（分类预订）。
  • 直播时通知 - 这表示提交广告位的人是否希望在广告在时事通讯中直播时收到电子邮件（复选框字段）
  • 电子邮件 - 如果用户选择在其广告投放时收到通知，则在此字段（电子邮件字段）中跟踪他们的电子邮件地址

意见：

  • 公共 - 我创建了一个公共视图，用于 Gumroad 列表中，以向人们展示可用的广告位。 它只显示字段 Date、Slot 1 和 Slot 2。它还被过滤为仅显示未选中Fully Booked 的记录。 这意味着新闻通讯的订满版本将不会继续显示。
  • 私有 - 这是包含所有字段但没有过滤器的私有视图。

随着我设置提交表单和各种自动化等其他组件，这些表格随着时间的推移而演变。

一旦我设置了我需要的表，接下来就是创建一个表单来处理用户提交。

我原本打算在 Google 表单上管理它，但发现 Airtable 有表单功能。

在 Airtable 中，表单作为一种视图进行管理。 在我的例子中，我打开了 Classified Bookings 表并创建了一个名为 Design Insight Ad Spot 的表单视图。

表单字段：

  • 标题 - 对应于标题字段。 必需的
  • 描述 - 对应于描述字段。 必需的
  • 链接 - 对应于链接字段。 必需的
  • 广告位可用性 - 对应于广告位可用性字段。 我打开了“将记录选择限制为视图”选项并选择了公共视图。 这意味着只会显示公共视图中的记录。 如果您还记得上面的内容，公共视图仅显示未检查已订满的记录，因此用户只能从可用日期中进行选择。
  • 当我的广告上线时通知我 - 对应于上线时通知字段。
  • 电子邮件 - 一个条件字段，仅在上面的字段被选中时显示。

我已经打开了在填写表格时向我发送电子邮件的选项，因此我现在可以手动关注事情。

由于我使用的是 Airtable 的免费版本，因此在设计方面我可以使用的自定义选项有限，但目前效果很好。

在用户通过 Gumroad 购买广告位后，此表单的链接会显示给用户。

在我将此步骤标记为完成之前，我测试了几次提交表单以确保一切按预期工作。

与 Forms 类似，您可以在 Airtable 中创建日历视图。

这使我可以查看为哪个时事通讯版本安排了哪些广告位。 将此日历链接到我的 Google 日历也更加方便，因此我可以从任何地方查看它。

我在 Classified Bookings 表下创建了一个日历视图，并将其命名为广告日历。 然后我选择了 Ad Spot Availability 字段。

要将日历链接到我的 Google 日历，我选择了共享视图，然后选择了同步到外部日历。 这给了我一个链接，然后我将其粘贴到我的日历应用程序中。

好的，现在我已经设置好了表格、表格和日历，所以我有足够的空间来整理 Gumroad 列表。

我创建了一个名为 Design Insight Ad Spot 的新产品，并开始为广告位编写副本。

我包括了有关时事通讯的背景、各种统计数据以及时事通讯广告位的示例。

在列表的底部，我粘贴了分类预订表的公共 URL，它看起来很完美。

我确保产品 URL 清晰，创建了封面图片和缩略图，并设置了价格。

最后一步是在产品列表的内容部分中包含指向 Design Insight Ad Spot 表单的 URL。 为此，我将 URL 放入一个文本文件并将其上传到 Gumroad。 这允许我在购买后添加一条短消息，以感谢用户购买广告位。

我点击了发布，然后查看了新列表，以确保一切看起来都不错，包括 Airtable 嵌入。 您可以在此处查看实时 Gumroad 列表： Design Insight - Ad Spot 。

现在一切都已设置并运行，我想尝试一些 Airtable 自动化，以使维护一切变得更容易。

这部分本身可能是一篇完整的文章，所以我不会深入研究它是如何设置的。 我将包括有助于了解它是如何工作的屏幕截图。

我发现一些有助于使用 Airtable 自动化的技巧：

  • 您需要创建符合您尝试测试的标准的示例记录，否则您将无法正确测试自动化。
  • 创建自动化后，运行手动触发自动化所需的步骤并确保其按预期工作。
  • 清楚地命名您的自动化，以便您一眼就知道它们是什么。
  • 如果您正在努力组装自动化，请先在纸上写下您想要发生的事情。 我陷入了 UI 的困境，发现先写出自动化会更快。

自动化 1 - 将已预订的问题标记为已预订

当一期时事通讯被预订满时，会自动运行以选中“分类可用性”表中的“已预订”复选框。

这会将其从 Public Classified Availability 视图中移除，这也将其从 Design Insight Ad Spot 表单中移除。

自动化 2 - 提交表单时更新广告位可用性

提交 Design Insight Ad Spot 表单后，将该日期的相应广告位标记为已在分类可用性表中预订。

这意味着我不必手动查看每个表单提交并将每个插槽的可用性日历更改为已预订。

我在这篇文章中讨论了很多我知道的。 因此，如果您对其中的任何工作方式有任何疑问，请告诉我🙂

如果你喜欢这个，请考虑与其他可能觉得它有用的人分享🤗



原文:

My newsletter Design Insight now features a Classifieds section. For a fee, people can buy an ad spot in my newsletter to promote their product or service.

To make this process easier to manage I set up a Gumroad listing and combined it with an Airtable base. With this setup, I've automated most of the process to the point where I only need to check in on things once a week.

This article describes how I set up this process including how I created the Airtable base, published a form for user submissions, added automations, and set up the Gumroad listing.

User Flow

Here's the user flow for buying an ad spot:

  • A user navigates to the Gumroad listing.
  • They read about the ad spot and see the available ad spots from the embedded Airtable view.
  • They purchase an ad spot through Gumroad and are redirected to a form where they fill in detail related to the ad spot.
  • They fill in the form and press submit.
  • Airtable and I will handle the rest including adding it to the relevant newsletter edition.
  • If the user chose to be notified when their ad is live, I'll send them an email letting them know.

Airtable Base + Tables Setup

I started by setting up a new Airtable account. I'm currently using the free account and haven't run into any limitations.

I then created a new base to manage the ad spot data. I called it Design Insight Classifieds.

I knew I wanted 2 tables, one to manage the availability of ad spots and one to manage the booking details.

Table: Classified Availability

Fields:

  • Date - the date of the newsletter edition (Date field)
  • Slot 1 - I have space for 2 ad spots in each edition so each slot is accounted for here (Multiselect - Available + Booked)
  • Slot 2 - Same as above.
  • Fully Booked - This field denotes that a newsletter edition has both ad slots booked (Checkbox field)
  • Ad Bookings - This is a linked field that's linked to the Classified Bookings table below. When a record in Classified Bookings matches the Date field in this table (Classified Availability), it will be included in this field. This shows me which ad bookings are scheduled for each week.

Table: Classified Bookings

Fields:

  • Title - This is the title used for the ad in the newsletter (Text field)
  • Description - This is the main copy used for the ad in the newsletter (Long text field)
  • Link - This is the URL to the resource for the ad in the newsletter (URL field)
  • Ad Spot Availability - I wanted to give people the option to select when they'd like the ad to be in the newsletter. This field is linked to the Date field in the other table (Classified Availability). This means any dates included in that table will appear in this table (Classified Bookings).
  • Notify When Live - This denotes if the person submitting the ad spot wants to be emailed when the ad is live in the newsletter (Checkbox field)
  • Email - If the person chose to be notified when their ad is live, their email address is tracked in this field (Email field)

Views:

  • Public - I created a public view to be used in the Gumroad listing to show people what ad spots are available. It just shows the fields Date, Slot 1, and Slot 2. It is also filtered to only show records where Fully Booked is NOT checked. This means fully booked editions of the newsletter won't continue to show.
  • Private - This is the private view that has all the fields and no filters.

These tables evolved over time as I set up other components like the submission form and various automations.

Airtable Form Setup

Once I set up the tables I needed, the next thing was to create a form to handle user submissions.

I was originally going to manage this on a Google form but found out Airtable has a forms feature.

In Airtable, forms are managed as a type of view. In my case, I opened the Classified Bookings table and created a form view called Design Insight Ad Spot.

Form Fields:

  • Title - corresponds to the Title field. Required
  • Description - corresponds to the Description field. Required
  • Link - corresponds to the Link field. Required
  • Ad Spot Availability - corresponds to the Ad Spot Availability field. I turned on the option to "Limit record selection to a view" and chose the Public view. This means only records in the Public view will be shown. If you remember from above, the Public view only shows records where fully booked is NOT checked so users can only pick from available dates.
  • Notify Me When My Ad Is Live - corresponds to Notify When Live field.
  • Email - a conditional field that only shows if the field above is checked.

I've turned on the option to email me whenever the form is filled in so I can manually keep an eye on things for now.

As I'm on the free version of Airtable, there are limited customization options I can work with as regards the design but this works well for now.

The link to this form is presented to a user after they purchase an ad spot via Gumroad.

Before I marked this step as finished, I tested out submitting the form a few times to make sure everything worked as expected.

Airtable Calendar Setup

Similar to Forms, you can create Calendar views in Airtable.

This allows me to see which ad spots are scheduled for which newsletter edition. There's also the added convenience of linking this calendar to my Google calendar so I can view this from anywhere.

I created a Calendar view under the Classified Bookings table and called it Ad Calendar. I then selected the Ad Spot Availability field.

To link the calendar to my Google calendar, I selected Share view then selected Sync to an external calendar. This gave me a link which I then pasted into my calendar app.

Gumroad Listing Setup

OK, so by now I have the tables, form, and calendar set up so I have enough in place to put together the Gumroad listing.

I created a new product called Design Insight Ad Spot and started writing out the copy for the ad spot.

I included a background about the newsletter, various stats, and examples of newsletter ad spots.

At the bottom of the listing I pasted in the Public URL of the Classified Bookings table and it appeared perfectly.

I ensured the product URL was clear, created a cover image and thumbnail, and set the price.

The final step was to include the URL to the Design Insight Ad Spot form in the Content section of the product listing. To do this I put the URL into a text file and uploaded that to Gumroad. This allows me to add a short message after the purchase to thank the user for buying an ad spot.

I hit publish and then viewed the new listing to make sure everything looked good including the Airtable embed. You can see the live Gumroad listing here: Design Insight - Ad Spot.

Airtable Automations

Now that everything was set up and running, I wanted to experiment with some Airtable automations to make maintaining everything a bit easier.

This section could be a whole article in itself so I won't delve too deep into how it's all set up. I'll include screenshots where helpful to give an idea of how it's all working.

Some tips I found helpful for using Airtable automations:

  • You'll need to create sample records that fit the criteria you're trying to test otherwise you won't be able to test the automation properly.
  • Once you've created the automation, run the steps necessary to trigger the automation manually and make sure it works as expected.
  • Name your automations clearly so you know what they are at a glance.
  • If you're struggling to assemble the automation, write down what you want to happen on paper first. I got bogged down in the UI and found it quicker to write out the automation first.

Automation 1 - Mark fully booked issues as fully booked

When an issue of the newsletter is fully booked, an automation runs to check the checkbox "Fully booked" in the Classified Availability table.

This removes it from the Public Classified Availability view which also removes it from being selectable on the Design Insight Ad Spot form.

Automation 2 - Update Ad Spot Availability When Form Submitted

When the Design Insight Ad Spot form is submitted, mark the appropriate slot for that date as booked in the Classified Availability table.

This means I don't have to manually review every form submission and change the availability calendar for each slot to booked.

I've discussed a lot in this article I know. So if you have any questions for me on how any of this works, just let me know 🙂

If you enjoyed this, please consider sharing it with someone else who might find it useful 🤗

Items.Title: Shopware 数据关联示例 | Shopware data associations by examples
Items.Description:
文阮

发表于数据库表通常相互关联。 例如，一篇博客文章可能有很多评论，或者一个订单可能与发布它的用户相关。

这篇博文描述了如何向您的实体添加关联的小示例。

一对一

一对一关联是一种非常基本的数据库关联类型。 例如，一个User表可能与一个Media表相关联。 要定义此关联，我们将在UserDefinition和MediaDefinition上放置一个OneToOneAssociationField 。

让我们看一下两个实体定义的defineFields方法：

 // class UserDefinition extends EntityDefinition protected function defineFields (): FieldCollection { return new FieldCollection ([ ( new IdField ( 'id' , 'id' )) -> addFlags ( new PrimaryKey (), new Required ()), ( new FkField ( 'avatar_id' , 'avatarId' , MediaDefinition :: class )), ( new OneToOneAssociationField ( 'avatarMedia' , 'avatar_id' , 'id' , MediaDefinition :: class )), ]); }
 // class MediaDefinition extends EntityDefinition protected function defineFields (): FieldCollection { return new FieldCollection ([ ( new IdField ( 'id' , 'id' )) -> addFlags ( new PrimaryKey (), new Required ()), ( new OneToOneAssociationField ( 'avatarUser' , 'id' , 'avatar_id' , UserDefinition :: class , false )) -> addFlags ( new CascadeDelete ()), ]); }

一对多

一对多关联用于定义单个表是一个或多个子表的父表的关联。 例如，一个Product可能有无限数量的Price 。


 // class ProductDefinition extends EntityDefinition protected function defineFields (): FieldCollection { return new FieldCollection ([ ( new IdField ( 'id' , 'id' )) -> addFlags ( new PrimaryKey (), new Required ()), ( new OneToManyAssociationField ( 'prices' , ProductPriceDefinition :: class , 'product_id' )) -> addFlags ( new CascadeDelete (), new Inherited ()), ]); }
 // class PriceDefinition extends EntityDefinition protected function defineFields (): FieldCollection { return new FieldCollection ([ ( new IdField ( 'id' , 'id' )) -> addFlags ( new PrimaryKey (), new Required ()), ( new FkField ( 'product_id' , 'productId' , ProductDefinition :: class )) -> addFlags ( new Required ()), ( new ManyToOneAssociationField ( 'product' , 'product_id' , ProductDefinition :: class , 'id' , false )) -> addFlags ( new ReverseInherited ( 'prices' )), ]); }

多对多

例如，假设一个Product可以有很多Category并且Category可以有很多Product 。 此关联要求第三方实体可用。 它将被称为ProductCategoryDefinition并负责连接这两个定义。 它还需要自己的数据库表product_category 。


 // class ProductDefinition extends EntityDefinition protected function defineFields (): FieldCollection { return new FieldCollection ([ ( new IdField ( 'id' , 'id' )) -> addFlags ( new PrimaryKey (), new Required ()), ( new VersionField ()) -> addFlags ( new ApiAware ()), ( new ManyToManyAssociationField ( 'categories' , CategoryDefinition :: class , ProductCategoryDefinition :: class , 'product_id' , 'category_id' )) -> addFlags ( new ApiAware (), new CascadeDelete (), new Inherited (), new SearchRanking ( SearchRanking :: ASSOCIATION_SEARCH_RANKING )), ]); }
 // class CategoryDefinition extends EntityDefinition protected function defineFields (): FieldCollection { return new FieldCollection ([ ( new IdField ( 'id' , 'id' )) -> addFlags ( new PrimaryKey (), new Required ()), ( new VersionField ()) -> addFlags ( new ApiAware ()), ( new ManyToManyAssociationField ( 'products' , ProductDefinition :: class , ProductCategoryDefinition :: class , 'category_id' , 'product_id' )) -> addFlags ( new CascadeDelete (), new ReverseInherited ( 'categories' )), ]); }
 // class ProductCategoryDefinition extends MappingEntityDefinition protected function defineFields (): FieldCollection { return new FieldCollection ([ ( new FkField ( 'product_id' , 'productId' , ProductDefinition :: class )) -> addFlags ( new PrimaryKey (), new Required ()), ( new ReferenceVersionField ( ProductDefinition :: class )) -> addFlags ( new PrimaryKey (), new Required ()), ( new FkField ( 'category_id' , 'categoryId' , CategoryDefinition :: class )) -> addFlags ( new PrimaryKey (), new Required ()), ( new ReferenceVersionField ( CategoryDefinition :: class )) -> addFlags ( new PrimaryKey (), new Required ()), ( new ManyToOneAssociationField ( 'product' , 'product_id' , ProductDefinition :: class , 'id' , false )), ( new ManyToOneAssociationField ( 'category' , 'category_id' , CategoryDefinition :: class , 'id' , false )), ]); }

来源： https ://vient.me/shopware-data-associations-by-examples



原文:

Vien Nguyen

Posted on May 2

Database tables are often related to one another. For example, a blog post may have many comments or an order could be related to the user who placed it.

This blog post describes small examples of how to add associations to your entities.

One to One

A one-to-one association is a very basic type of database association. For example, a User table might be associated with one Media table. To define this association, we will place an OneToOneAssociationField on the UserDefinition and MediaDefinition.

Let's have a look at the defineFields methods of both entity definitions:

// class UserDefinition extends EntityDefinition
protected function defineFields(): FieldCollection
{ return new FieldCollection([ (new IdField('id', 'id'))->addFlags(new PrimaryKey(), new Required()), (new FkField('avatar_id', 'avatarId', MediaDefinition::class)), (new OneToOneAssociationField('avatarMedia', 'avatar_id', 'id', MediaDefinition::class)), ]);
}

// class MediaDefinition extends EntityDefinition
protected function defineFields(): FieldCollection
{ return new FieldCollection([ (new IdField('id', 'id'))->addFlags(new PrimaryKey(), new Required()), (new OneToOneAssociationField('avatarUser', 'id', 'avatar_id', UserDefinition::class, false))->addFlags(new CascadeDelete()), ]);
}

One to Many

A one-to-many association is used to define associations where a single table is a parent to one or more child tables. For example, a Product may have an infinite number of Price.


// class ProductDefinition extends EntityDefinition
protected function defineFields(): FieldCollection
{ return new FieldCollection([ (new IdField('id', 'id'))->addFlags(new PrimaryKey(), new Required()), (new OneToManyAssociationField('prices', ProductPriceDefinition::class, 'product_id'))->addFlags(new CascadeDelete(), new Inherited()), ]);
}

// class PriceDefinition extends EntityDefinition
protected function defineFields(): FieldCollection
{ return new FieldCollection([ (new IdField('id', 'id'))->addFlags(new PrimaryKey(), new Required()), (new FkField('product_id', 'productId', ProductDefinition::class))->addFlags(new Required()), (new ManyToOneAssociationField('product', 'product_id', ProductDefinition::class, 'id', false))->addFlags(new ReverseInherited('prices')), ]);
}

Many to Many

Shopware also provides methods to make working with many-to-many associations more convenient. For example, let's imagine a Product can have many Category and a Category can have many Product. This association require the third entity to be available. It will be called ProductCategoryDefinition and is responsible for connecting both definitions. It also needs its own database table product_category.


// class ProductDefinition extends EntityDefinition
protected function defineFields(): FieldCollection
{ return new FieldCollection([ (new IdField('id', 'id'))->addFlags(new PrimaryKey(), new Required()), (new VersionField())->addFlags(new ApiAware()), (new ManyToManyAssociationField('categories', CategoryDefinition::class, ProductCategoryDefinition::class, 'product_id', 'category_id'))->addFlags(new ApiAware(), new CascadeDelete(), new Inherited(), new SearchRanking(SearchRanking::ASSOCIATION_SEARCH_RANKING)), ]);
}

// class CategoryDefinition extends EntityDefinition
protected function defineFields(): FieldCollection
{ return new FieldCollection([ (new IdField('id', 'id'))->addFlags(new PrimaryKey(), new Required()), (new VersionField())->addFlags(new ApiAware()), (new ManyToManyAssociationField('products', ProductDefinition::class, ProductCategoryDefinition::class, 'category_id', 'product_id'))->addFlags(new CascadeDelete(), new ReverseInherited('categories')), ]);
}

// class ProductCategoryDefinition extends MappingEntityDefinition
protected function defineFields(): FieldCollection
{ return new FieldCollection([ (new FkField('product_id', 'productId', ProductDefinition::class))->addFlags(new PrimaryKey(), new Required()), (new ReferenceVersionField(ProductDefinition::class))->addFlags(new PrimaryKey(), new Required()), (new FkField('category_id', 'categoryId', CategoryDefinition::class))->addFlags(new PrimaryKey(), new Required()), (new ReferenceVersionField(CategoryDefinition::class))->addFlags(new PrimaryKey(), new Required()), (new ManyToOneAssociationField('product', 'product_id', ProductDefinition::class, 'id', false)), (new ManyToOneAssociationField('category', 'category_id', CategoryDefinition::class, 'id', false)), ]);
}

Source: https://viennt.me/shopware-data-associations-by-examples

Items.Title: 什么是搜索引擎营销？ | WHAT IS SEARCH ENGINE MARKETING?
Items.Description:
阿布舍克杜尔

发表于随着营销的进步和数字营销的出现，搜索引擎营销的持续问题围绕着各种广告商和营销商展开。

如果您对数字营销感兴趣，请访问本文的网站源

我们想出了这篇文章来回答这个关于什么是搜索引擎营销的非常具体的问题。

搜索引擎营销(SEM) 通过在 Google、Yahoo 或 Bing 等搜索引擎上购买广告来帮助公司进入在线市场。 事不宜迟，我将引导您完成五个步骤，以帮助您通过 5 个简单的步骤了解什么是搜索引擎营销以及它是如何工作的。 所以让我们直接进入它。

一、搜索引擎营销的定义
付费搜索广告可帮助营销人员通过在计算机或网络上进行搜索来获得更多网络流量。 要了解什么是搜索引擎营销，了解付费和无偿搜索引擎营销工具非常重要。 搜索引擎的主要帮助是准确地提供爬虫正在寻找的内容，而 SEM 帮助公司影响搜索引擎以向搜索引擎展示他们的广告。

要了解什么是搜索引擎营销，您必须了解以下因素

目标受众 您的目标 关键字的选择 在您的广告系列中排列这些关键字 制作引人入胜的广告 包含有关您的产品或服务的充分信息的登录页面 2. 与 SEM 相关的术语

在学习什么是搜索引擎营销时，接下来要了解的是了解与 SEM 相关的各种同义词和首字母缩略词，因为这会给初学者带来很多困惑。

事实上，在搜索营销之初，“SEM”一词是搜索引擎优化和付费搜索活动的总称，但随着时间的推移，SEM 专指付费搜索。

以下是大多数 SEM 专家用来演示 SEM 的一些同义词和缩写的列表-

付费广告 每次点击付费 (PPC) 每次点击费用 (CPC) 每千次展示付费 (PPM) 每千次展示费用 (CPM) 3. SEM 类型

我想写这篇文章的原因是“什么是搜索营销？ 打破搜索引擎营销不同层次的争议。 我们以无偿和有偿 SEM 的想法开始本文，现在是澄清情况的时候了。

根据搜索引擎优化方法，使用免费或免费搜索获得流量。 搜索引擎优化图片 SEARCH ENGINE OPTIMIZATION IMAGE 1.有机搜索引擎优化：

要通过 SEO 获得自然结果，学习 SEO 非常重要，这样您就可以创建一个与 SEO 兼容的网站，帮助您建立对业务的长期信任。

您应该知道如何使用白帽 SEO 技术来提高 SEO 的位置​​。 针对搜索引擎优化并专注于相关性和自然排名的网站使用白帽 SEO 技术进行了优化。

白帽搜索引擎优化的一些最著名的方法是

使用优质内容，优化网站，在内容中使用富有表现力的关键字。

您可以通过提高分数以及使用您的网站和应用程序来获得无机 SEO。 虽然有机过程可能需要很长时间才能实现，但他们会认识到快速吸引想法的负面后果。

但是，SEO 有时被宣传为支付 SEM 或 PPC。

无机 SEO 提示

挖掘和分组关键字，测试您的广告活动和着陆页。 争取更高的 PA/DA 分数 针对确切的位置和受众 对大多数搜索关键字进行适当出价 计算您的投资回报率以及您的营销策略 3.PPC ppc 图片 按点击图片付费

进行 PPC 时的重要因素

产品销售商或服务提供商。 计算PPC的一般公式是：每次点击付费（$）=广告费用（$）÷广告点击次数

您的 PPC 广告系列的成功取决于您的 Google AdWords 副本的编写、效率、说服力和简洁程度。 要运行 AdWords PPC 广告系列，您必须按照以下步骤操作 -

您还应该知道您的特定产品或服务如何最好地满足他们的需求。 您必须审查您的业务的市场地位并确定您的竞争对手。 现在，您需要确定最佳引擎营销指南建议的重要目标和推荐”，它们是搜索排名、网站流量、销售额等。 此外，您还应该搜索受众经常搜索的关键字。在研究其提供的产品和服务类型时，您应该考虑受众使用的热门术语。 您必须不断与您最好的客户服务、销售和客户服务团队互动，以找到最常用的短语。 您还可以使用关键字搜索工具来编制高度搜索的关键字词列表。 网站优化搜索引擎营销指南提供的重要建议是，每个营销人员都需要了解搜索引擎会搜索网站的底层代码，而您应该始终寻找可能吸引用户的准确、清晰、有趣、清晰和正确的内容. 在页面标题标签、目录名称、标题标签、文件名、元标签和 alt 标签中使用相关的关键字词组非常重要。 您应该注意您的元描述，它显示在您的链接下方的搜索结果中，并提供了一个吸引访问者访问您网站的绝佳机会。 为了加快这个过程，您可以将您的网站提交给 DMOZ.org，这是一个开放目录项目 DMOZ 接受的网站很容易获得其他搜索引擎的批准。 拥有高质量的入站链接与您的网站在搜索引擎中的受欢迎程度成正比。 在您的网站上创建值得链接的内容也很重要，您需要为您的网站创建有趣且信息丰富的内容。 您还应该尝试与合作伙伴、工会、供应商和客户取得联系。 基于网络的新闻稿和文章分发也是搜索引擎营销专家建议的最重要的搜索引擎营销策略之一。 使用网络分析来跟踪您的搜索引擎营销策略的进展和问题 始终监控您在 SERP 中的位置并制定策略以改善您的搜索结果。

原文:

abhishek dure

Posted on May 2

With the advancement in marketing and the advent of digital marketing, the ongoing question of what search engine marketing revolves around various advertisers and marketers.

If you have some interest in digital marketing pls visit SiteSource for this article

We came up with this post to answer this very specific question about what search engine marketing is.

Search Engine Marketing (SEM) helps companies enter the online market by buying ads on search engines such as Google, Yahoo or Bing. SEM practices revolve around website promotion in search results to optimize brand presence and conversion.

SEM uses paid and unpaid advertising media to optimize the advancement of online businesses, and we specially created this guide “What is search engine marketing” to help you understand how “paid and unpaid advertising” works under the general term “Search Engine Marketing”.

Without further ado, I’ll walk you through five steps to help you learn what search engine marketing is and how it works, in 5 simple steps. So let’s go straight into it.

1.DEFINITION OF SEARCH ENGINE MARKETING
SEM includes SEO and paid advertising, in which SEO is the practice of receiving traffic through unpaid or free lists, while paid advertising buys traffic through paid search lists.

Paid search ads help marketers get more web traffic by searching on a computer or network. To understand what search engine marketing is, it’s important to understand both paid and unpaid search engine marketing tools. SEM is one of the best ways to use competitive methods of acquisition costs to attract new customers, increase search engine rankings, strengthen your presence in search engines and optimize other marketing campaigns.

The main help of search engines is to deliver exactly what the crawler is looking for, and SEM helps companies influence search engines to show their ads to search engines.

To understand what search engine marketing is, you must understand the following factors

Target Audience Your goal Selection of keywords Arrangement of those keywords in your campaign Creating captivating Ads A landing page with adequate information about your product or service 2.TERMS RELATED TO SEM

The next thing to know when learning what search engine marketing is is to understand the various synonyms and acronyms associated with SEM, as this can create a lot of confusion for beginners.

In fact, at the very beginning of search marketing, the term “SEM” was a general term for search engine optimization and paid search activity, but over time, SEM refers exclusively to paid search.

The following is a list of some synonyms and abbreviations that most of SEM Experts uses to demonstrate SEM-

Paid ads Pay per click (PPC) Cost per click (CPC) Pay per thousand impressions (PPM) Cost per thousand impressions (CPM) 3.TYPES OF SEM

The reason I was thinking of writing this is “What is Search Marketing? To break the controversy with the different levels of search engine marketing. We started this article with an idea of unpaid and paid SEM, and now is the time to clarify the situation.

Traffic is earned using free or free searches according to search engine optimization methods. SEM can be unpaid, i.e. SEO, split into two different categories:

Search Engine Optimization image SEARCH ENGINE OPTIMIZATION IMAGE 1.Organic Search Engine Optimization:

To get organic results with SEO, it is important to learn SEO so that you can create an SEO-compatible website that helps you build long-term trust in your business.

You should know how to use White Hat SEO techniques to improve the position of SEO. Websites that are optimized for search engines and focus on relevance and organic ranking are optimized with White Hat SEO techniques.

Some of the most notable methods of White Hat SEO are

Using quality content, by optimizing the website, using expressive keywords in your content.

2.Inorganic Search Engine Optimization:
You can get inorganic SEO by improving your score and by using your website and application. While organic processes can take a long time to come, they will recognize the negative consequences of attracting ideas quickly.

However, SEOs are sometimes advertised as paying for SEM or PPC.

Tips for Inorganic SEO

Mining and grouping the keywords, Testing your ad campaign and your landing page. Aim for higher PA/DA Scores Targeting exact location and audience Proper bidding on most searched keywords Calculate your ROI as well as your marketing strategies 3.PPC ppc image PAY PER CLICK IMAGE

PPC or paid search engine marketing helps you implement an online advertising system that drives online traffic to your web pages and for that, you have to pay the publisher’s certain price when your PPC ad is clicked.

Factors that matter while doing PPC

Product seller or service provider. PPC marketer Your landing page Landing page provider The visitor

The general formula to calculate PPC is: Pay per click ($) = Advertising cost ($) ÷ Number of ad clicks

The success of your PPC campaign depends on how well-written, efficient, convincing, and concise your copy of Google AdWords is. To run an AdWords PPC campaign, you must follow the steps below –

Know the elements of PPC Ad. Know the right bidding amount Identify your USP. Know your competitors. Test your ad before launching. 4.SEARCH ENGINE MARKETING STEPS search engine marketing steps image SEARCH ENGINE MARKETING IMAGE

The following steps are important for paid SMM.

A.Marketing Strategy You must define your target audience and identify their needs and motivations. You should also know how your specific product or service might be best to meet their needs. You must review the market position of your business and identify your competitors. Now you need to identify important goals and referrals suggested by the best engine marketing guides” and they are search rank, site traffic, sales, and others. B.Keyword Research The next step, according to the “Search Engine Marketing Guide”, is to select the relevant keyword phrases. In addition, you should also search for the keywords that your audience frequently searches for You should think about the popular terms your audience uses when researching the type of products and services it offers. You must constantly interact with your best customer service, sales and customer service teams to find the most used phrases. You can also use keyword search tools to compile a list of highly searched keyword terms. C.Optimization of Website To optimize your site content for SERPs, it’s important to focus on your site structure and make it simple and search engine friendly. The important suggestions offered by the Website Optimization Search Engine Marketing Guides are that every marketer needs to understand that search engines search the underlying code of websites while You should always look for accurate, clear, interesting, clear and correct content that might appeal to your users. It is very important to use relevant keyword phrases in page title tags, directory names, header tags, file names, Meta tags, and alt tags. You should pay attention to your Meta description, which is shown in the search results below your link and offers a great opportunity to attract visitors to your site. D.Indexing of Website The next thing you should do is submit your site for indexing as this allows your site to be visible online to customers It is necessary to allow all pages of the site to be indexed by search engines. To speed up the process, you can submit your site to DMOZ.org which is an open directory project Websites accepted by DMOZ get easy approval from other search engines. E.Quality Links According to experts, this is one of the most important steps, and marketers need to create links for valuable links from sites to other sites that visitors you frequently visit. Having high-quality inbound links is proportional to your site’s popularity in search engines. Creating link-worthy content on your site is also important, and you need to create interesting and informative content for your site. You should also try to get contacts from partners, trade unions, vendors, and customers. Web-based distribution of press releases and articles is also one of the most important search engine marketing strategies suggested by Search engine marketing experts. F.Budget Management Paid search engine marketing, like “What is search engine marketing”, is an integral part of today’s direct marketing Bid for the most relevant keywords and make sure your products are interesting to your potential customers To run a PPC campaign, you must consider the following checklist – Monthly budget Daily budget Scope of geographical targeting Keyword list G.Measure your success Measuring your success is one of the most important search engine marketing strategies suggested by Experts To measure the success of an SEM campaign, use CPC, clicks, CPR, impressions, bounce rate, ROI, and so on. Use web analytics to track progress and issues in your search engine marketing strategies Always monitor your position in the SERPs and develop strategies to improve your search results.

Learn more by joining our DIGITAL MARKETING Class today!!!

Items.Title: Python 终极介绍 | Ultimate Introduction to Python
Items.Description:
发表于为什么是 Python？

  1. 易于学习：Python 关键字少，结构简单，语法明确。 这使学生能够快速掌握语言。
  2. 易于阅读：Python 代码定义更清晰，肉眼可见。
  3. 易于维护：Python 的源代码相当易于维护。
  4. 一个广泛的标准库：Python 的大部分库在 UNIX、Windows 和 Macintosh 上非常便携且跨平台兼容。
  5. 交互模式：Python 支持交互模式，允许对代码片段进行交互测试和调试。
  6. 可扩展性：Python 为大型程序提供了比 shell 脚本更好的结构和支持。
  7. 数据库：Python 提供所有主要商业数据库的接口

安装 Python

在此链接下载适用于 Windows、Linux 和 MacOS 的最新版本
变量变量存储可以在程序中使用和/或更改的信息。 此信息可以是整数、文本、集合等。变量用于保存用户输入、程序的本地状态等。变量有一个名称，以便可以在代码中引用它们。 要理解的基本概念是 Python 中的一切都是对象。 这些是标准数据类型。 要为变量赋值，请使用等号 (=)

Var_1= 1


var_2 = 'dog'
var_3 = "Caroline"

数据结构和序列

它们包括数字、字符串、列表、元组、集合和字典
数字包括整数、小数、浮点数

字符串


文字信息。 字符串是字母序列。 它们用引号括起来（单引号、双引号或三引号）。 它们是不可变的。 这意味着，一旦创建，它们就无法更改

元组 元组是一个固定长度、不可变的 Python 对象序列，由逗号分隔，括在括号中

{ tup1= (8,9,10) #create a tuple by enclosing a sequence in parathesis tup2=tuple('string') #convert any sequence to a tuple tup1[2] =10 #access any item in a tuple using square brackets }

列表列表是可变长度的，它们的内容可以就地修改（可变）。 它们由方括号 [] 或列表类型函数定义。

 { List_1= [2,3,4] #create list by enclosing sequence in square brackets List_2=list(tup1) #use the list type function Output= [8,9,10] }

列表中的操作 以下操作可以在列表中执行。

 { del(List_1[1]) #delete the second element using the del() function cop_list=List_1[:] #clone a list or create a copy by using a colon in square brackets List_2.append('cats') #add an element at the end of the list Output: List_2 [8,9,10,'cats'] List_2.insert(1, 'fox') #insert and element at index 1 of list 2 List_2 [8,'fox',9,10,'cats'] List_2.pop(3) #remove element at index 3 List_2 Output: [8,'fox',9,'cats'] List_2.extend([6, 'dogs']) #append multiple elements to a list using the extend function List_2 Output: [8,'fox',9,'cats', 6, 'dogs'] Print(len(List_2)) #obtain number of elements in list Output:6 }

字典字典是大小灵活的键值对集合，其中键和值是 Python 对象。 创建一个的一种方法是使用花括号 {} 和冒号来分隔键和值。 键可以是字符串、数字或元组。 值可以是任何数据类型。

 { dict_a={'a':[2,3], 'b':[4,5], 'c':[8,9]} #create a dictionary by assigning values to keys dict_a['a'] =[2,3] #access value of a key using square brackets }

集合集合是唯一元素的无序集合。 它们就像字典，但只有键，没有值。 可以通过两种方式创建集合：通过 set 函数或通过带有花括号的集合文字。 它们不记录元素位置并且具有独特的元素。 它们还支持算术运算。

 { Set1=('apple', 'banana',' apple', 'mango', 'kiwi') #create a set and excludes duplicates Set1 Output: {'apple', 'banana', 'mango', 'kiwi'} List3= [6,7,8] Set2= set(List3) #covert a list to a set Set2={6,7,8} Set1.remove('banana') Set1: {'apple', 'mango', 'kiwi'} }

算术运算

原文:

mwang-cmn

Posted on May 2

Python is a general-purpose interpreted, interactive, object-oriented, and high-level programming language created by Guido van Rossum during 1985- 1990.
Why Python?

  1. Easy-to-learn: Python has few keywords, simple structure, and a clearly defined syntax. This allows the student to pick up the language quickly.
  2. Easy-to-read: Python code is more clearly defined and visible to the eyes.
  3. Easy-to-maintain: Python's source code is fairly easy-to-maintain.
  4. A broad standard library: Python's bulk of the library is very portable and cross platform compatible on UNIX, Windows, and Macintosh.
  5. Interactive Mode: Python has support for an interactive mode which allows interactive testing and debugging of snippets of code.
  6. Scalable: Python provides a better structure and support for large programs than shell scripting.
  7. Databases: Python provides interfaces to all major commercial databases

Installing Python

Download the latest version for Windows, Linux and MacOS at this link
Variables Variables store information that can be used and/or changed in your program. This information can be an integer, text, collection, etc. Variables are used to hold user inputs, local states of your program, etc. Variables have a name so that they can be referenced in the code. The fundamental concept to understand is that everything is an object in Python. Python supports numbers, strings, sets, lists, tuples, and dictionaries. These are the standard data types. To assign variable a value, use the equals sign (=)

Var_1= 1


var_2 = ‘dog’
var_3 = "Caroline"

Data structures and sequences

They include numbers, strings, lists, tuples, sets and dictionaries
Numbers Include integers, decimals, floats

Strings


Textual information. Strings are sequence of letters. They are enclosed in quotation marks (single, double or triple). They are immutable. This means, once created, they cannot be changed

Tuples A tuple is a fixed length, immutable sequence of python objects separated by commas enclosed in parathesis

{
tup1= (8,9,10) #create a tuple by enclosing a sequence in parathesis
tup2=tuple(‘string’) #convert any sequence to a tuple
tup1[2] =10 #access any item in a tuple using square brackets
}

Lists Lists are variable length and their contents can be modified in place (mutable). They are defined by square brackets [] or the list type function.

{
List_1= [2,3,4] #create list by enclosing sequence in square brackets
List_2=list(tup1) #use the list type function
Output= [8,9,10]
}

Operations in lists The following operations can be carried out in lists.

{
del(List_1[1])  #delete the second element using  the del() function
cop_list=List_1[:] #clone a list or create a copy by using a colon in square brackets
List_2.append(‘cats’) #add an element at the end of the list
Output:
 List_2
[8,9,10,’cats’]
List_2.insert(1, ‘fox’) #insert and element at index 1 of list 2
List_2
[8,’fox’,9,10,’cats’]
List_2.pop(3) #remove element at index 3
List_2
Output: [8,’fox’,9,’cats’]
List_2.extend([6, ‘dogs’]) #append multiple elements to a list using the extend function
List_2
Output: [8,’fox’,9,’cats’, 6, ‘dogs’]
Print(len(List_2)) #obtain number of elements in list
Output:6
}

Dictionaries A dictionary is a flexibly sized collection of key-value pairs, where key and value are Python objects. One approach for creating one is to use curly braces {} and colons to separate keys and values. Keys can be strings, numbers or tuples. Values can be any data type.

{
dict_a={‘a’:[2,3], ‘b’:[4,5], ‘c’:[8,9]} #create a dictionary by assigning values to keys
dict_a[‘a’] =[2,3] #access value of a key using square brackets

}

Sets A set is an unordered collection of unique elements. They are like dictionaries, but keys only, no values. A set can be created in two ways: via the set function or via a set literal with curly braces. They do not record element position and have unique elements. They also support arithmetic operations.

{
Set1=(‘apple’, ‘banana’,’ apple’, ‘mango’, ‘kiwi’) #create a set and excludes duplicates
Set1
Output: {‘apple’, ‘banana’, ‘mango’, ‘kiwi’}
List3= [6,7,8]
Set2= set(List3) #covert a list to a set
Set2={6,7,8}
Set1.remove(‘banana’)
Set1: {‘apple’, ‘mango’, ‘kiwi’}
}

Arithmetic operations

Python supports the following operations

{
a+b #Addition 
a-b #Subtraction 
a*b #Multiplication
a/b #Division
a%b #Modulus that returns remainder of division
a**b #Exponentiation
a//b #Floor division that rounds the result to nearest whole number
}

Items.Title: 参加黑客马拉松的初学者指南 | A beginner's guide to participating in Hackathons
Items.Description:
黑客马拉松是任何初学者最热情和最具挑战性的平台！ 尽管您可能会严重失败或只是有资格在某些黑客马拉松中提交有效的项目，但您将在每个黑客马拉松中学到重要的一课。 如果您是某个特定领域的初学者并不重要，只需踏入并开始 hack！

我是一名前端开发人员，最近涉足 web3 并开始探索该领域。 有趣的是，使用包和依赖项（如 ThirdwebProvider、Alchemy 和 Ethers.js）开始为区块链编写前端非常容易。 在 web3 上开发一个月后，我决定开始参加黑客马拉松，以寻找志同道合的开发人员并提高我的技能。 我在hackerearth上遇到了Circle 黑客马拉松。 我立即申请并加入了他们的不和谐服务器。 到目前为止，我所知道的只是使用 React 或 Next.js 创建一个无服务器的前端网页。 但是，我知道我擅长我的技能，而且我学得很快。

几天后，他们discord服务器上的一位开发人员说他正在寻找一个前端开发人员加入他们的团队，他们的想法最终确定了。 因此，让我向您简要介绍一下任何黑客马拉松的常用方法。 通常，成功提交项目的两个主要组成部分是项目背后的想法和想法的执行。 我们是一个由 4 名、2 名后端开发人员、1 名区块链开发人员和 1 名前端开发人员（我自己）组成的团队。 我的队友向我介绍了在 circle API 上创建借贷系统的想法。 这个想法激发了我为这次黑客马拉松做更多事情的精神！

我尽了最大的努力，学习了如何进行前端 API 调用，并设置了路由器系统。 我试着让 UI 看起来很干净，虽然我不太擅长。 当我在虚拟电话中遇到我的队友时，我们对这个想法非常确定，并且也讨论了其他话题。 我了解到，与新朋友合作可以让您有机会扩大您的网络，并帮助您找到志同道合的人，您以后可能会与他们合作开展自己的项目。

我们在尝试使整个项目正常工作时遇到了几个错误，集成了前端、后端和区块链合约，但我们成功地完成了它并提交了一个有效的项目。

我的主要收获：

  • 不要自我怀疑自己。 你可能没有经验，但你不是无能。
  • 不要等待完全学习一些东西，或者不要陷入教程的黑洞。
  • 潜入并开始构建，您将更快地学习东西。
  • 如果您可以编写任何代码，那就是魔术的下一个目标！
  • 扩大您的网络，并与其他开发人员联系以进行协作和联系。

如果您是初学者并且不想参加黑客马拉松，请先尝试构建一个小项目，也许您想要自己的投资组合页面，或者您想为自己构建一个博客页面，任何东西。 但请记住记录您所做的一切并与您的朋友分享并征求他们的反馈。 这将帮助您更好地了解您的受众。

抛开所有不安全感不谈，如果您准备好潜入并参加黑客马拉松，这里有一些资源可以搜索您可以参与的即将到来的黑客活动！

我同意所有这些资源每天都会向您的电子邮件发送垃圾邮件，但我想它们给您的机会值得！

我希望这个博客能激发你的编码员。 如果你想和我联系，你可以在Twitter 上联系我



原文:

Hackathons are the most enthusiastic and challenging platforms for any beginner! Although you might fail badly or just qualify to submit a valid project in some of the hackathons, you will learn an important lesson in each of them. It doesn't matter if you are a beginner in a particular field, just step your foot in and start hacking!

I am a frontend developer who recently stepped into web3 and started exploring the space. Interestingly, it was pretty easy to start coding the frontend for blockchain using packages and dependencies like ThirdwebProvider, Alchemy, and Ethers.js. After a month of developing on web3, I decided to start participating in hackathons to find like-minded developers and sharpen my skills. I came across the Circle hackathon on hackerearth. I immediately applied and joined their discord server. Until now, all I knew was to create a server-less frontend webpage using React or Next.js. But, I knew that I was good at my skills and I am a fast learner.

After a few days, a fellow developer on their discord server said that he was looking for a frontend developer to join their team, and their idea was finalized. So let me brief you about the common approach to any hackathon. Generally, the 2 main components for a successful project submission would be the idea behind the project and the execution of the idea. We were a team of four, 2 backend developers, a blockchain developer, and a frontend developer(myself). My teammates briefed me on the idea of creating a lending system on the circle API. The idea just fuelled my spirit to do more for this hackathon!

I gave my best, learned how to make frontend API calls, and set up the router system. I tried to make the UI look clean, though I am not so good at it. When I met my teammates on a virtual call, we were pretty sure about the idea and connected on other topics too. I learned that teaming up with new people, gives you an opportunity to increase your network and helps you find like-minded people with whom you might, later on, collaborate on your own projects.

We ran into several errors while trying to make the whole project work, integrating the frontend, backend, and the blockchain contracts, but we managed to pull it off and submit a valid project.

My key takeaways:

  • Don't self-doubt yourself. You might be inexperienced, but you aren't incapable.
  • Don't wait to learn something completely, or don't get stuck in a black hole of tutorials.
  • Dive right in, and start building, you will learn things faster.
  • If you can code anything, that's the next thing to magic!
  • Increase your network, and reach out to other developers to collaborate and connect.

If you are a beginner and don't want to participate in hackathons, start by trying to build a small project, maybe you want your own portfolio page, or you want to build a blog page for yourself, anything. But remember to document everything you do and share it with your friends and ask for their feedback too. This will help you understand your audience better.

All your insecurities aside, if you are ready to dive in and participate in hackathons, here are a few resources to search for upcoming hacks you can participate in!

I agree that all these resources will spam your email every day, but I guess it's worth the opportunity they give you!

I hope this blog fuelled the coder in you. If you want to connect with me, you can connect me on Twitter

Items.Title: OOP 一种软件开发大众心理 | OOP a software development mass psychosis
Items.Description:
广义的外行对精神病的定义大致如下： 大规模精神病的定义是很多人都分享这种虚假的信念。

历史充满了大规模的精神病事件，今天在任何方面都没有什么不同。 历史上我最喜欢的大规模精神病事件是 500 年前在德国发生的“跳舞瘟疫” ，当时成千上万的人在没有明显原因的情况下因筋疲力尽而跳舞至死。 大约 30 年前，俄罗斯当局不得不关闭几个地区的小学，以遏制他们的“笑瘟疫” ，这种瘟疫开始在一所小学在青少年和儿童中蔓延，导致孩子们笑得无法自拔。

面向对象编程是个好东西

事实上，30 多年的历史告诉我们恰恰相反。 例如 ...

  • 在 OOP 中实现封装会导致不必要的复杂性，并且几乎无法理解代码
  • 多态性导致混乱并且极难追踪错误
  • 将数据（字段）与逻辑（方法）耦合是灾难的根源，并且以可以说是疯狂的定义的方式纠缠你的逻辑和数据
  • 单一职责会产生 1,000 多个类，这些类本来可以用 FP 中的 5 个函数完成。

我可以继续提到数百个有关 OOP 的此类问题。 然而，证据在于设计模式、干净的架构和 SOLID。 如果 OOP 真的是任何事情的解决方案，我们就不需要设计模式、干净的架构或 SOLID 设计原则。 我曾经听过一位 LISP 开发人员证明，GoF 书中 23 种原始设计模式中有 19 种在 LISP 中完全没有意义。 实际上，您可以根据编程语言所需的设计模式数量来量化编程语言的“精神病”数量，以便正确使用该语言。 如果你这样做了，你会意识到 99% 的设计模式只是围绕 OOP 的不足之处进行的“hacks” 。

当然，对于刚开始编码的开发人员来说，已经阅读了所有面向 OOP 的营销废话，将事实与虚构区分开来变得非常困难。 因此高级开发人员有责任制止这种精神病，并向初级开发人员解释更多函数式编程语言的优势，这样我们就可以有希望集体抛弃这种最初源于 50 多年前奥斯陆 Simula 67 的范式—— OOP 那当然是...

矛盾的是，相同的步骤几乎可以应用于这个世界上的任何事情，而第一步总是如下......

第一步，意识到你有问题

问题当然是面向对象编程，实现点是你大声说出这句话的时间点，不怕后果——承认面向对象实际上是一种精神病，无论如何都不是“出色的软件开发范式”怎么回事。 为了大声喊叫，每个计算机进程都包含或多或少完全相同的部分。 输入+过程产生输出。 用动词交换前一句中的过程，您已经有效地说明了曾经创建的每一个（成功的）计算机过程。

在动词方面，函数式编程在存在的各个方面都优于带有“主题”的 OOP。 所以在我之后说如下......

这不是解决方案，而是问题！

作为记录，如果你想使用一种理智的编程语言，那里有几十种不错的语言，例如......

  • 或者我个人最喜欢的（无耻插件）当然是 Hyperlambda

谈到 OOP，请问自己以下问题； 拒绝吧！



原文:

A broad layman's definition of a psychosis is roughly as follows; "The belief in something that has been proven to be wrong". The definition of a mass psychosis is when a lot of people shares this fake belief.

History is full of mass psychotic incidents, and today is not different in any ways. My favourite mass psychotic incident from history was "the dancing plague" from 500 years back in time in Germany, when literally thousands of people danced themselves to death from exhaustion without no apparent reasons what so ever. Some 30 years ago, Russian authorities has to close down primary schools in several districts to contain their "laughing plague" that started spreading at one primary school amongst teenagers and kids, resulting in children laughing so hard they could barely breadth.

For 30+ years, software developers have been taught a mantra that over time has turned into more or less the declaration of faith required to believe in to be able to land a job as a software developer, and this mantra is as follows.

Object Oriented Programming is a good thing

When in fact 30+ years of history has taught us the exact opposite. For instance ...

  • Implementing encapsulation in OOP results in unnecessary complexity and virtually impossible to understand code
  • Polymorphism results in chaos and extremely hard to track down bugs
  • Coupling data (fields) with logic (methods) is the recipe for disaster, and entangles your logic and data in ways that are arguably the very definition of madness
  • Single responsibility results in 1,000+ classes for something that could have been done with 5 functions in FP.

I could go on and mention hundreds of such issues with OOP. However the proof is in design patterns, clean architecture, and SOLID. If OOP was a solution to anything really, we wouldn't need design patterns, clean architecture, or SOLID design principles. OOP itself would be enough for us. The fact that OOP need crutches to stand upright, is by itself enough proof for us.

I once heard a LISP developer prove how 19 of the 23 original design patterns from GoF's book made absolutely no sense what so ever in LISP. In fact, you can quantify a programming language's amount of "psychosis" according to its required number of design patterns in order to correctly use the language. If you do, you will realise that 99% of all design patterns are simply "hacks" around OOP's inadequacies.

Of course for a developer who just started out coding, having read all the marketing gibberish for OOP, separating the truth from fiction becomes incredibly hard. It's therefor the responsibility of the senior developer to stop this psychosis, and explain the advantages of more functional programming languages to junior developers, such that we can hopefully collectively discard this paradigm that originally came out of Simula 67 in Oslo more than 50 years ago - OOP that is of course ...

AA have a 12 step program for healing yourself from addiction. Paradoxically, the same steps can be applied to almost anything in this world, and the first step is always as follows ...

Step 1, realise you have a problem

The problem of course is OOP, and the realisation point is the point in time when you utter this out loud, not afraid of the consequences - Admitting that OO is in fact a psychosis, and not a "brilliant software development paradigm" in any ways what so ever. For crying out loud, every single computer process consists of more or less the exact same parts; Input + process results in output. Exchange process in the former sentence with a verb, and you've effectively illustrated every single (successful) computer process that was ever created.

When it comes to verbs, functional programming is simply superior to OOP with its "subjects", in every regard that exists. So say after me as follows ...

OOP is a psychosis! It is not a solution, it is the problem!

For the record, if you want to work in a sane programming language, there exists dozens of nice languages out there, such as for instance ...

  • LISP
  • GoLang (yup, no OO here)
  • F#
  • Or my personal favourite (shameless plug) of course Hyperlambda

When it comes to OOP, ask yourself the following; "What would Ronald Reagan and Nancy Reagan do?" - The answer of course is simple ...

Just say no!
Items.Author: polterguy

Items.Title: 招聘 React 开发人员时要考虑的 10 个关键因素 | 10 Key Factors to Consider When Hiring React Developers
Items.Description:
由最大的社交网络公司开发。 该技术帮助 React Native 开发公司及其开发人员使用与 iOS 和 Android 移动软件兼容的代码来构建移动应用程序。 如果您也想使用 React Native 框架开发移动应用程序，那么建议您聘请专业的 React Native 开发人员或公司，他们可以为您提供物超所值的服务。 在移交您的移动应用程序开发项目之前，您应该牢记以下几点：

因此，对于 RN 开发人员来说，具备良好的 JS 知识是必须的。 因此，当您搜索React Native 开发人员招聘时，不要忘记这一点。

精通 ES6 和 React
脚本语言有助于不同平台上的多个独立实现。 与候选人或公司核对相同的内容。

在使用 React Native 开发移动应用程序时，最好熟悉 Redux - 一个有助于构建用户界面的开源 JS 库。 询问您雇用的 RN 开发人员。

完善的后端技能
如果您的项目涉及后端工作，请记住向您的开发人员询问 Meteor、Parse Server 或任何其他后端技术。

熟练掌握 React Native
建议仅雇用有经验的 React Native 来获得高效且功能强大的移动应用程序。 要找出相同的信息，请不要犹豫，询问有关该技术的问题。 另外，看看他们对常见的 RN 开发工具了解多少，例如 Sentry 错误跟踪、Bitrise、Hockeyapp、ESLint 和 Enzyme。

调试和测试技能
在开发应用程序时，很明显会面临错误和错误。 因此，当您雇用 React Native 开发人员时，请确保他们具备立即诊断和排除错误的技能。

通量设计模式的知识
确保您的 RN 开发人员非常了解它。

使用 Jest 或 Karma 进行单元测试的诀窍
使用 Jest 或 Karma 进行单元测试可确保应用程序的完美运行。 这些 JS 测试运行器需要在浏览器中运行前端测试。 不要忘记向您的 RN 开发人员询问这两个问题。

熟练掌握 CSS 和 HTML
因此，拥有它的知识将是一个额外的优势。

熟练使用 Gulp、Grunt 和 Bower 等 JavaScript 构建工具
询问有关在开发移动应用程序时用于构建用户界面的常见 JS 工具的问题。 如果您的开发人员知道它们，那么这绝对是一件好事。

应该能够提供全天候支持
您的 React Native 开发公司应该能够提供 24*7 的支持服务，以帮助您处理一天中任何时间可能发生的错误。 一些知名公司，例如 Quytech，在提供最佳React Native 开发服务方面有着良好的记录。 立即选择它们并为您的企业获取功能齐全的应用程序。



原文:

React Native is an open-source cross-platform mobile app framework that has made its way to the list of top front-end development technologies. Developed by the biggest social networking company. Facebook, the prime purpose behind the development of RN is to reduce the cost of the development process and time of coding.

The technology helps a React Native development company and its developers to build a mobile application by using the codes that was compatible with iOS and Android mobile software. The popularity of RN can be understood from the fact that even the renowned tech giants (Instagram, Skype, delivery.com, Pinterest, UberEats, Vogue, and Bloomberg) are using it for their mobile app platforms.

If you too want to develop a mobile app using React Native framework, then it is recommended to hire a professional React Native developer or company that can give you the best value for your money. Here are the points you should keep in mind before handing over your mobile app development project:

Expertise on Javascript and Its Libraries
JavaScript is the backbone of React Native; therefore, having a sound knowledge of JS is a must for an RN developer. So, don’t forget this important point when you search React Native developers for hire.

Well-Versed With ES6 and React
A React Native developer must have knowledge of ES6 or ECMAScript, which has been developed to standardize JavaScript. The scripting language helps in multiple independent implementations on different platforms. Check the same with the candidate or the company.

Knowledge of Redux
When developing a mobile app with React Native, it is good to be conversant with Redux- an open-source JS library that helps in building user interfaces. Ask the RN developer you hire for the same.

Sound Backend Skills
In case, if your project involves backend work, then remember to ask your developer about Meteor, Parse Server, or any other backend technology.

Proficiency in React Native
It is recommended to hire only experienced React Native to get an efficient and functional mobile application. To find out the same, don’t hesitate to ask questions about the technology. Also, check how much they know about the common RN development tools such as Sentry error tracking, Bitrise, Hockeyapp, ESLint, and Enzyme.

Debugging and Testing Skills
While developing an application, it is obvious to face errors and bugs. So, when you hire React Native developers, make sure they have this skill of diagnosing and troubleshooting errors immediately.

Knowledge of the Flux Design Pattern
Flux along with its libraries such as Fluxxor, Redux, MobX, etc. are used to build client-side web server applications. Ensure that your RN developer knows well about it.

Know-how of Unit Testing with Jest or Karma
Unit testing with Jest or Karma ensures the flawless functioning of an app. These JS test runners are required to run the testing of the frontend in a browser. Don’t forget to ask about these two to your RN developer.

Good command over CSS and HTML
React Native utilizes JSX, which is almost similar to HTML; therefore, having its knowledge would be an added advantage.

Proficiency in JavaScript build tools like Gulp, Grunt, and Bower
Ask questions about the common JS tools that are used for building user interfaces while developing a mobile application. If your developer knows about them, then it is definitely a good thing.

Should be able to provide round the clock support
Your React Native development company should be able to deliver 24*7 support services to help you deal with the bugs that may occur at any hour of the day. Some of the top-renowned companies, such as Quytech, have a proven record of delivering the best React Native development services. Choose them today and get a fully-functional app for your business.
Items.Author: hiwilliam2

Items.Title: 从特定网站上抓取 Google Scholar 出版物 | Scrape Google Scholar Publications from a particular website
Items.Description:
过滤的工作原理

要按某个网站过滤结果，您需要使用site:运算符，它将搜索结果限制为由名称中包含<website_name>的网站发布的论文。

除了OR运算符（即site:cabdirect.org OR site:<other_website> ）之外，还可以使用此运算符。 因此搜索查询将变为：

 search terms site:cabdirect.org OR site:<other_website>

先决条件

使用 CSS 选择器抓取基础知识

如果您还没有使用 CSS 选择器进行抓取，我有一篇专门的博客文章介绍了如何在网络抓取时使用 CSS 选择器，其中涵盖了它是什么、利弊，以及为什么从网络抓取的角度来看它们很重要和展示在网页抓取时使用 CSS 选择器的最常见方法。

独立的虚拟环境

简而言之，它创建了一组独立的已安装库，包括可以在同一系统中相互共存的不同 Python 版本，从而防止库或 Python 版本冲突。

如果您之前没有使用过虚拟环境，请查看使用 Virtualenv 和 Poetry 博客文章的专用 Python 虚拟环境教程以熟悉一下。

安装库：

 pip install requests parsel

减少被阻止的机会

请求可能会被阻止。 看看如何减少网页抓取时被阻止的机会，有十一种方法可以绕过大多数网站的阻止。

完整代码

from parsel import Selector import requests , json , os def check_websites ( website : list or str ): if isinstance ( website , str ): return website # cabdirect.org elif isinstance ( website , list ): return " OR " . join ([ f 'site: { site } ' for site in website ]) # site:cabdirect.org OR site:cab.net def scrape_website_publications ( query : str , website : list or str ): """ Add a search query and site or multiple websites. Following will work: ["cabdirect.org", "lololo.com", "brabus.org"] -> list[str] ["cabdirect.org"] -> list[str] "cabdirect.org" -> str """ # https://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#passing-parameters-in-urls params = { "q" : f ' { query . lower () } { check_websites ( website = website ) } ' , # search query "hl" : "en" , # language of the search "gl" : "us" # country of the search } # https://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#custom-headers headers = { "User-Agent" : "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/100.0.4896.127 Safari/537.36" } html = requests . get ( "https://scholar.google.com/scholar" , params = params , headers = headers , timeout = 30 ) selector = Selector ( html . text ) publications = [] # iterate over every element from organic results from the first page and extract the data for result in selector . css ( ".gs_r.gs_scl" ): title = result . css ( ".gs_rt" ). xpath ( "normalize-space()" ). get () link = result . css ( ".gs_rt a::attr(href)" ). get () result_id = result . attrib [ "data-cid" ] snippet = result . css ( ".gs_rs::text" ). get () publication_info = result . css ( ".gs_a" ). xpath ( "normalize-space()" ). get () cite_by_link = f 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar { result . css ( ".gs_or_btn.gs_nph+ a::attr(href)" ). get () } ' all_versions_link = f 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar { result . css ( "a~ a+ .gs_nph::attr(href)" ). get () } ' related_articles_link = f 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar { result . css ( "a:nth-child(4)::attr(href)" ). get () } ' publications . append ({ "result_id" : result_id , "title" : title , "link" : link , "snippet" : snippet , "publication_info" : publication_info , "cite_by_link" : cite_by_link , "all_versions_link" : all_versions_link , "related_articles_link" : related_articles_link , }) # print or return the results # return publications print ( json . dumps ( publications , indent = 2 , ensure_ascii = False )) scrape_website_publications ( query = "biology" , website = "cabdirect.org" )

导入库并定义一个函数：

 from parsel import Selector import requests , json , os

创建一个函数来检查website参数是str list还是string ：

 # check if returned website argument is string or a list def check_websites ( website : list or str ): if isinstance ( website , str ): return website # cabdirect.org elif isinstance ( website , list ): return " OR " . join ([ f 'site: { site } ' for site in website ]) # site:cabdirect.org OR site:cab.com

定义解析函数：

 def scrape_website_publications ( query : str , website : list or str ): # further code
代码解释
告诉 Python query和website参数应该是strings list或string类型

创建搜索查询参数，请求标头，将它们传递给请求：

 # https://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#passing-parameters-in-urls params = { "q" : f ' { query . lower () } site: { website } ' , # search query "hl" : "en" , # language of the search "gl" : "us" # country of the search } # https://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#custom-headers headers = { "User-Agent" : "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/100.0.4896.127 Safari/537.36" } html = requests . get ( "https://scholar.google.com/scholar" , params = params , headers = headers , timeout = 30 ) selector = Selector ( html . text )
代码解释
params 是作为dict传递给requests.get()的查询参数
haeders 是请求标头，而user-agent是用于充当“真实”用户访问的东西，因此网站（并非所有情况下）不会阻止请求。 我们需要传递我们的user-agent ，因为默认requests user-agent是python-requests ，因此网站可以理解它是一个脚本。
timeout 告诉请求在 30 秒后停止等待响应。

创建一个临时list ，遍历所有有机结果，然后提取数据：

 publications = [] # iterate over every element from organic results from the first page and extract the data for result in selector . css ( ".gs_r.gs_scl" ): title = result . css ( ".gs_rt" ). xpath ( "normalize-space()" ). get () link = result . css ( ".gs_rt a::attr(href)" ). get () result_id = result . attrib [ "data-cid" ] snippet = result . css ( ".gs_rs::text" ). get () publication_info = result . css ( ".gs_a" ). xpath ( "normalize-space()" ). get () cite_by_link = f 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar { result . css ( ".gs_or_btn.gs_nph+ a::attr(href)" ). get () } ' all_versions_link = f 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar { result . css ( "a~ a+ .gs_nph::attr(href)" ). get () } ' related_articles_link = f 'https://scholar.google.com/scholar { result . css ( "a:nth-child(4)::attr(href)" ). get () } '

将提取的数据作为dict附加到list中，并return或print结果：

 publications . append ({ "result_id" : result_id , "title" : title , "link" : link , "snippet" : snippet , "publication_info" : publication_info , "cite_by_link" : cite_by_link , "all_versions_link" : all_versions_link , "related_articles_link" : related_articles_link , }) # print or return the results # return publications print ( json . dumps ( publications , indent = 2 , ensure_ascii = False )) # call the function scrape_website_publications ( query = "biology" , website = "cabdirect.org" )

输出：

 [ { "result_id" : "6zRLFbcxtREJ" , "title" : "The biology of mycorrhiza." , "link" : "https://www.cabdirect.org/cabdirect/abstract/19690600367" , "snippet" : "In the second, revised and extended, edition of this work [cf. FA 20 No. 4264], two new " , "publication_info" : "JL Harley - The biology of mycorrhiza., 1969 - cabdirect.org" , "cite_by_link" : "https://scholar.google.com/scholar/scholar?cites=1275980731835430123&as_sdt=2005&sciodt=0,5&hl=en" , "all_versions_link" : "https://scholar.google.com/scholar/scholar?cluster=1275980731835430123&hl=en&as_sdt=0,5" , "related_articles_link" : "https://scholar.google.com/scholar/scholar?q=related:6zRLFbcxtREJ:scholar.google.com/&scioq=biology+site:cabdirect.org&hl=en&as_sdt=0,5" }, ... other results
]

或者，您可以使用来自 SerpApi 的Google Scholar Organic Results API来做同样的事情。 这是一个带有免费计划的付费 API。

不同之处在于您不必从头开始创建解析器、维护它、弄清楚如何扩展它、如何绕过 Google 的块以及找出哪些代理/验证码提供者是好的。

 # pip install google-search-results import os , json from serpapi import GoogleSearch from urllib.parse import urlsplit , parse_qsl def serpapi_scrape ( query : str , website : str ): params = { # https://docs.python.org/3/library/os.html#os.getenv "api_key" : os . getenv ( "API_KEY" ), # your serpapi API key "engine" : "google_scholar" , # search engine "q" : f " { query } site: { website } " , # search query "hl" : "en" , # language # "as_ylo": "2017", # from 2017 # "as_yhi": "2021", # to 2021 "start" : "0" # first page } search = GoogleSearch ( params ) publications = [] publications_is_present = True while publications_is_present : results = search . get_dict () print ( f "Currently extracting page # { results . get ( 'serpapi_pagination' , {} ). get ( 'current' ) } .." ) for result in results [ "organic_results" ]: position = result [ "position" ] title = result [ "title" ] publication_info_summary = result [ "publication_info" ][ "summary" ] result_id = result [ "result_id" ] link = result . get ( "link" ) result_type = result . get ( "type" ) snippet = result . get ( "snippet" ) publications . append ({ "page_number" : results . get ( "serpapi_pagination" , {}). get ( "current" ), "position" : position + 1 , "result_type" : result_type , "title" : title , "link" : link , "result_id" : result_id , "publication_info_summary" : publication_info_summary , "snippet" : snippet , }) if "next" in results . get ( "serpapi_pagination" , {}): # splits URL in parts as a dict and passes it to a GoogleSearch() class. search . params_dict . update ( dict ( parse_qsl ( urlsplit ( results [ "serpapi_pagination" ][ "next" ]). query ))) else : papers_is_present = False print ( json . dumps ( organic_results_data , indent = 2 , ensure_ascii = False ))
  • 谷歌学术有机结果 API

在推特上加入我们 | 添加功能请求💫或错误🐞



原文:

How filtering works

To filter results by a certain website, you need to use site: operator which restricts search results to papers published by websites containing <website_name> in their name.

This operator can be used in addition to OR operator i.e site:cabdirect.org OR site:<other_website>. So the search query would become:

search terms site:cabdirect.org OR site:<other_website>

Prerequisites

Basic knowledge scraping with CSS selectors

CSS selectors declare which part of the markup a style applies to thus allowing to extract of data from matching tags and attributes.

If you haven't scraped with CSS selectors, there's a dedicated blog post of mine about how to use CSS selectors when web-scraping that covers what it is, pros and cons, and why they're matter from a web-scraping perspective and show the most common approaches of using CSS selectors when web scraping.

Separate virtual environment

In short, it's a thing that creates an independent set of installed libraries including different Python versions that can coexist with each other in the same system thus preventing libraries or Python version conflicts.

If you didn't work with a virtual environment before, have a look at the dedicated Python virtual environments tutorial using Virtualenv and Poetry blog post of mine to get familiar.

📌Note: this is not a strict requirement for this blog post.

Install libraries:

pip install requests parsel

Reduce the chance of being blocked

There's a chance that a request might be blocked. Have a look at how to reduce the chance of being blocked while web-scraping, there are eleven methods to bypass blocks from most websites.

Full Code

from parsel import Selector
import requests, json, os def check_websites(website: list or str): if isinstance(website, str): return website # cabdirect.org
 elif isinstance(website, list): return " OR ".join([f'site:{site}' for site in website]) # site:cabdirect.org OR site:cab.net
 def scrape_website_publications(query: str, website: list or str): """ Add a search query and site or multiple websites. Following will work: ["cabdirect.org", "lololo.com", "brabus.org"] -> list[str] ["cabdirect.org"] -> list[str] "cabdirect.org" -> str """ # https://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#passing-parameters-in-urls
 params = { "q": f'{query.lower()} {check_websites(website=website)}', # search query
 "hl": "en", # language of the search
 "gl": "us" # country of the search
 } # https://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#custom-headers
 headers = { "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/100.0.4896.127 Safari/537.36" } html = requests.get("https://scholar.google.com/scholar", params=params, headers=headers, timeout=30) selector = Selector(html.text) publications = [] # iterate over every element from organic results from the first page and extract the data
 for result in selector.css(".gs_r.gs_scl"): title = result.css(".gs_rt").xpath("normalize-space()").get() link = result.css(".gs_rt a::attr(href)").get() result_id = result.attrib["data-cid"] snippet = result.css(".gs_rs::text").get() publication_info = result.css(".gs_a").xpath("normalize-space()").get() cite_by_link = f'https://scholar.google.com/scholar{result.css(".gs_or_btn.gs_nph+ a::attr(href)").get()}' all_versions_link = f'https://scholar.google.com/scholar{result.css("a~ a+ .gs_nph::attr(href)").get()}' related_articles_link = f'https://scholar.google.com/scholar{result.css("a:nth-child(4)::attr(href)").get()}' publications.append({ "result_id": result_id, "title": title, "link": link, "snippet": snippet, "publication_info": publication_info, "cite_by_link": cite_by_link, "all_versions_link": all_versions_link, "related_articles_link": related_articles_link, }) # print or return the results
 # return publications
 print(json.dumps(publications, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)) scrape_website_publications(query="biology", website="cabdirect.org")

Import libraries and define a function:

from parsel import Selector
import requests, json, os

Create a function to check if website argument is either a list of str or a string:

# check if returned website argument is string or a list

def check_websites(website: list or str): if isinstance(website, str): return website # cabdirect.org
 elif isinstance(website, list): return " OR ".join([f'site:{site}' for site in website]) # site:cabdirect.org OR site:cab.com

Define a parse function:

def scrape_website_publications(query: str, website: list or str): # further code
Code Explanation
query: str/website: list or str to tell Python that query and website arguments should be with a type of list of strings or a string

Create search query parameters, request headers, pass them to request:

# https://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#passing-parameters-in-urls
params = { "q": f'{query.lower()} site:{website}', # search query
 "hl": "en", # language of the search
 "gl": "us" # country of the search
} # https://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#custom-headers
headers = { "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/100.0.4896.127 Safari/537.36"
} html = requests.get("https://scholar.google.com/scholar", params=params, headers=headers, timeout=30)
selector = Selector(html.text)
Code Explanation
params is a query parameters that passed to requests.get() as a dict
haeders is request headers, and user-agent is a thing that is used to act as a "real" user visit so websites (not all and in all cases) don't block the request. We need to pass our user-agent because the default requests user-agent is python-requests so websites understand that it's a script.
timeout to tell requests to stop waiting for a response after 30 seconds.

Create a temporary list, iterate over all organic results, and extract the data:

publications = [] # iterate over every element from organic results from the first page and extract the data
for result in selector.css(".gs_r.gs_scl"): title = result.css(".gs_rt").xpath("normalize-space()").get() link = result.css(".gs_rt a::attr(href)").get() result_id = result.attrib["data-cid"] snippet = result.css(".gs_rs::text").get() publication_info = result.css(".gs_a").xpath("normalize-space()").get() cite_by_link = f'https://scholar.google.com/scholar{result.css(".gs_or_btn.gs_nph+ a::attr(href)").get()}' all_versions_link = f'https://scholar.google.com/scholar{result.css("a~ a+ .gs_nph::attr(href)").get()}' related_articles_link = f'https://scholar.google.com/scholar{result.css("a:nth-child(4)::attr(href)").get()}'

Append extracted data to the list as a dict, and return or print the results:

publications.append({ "result_id": result_id, "title": title, "link": link, "snippet": snippet, "publication_info": publication_info, "cite_by_link": cite_by_link, "all_versions_link": all_versions_link, "related_articles_link": related_articles_link,
}) # print or return the results
# return publications

print(json.dumps(publications, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)) # call the function
scrape_website_publications(query="biology", website="cabdirect.org")

Outputs:

[ { "result_id": "6zRLFbcxtREJ", "title": "The biology of mycorrhiza.", "link": "https://www.cabdirect.org/cabdirect/abstract/19690600367", "snippet": "In the second, revised and extended, edition of this work [cf. FA 20 No. 4264], two new ", "publication_info": "JL Harley - The biology of mycorrhiza., 1969 - cabdirect.org", "cite_by_link": "https://scholar.google.com/scholar/scholar?cites=1275980731835430123&as_sdt=2005&sciodt=0,5&hl=en", "all_versions_link": "https://scholar.google.com/scholar/scholar?cluster=1275980731835430123&hl=en&as_sdt=0,5", "related_articles_link": "https://scholar.google.com/scholar/scholar?q=related:6zRLFbcxtREJ:scholar.google.com/&scioq=biology+site:cabdirect.org&hl=en&as_sdt=0,5" }, ... other results
]

Alternatively, you can do the same thing using Google Scholar Organic Results API from SerpApi. It's a paid API with a free plan.

The difference is that you don't have to create the parser from scratch, maintain it, figure out how to scale it, how bypass blocks from Google, and figure out which proxy/captcha providers are good.

# pip install google-search-results

import os, json
from serpapi import GoogleSearch
from urllib.parse import urlsplit, parse_qsl def serpapi_scrape(query: str, website: str): params = { # https://docs.python.org/3/library/os.html#os.getenv
 "api_key": os.getenv("API_KEY"), # your serpapi API key
 "engine": "google_scholar", # search engine
 "q": f"{query} site:{website}", # search query
 "hl": "en", # language
 # "as_ylo": "2017", # from 2017
 # "as_yhi": "2021", # to 2021
 "start": "0" # first page
 } search = GoogleSearch(params) publications = [] publications_is_present = True while publications_is_present: results = search.get_dict() print(f"Currently extracting page #{results.get('serpapi_pagination', {}).get('current')}..") for result in results["organic_results"]: position = result["position"] title = result["title"] publication_info_summary = result["publication_info"]["summary"] result_id = result["result_id"] link = result.get("link") result_type = result.get("type") snippet = result.get("snippet") publications.append({ "page_number": results.get("serpapi_pagination", {}).get("current"), "position": position + 1, "result_type": result_type, "title": title, "link": link, "result_id": result_id, "publication_info_summary": publication_info_summary, "snippet": snippet, }) if "next" in results.get("serpapi_pagination", {}): # splits URL in parts as a dict and passes it to a GoogleSearch() class.
 search.params_dict.update(dict(parse_qsl(urlsplit(results["serpapi_pagination"]["next"]).query))) else: papers_is_present = False print(json.dumps(organic_results_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))

Items.Title: 如果你明天失去一切怎么办？ | What if you lost everything tomorrow?
Items.Description:
蒂娜黄

发表于你还会每天在那个工作项目上花费 8 个小时以上，而不是你一直说你会建立的梦想业务吗？ 当你知道自己付出了 1000% 的努力时，你还会忍受在生活的任何方面付出一半的努力吗？

如果明天你知道除了你和你自己什么都不重要，你今天会做什么不同的事情？ 变化很大，不是吗？ 那么，为什么今天没有呢？ 是什么阻碍了你？

西尔维斯塔比斯特罗维奇 - 卢修斯·伊曼纽尔·埃马克森 - Mar 29 3月29日



原文:

Tina Huynh

Posted on May 1

Would you still be spending 8+ hours a day on that work project instead of the dream business you always said you would build? Would you still be putting up with any half-efforts in any area of your life when you know you're putting in 1000%?

What would you do differently today if you knew nothing else mattered but you and yourself tomorrow? It changes a lot, doesn't it? So, why doesn't it today? What's holding you back?

Silvestar Bistrović - Apr 3

Lucius Emmanuel Emmaccen - Mar 29

Items.Title: 美国前 23 家贷款抵押贷款初创公司 | Top 23 US Lending Mortgage Startups
Items.Description:
卡特琳娜·帕霍莫娃

发表于原文由 SoftFormance 撰写： https ://www.softformance.com/blog/top-us-lending-mortgage-startups/

这些天，借贷市场正在蓬勃发展。 事实上，抵押贷款占美国贷款市场的最大部分，总债务为 9.2 万亿美元。

这就是为什么越来越多的借贷金融科技初创公司进入市场也就不足为奇了。

那么为什么不利用这个绝妙的机会帮助人们更轻松地获得抵押贷款呢？

这些全新的抵押金融科技公司正在缓慢但肯定地取代传统银行。

它们帮助人们不必担心巨额利率、缓慢的申请流程和成堆的文件。

它们提供了更方便、更快捷、更方便的贷款流程，无论是用于房地产、大学还是购买汽车。

如果您曾经想成功进入抵押贷款市场并在行业处于上升期时创建您的初创公司——现在正是最佳时机。

在这篇文章中，我们将详细讨论当前的市场趋势以及创建贷款初创公司所面临的挑战。 此外，我们深入研究并列出了 23 家最成功的美国抵押贷款初创公司。

因此，如果您想启动您的在线借贷平台——请继续阅读。

为什么选择网贷？
截至 2021 年，美国抵押贷款债务总额为 10.44 万亿美元。 对于大多数美国人来说，住房是他们最大的开支。 他们将每月收入的大部分用于租金或抵押贷款。

那些有抵押贷款的美国人每月支付的中位数为 1,595 美元。 抵押贷款也称为无担保个人贷款，传统上由银行和其他金融机构提供。 直到最近几年。

现在，想要获得个人贷款的人可以在传统银行或金融科技公司之间自由选择。

以下是人们选择金融科技抵押贷款初创公司而不是银行或信用合作社等传统参与者的几个原因：

降低利率

根据研究，与传统银行相比，金融科技贷款公司提供的利率较低。 原因之一是它们不需要遵守同样严格的规定并且更加灵活。

没有人为因素
传统的抵押贷款行业是建立在与人沟通的基础上的，所以人的因素比较强。 有时，他们的决定可能会有偏差，这会影响客户是否收到贷款。 对于借贷初创公司，没有人为因素——一切都是自动化的，一个人可以在不与任何人交谈的情况下提交他们的数据。

可访问性
您可以随时随地申请贷款； 无需调整您的日程安排或通勤到银行。 借助在线借贷平台，一切尽在掌握。

方便
老实说，没有人喜欢在申请贷款时填写成堆的文件并淹没在其中。 在线抵押金融科技公司让客户忘记了这个问题。 相反，他们可以在线填写简单的表格。

速度
传统银行的申请过程需要大量时间。 当客户在线申请贷款时，他们可以随时查看贷款状态，即时收到所需信息，节省在银行排队的时间。

市场趋势
如果您想成功进入金融科技借贷市场，您需要了解当前的趋势。

了解它们可以帮助您推出一个既在市场上具有竞争力又为人们提供有效贷款方式的平台。

数字化和自动化

借贷市场的主要趋势之一是整个流程的完全自动化和数字化。

在您的智能手机上拥有一个 24/7 全天候可用的借贷平台对客户来说是一个巨大的好处，但这种趋势也为贷方带来了很多好处。

首先，数字化和自动化降低了运营成本。 无需在办公室租金、设施和聘请内部专家团队上浪费金钱。

如果您决定启动贷款创业公司，您肯定会优化预算并节省成本。

其次，自动化消除了人为错误。 每个人都会犯错误，有时这可能会损害流程或减慢流程。

通过自动化，您可以确保几乎没有错误。 这样，您的员工可以专注于其他任务，而不必担心在某些日常活动中犯错。

最后，自动化的在线抵押贷款平台使决策过程更快，客户的请求得到更快的处理。

这里的关键是构建能够处理大量数据处理的强大软件。 如果您的平台拥有出色的软件、用户友好且功能强大的界面，那么借贷过程将非常快速和简单。

全渠道用户体验
您是否知道 67% 拥有现有住房贷款的人愿意在笔记本电脑或台式电脑上完成贷款申请？ 只有 29% 的人更喜欢使用智能手机。

这就是为什么确保全渠道用户体验并为您的客户提供高效的贷款流程如此重要的原因。

如果你选择只坚持一个平台，你迟早会开始失去其他地方的客户。 在构建借贷应用程序时，请确保覆盖尽可能多的渠道，因为它可以增强用户体验并使借贷过程对他们来说尽可能舒适。

将数据转化为可行的见解
贷款过程就是收集客户数据。 在传统的烘焙中，人们在申请抵押贷款时会填写成堆的文件和表格。 通常，此过程可能非常耗时。

借助在线抵押平台，客户数据的验证速度加快，并且允许贷方在更短的时间内完成更多交易。

此外，它可以提高客户忠诚度，您的客户将与您保持更长时间，并且更有可能将您的平台推荐给其他人。

收集所有数据很重要，但您还需要正确分析它。

要做到这一点，您需要具有自学系统的人工智能工具。 因此，您的借贷平台将在市场上变得更具竞争力。

排名前 23 位的借贷初创公司
我们已经讨论了启动您的在线抵押贷款公司的原因以及要在市场上具有竞争力的趋势。 现在，是时候概述一下美国最成功的 30 家抵押贷款初创公司了。

募集资金：2050 万美元

服务：这不是一个借贷平台。 该公司在整个资金链中提供分析工具，以帮助提高流动性和管理风险。

募集资金：840 万美元

募集资金：1.819亿美元

成立年份：1983

募集资金：6亿美元

服务： PNC 为客户提供不同类型的抵押贷款，除了房屋净值信贷额度和房屋净值贷款。 对于低收入和中等收入的借款人，PNC 提供 1,500 美元的赠款。 除了在线申请流程和帮助外，人们还可以与代理人进行面对面的会议以获得额外的指导。

您可能面临的挑战
不幸的是，启动贷款初创公司并不是一个顺利的过程，您可能会面临大多数贷款平台所面临的几个挑战。 最好做好准备，所以让我们潜入：

低抵押贷款利率
不幸的是，抵押贷款利率预计将保持在与前几年相同的低水平，当时平均年利率约为 4%（年百分比率）。

虽然这对客户来说是个好消息，但在线抵押贷款机构认为这个消息是个坏消息。

待售房屋减少
虽然对房屋的需求正在上升，但待售房屋的数量却没有。 房地产选择短缺有几个原因。

首先，许多房主不想出售他们的财产。 租用它并每月赚取被动收入更容易。

其次，由于价格上涨，人们往往在同一所房子里住得更久。 根据全国房地产经纪人协会的数据，人们现在至少在一个地方生活了十年，而之前是六七年。

最后，高昂的监管支出使得建造新房变得过于昂贵。 事实上，根据全国房屋建筑商协会的数据，各级政府实施的监管占平均房屋成本的 32.1%。

由于此类贷款需要较低的最低首付，因此它是金融科技贷款初创公司的强大竞争对手。 当有更多选择可供选择时，贷款初创公司可能处于劣势。

包起来
随着越来越多的人想要出售或购买房屋，对借贷平台的需求也在不断增长。 这就是为什么抵押贷款市场现在正在上升，并且不会很快改变的原因。

如果您想利用这个机会创办一家借贷初创公司，请记住，大多数人都希望借贷过程尽可能快速、简单和舒适。

拥有一支优秀的专家团队，您将能够创建一个在市场上具有竞争力的应用程序，并帮助多人购买他们的梦想家园。
