Items.Title: 通过示例学习方案：IUP GUI 与鸡方案 | Learn Scheme by Example: IUP GUI with Chicken Scheme
Items.Description:
它在 Linux 上使用 GTK，在 macOS 上也使用 GTK。 根据路线图，有一个 macOS 原生驱动程序正在开发中，但它看起来像是那些永远“即将推出”的功能之一。 有各种编程语言的绑定，包括鸡蛋。 让我们构建一个用于生成音调的 GUI，而不是构建另一个计算器。

你需要安装鸡计划。 它在大多数 Linux 发行版的存储库中都可用。 在安装 IUP egg 之前，您还需要安装 IUP 库。 不幸的是，IUP 似乎没有为许多 Linux 发行版打包。 幸运的是，IUP 网站上有预编译的二进制文件可供下载。 它们被打包为 tarball。 每个 tarball 都包含用于安装库的 shell 脚本（将它们移动到可能需要sudo的系统范围的位置，具体取决于您的发行版）。 除了IUP之外，您还需要下载由 PUC-Rio 的 Tecgraf 研究所开发的另外两个库，称为IM和CD 。 对于 Linux，不同的内核版本有不同的下载。 这些似乎对应于不同 Ubuntu 版本附带的 Linux 内核版本。 我使用 Debian Sid，它的内核比任何 Ubuntu 版本都新，所以我刚刚下载了最新可用内核的软件包，它运行良好。 将自己放在下载 tarball 的目录中，然后：

 $ mkdir iup $ tar -xpvf iup- * _lib.tar.gz -C iup $ sudo iup/install $ sudo iup/install_dev $ sudo cp iup/ftgl/lib/Linux * / * /usr/lib64 $ mkdir im $ tar -xpvf im- * _lib.tar.gz -C im $ sudo im/install $ sudo im/install_dev $ mkdir cd $ tar -xpvf cd- * _lib.tar.gz -C cd $ sudo cd /install $ sudo cd /install_dev

上述脚本将库安装在 Debian 上的非标准路径中，而chicken-install无法找到它们。 您可以使用CSC_OPTIONS环境变量来告诉chicken-install在哪里可以找到这些文件。

 $ CSC_OPTIONS = '-I/usr/include/iup -I/usr/include/im -I/usr/include/cd -L/usr/lib64' chicken-install -sudo iup

使用 Chicken 执行程序时，您还需要指定动态库路径。

 $ LD_LIBRARY_PATH = /usr/lib64 csi bleep.scm

如果使用原生 C 中的 IUP，属性设置是与对象构造分开的步骤。 属性名称是可以与子小部件一起直接传递给小部件构造函数的关键字。 属性有一个尾随冒号。 您可以在原始 C 文档中查找小部件接受的属性。

 ( import iup ) ; Main window ( define dlg ( dialog title: "Bleep" ( vbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 25 margin: '25x25 ))) ( show dlg ) ( main-loop ) ( destroy! dlg ) ( exit 0 )

让我们首先在这个vbox中放置一个滑块。

 ( define slider ( valuator min: 20 max: 20000 value: 440 expand: 'Yes )) ; Main window ( define dlg ( dialog title: "Bleep" ( vbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 25 margin: '25x25 slider )))

在 IUP 的说法中，滑块是valuator 。 在 Scheme 中，您可以使用“是”和“否”符号。 为了帮助组织，您可以将小部件存储在变量中。 您可以通过在任何其他属性之后将子小部件传递给vbox构造函数来将子小部件添加到vbox （实际上，子小部件可以在其他属性之前传递，甚至可以与其他属性混合，但我发现将它们分组在结束）。

人类可听到的频率范围通常在 20 Hz 到 20 KHz 之间（随着年龄的增长，我们会失去听到其中一些更高频率的能力）。 中音 C 上方的音符 A为 440 Hz。 由于 A4 作为通用调优标准，它似乎是一个明智的默认设置，但如果您在 Chicken 中运行上述代码，您将看到以下内容：

理想情况下，440 会落在滑块的中间。 为了实现这一点，让我们使用对数刻度。

我找到了关于如何将滑块映射到对数刻度的Stack Overflow 答案。 答案中给出的代码是 JavaScript，但它很容易移植到 Scheme。

 ; Scale used by slider ( define *min-position* 0 ) ( define *max-position* 2000 ) ; Range of frequencies ( define *min-frequency* 20 ) ( define *max-frequency* 20000 ) ; Logarithmic scale for frequency (so middle A [440] falls about in the middle) ; Adapted from https://stackoverflow.com/questions/846221/logarithmic-slider ( define min-freq ( log *min-frequency* )) ( define max-freq ( log *max-frequency* )) ( define frequency-scale ( / ( - max-freq min-freq ) ( - *max-position* *min-position* ))) ; Convert slider position to frequency ( define ( position->frequency position ) ( round ( exp ( + min-freq ( * frequency-scale ( - position *min-position* )))))) ; Convert frequency to slider position ( define ( frequency->position freq ) ( round ( / ( - ( log freq ) min-freq ) ( + frequency-scale *min-position* ))))

我在脚本顶部添加了一些全局参数。 变量名*min-position*只是全局参数的 Lisp 命名约定。 我通过反复试验得出了 0-2,000 的范围。 它似乎在滑块的每个步骤之间取得了最佳平衡，从而显着改变了频率，同时仍然允许用户仅使用滑块缩小特定频率。

然后我们创建两个函数：一个获取滑块上的位置并返回频率（ position->frequency ），另一个获取频率并返回滑块上的位置（ frequency-position ）。 现在让我们使用frequency->position函数设置滑块的初始位置：

 ( define slider ( valuator min: *min-position* max: *max-position* value: ( frequency->position 440 ) expand: 'Yes ))

滑块下方是一个显示当前频率的旋转框和用于将频率增加/减少一个八度的按钮。

 ( define frequency-field ( textbox spin: 'Yes spinmin: *min-frequency* spinmax: *max-frequency* spinvalue: 440 )) ( define frequency-controls ( hbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 25 margin: '0x0 ( button title: '< ) ( hbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 5 frequency-field ( label "Hz" )) ( button title: '> ))) ; Main window ( define dlg ( dialog title: "Bleep" ( vbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 25 margin: '25x25 slider frequency-controls )))

在 IUP 中，旋转框只是一个将spin属性设置为“是”的文本框。 在这一点上，我们开始有一个漂亮的界面，但它没有做任何事情。 如果您单击按钮或滑动滑块，则不会发生任何事情。 小部件也接受回调。 回调就像属性一样被指定，并且是带有一个或多个参数的函数，通常是 self，它是回调所属的小部件，并返回一个符号：通常是'default但也'close以关闭对话框或其他，例如'ignore并'continue 。 可以在原始 C 文档中查找小部件接受的回调名称及其参数。 滑块/评估器和旋转框都有一个valuechanged-cb回调（从原始 C API 中的valuechanged_cb转换而来）。

 ( define slider ( valuator min: *min-position* max: *max-position* value: ( frequency->position 440 ) expand: 'Yes valuechanged-cb: ( lambda ( self ) ( attribute-set! frequency-field spinvalue: ( position->frequency ( string->number ( attribute self value: )))) 'default ))) ( define frequency-field ( textbox spin: 'Yes spinmin: *min-frequency* spinmax: *max-frequency* spinvalue: 440 valuechanged-cb: ( lambda ( self ) ( attribute-set! slider value: ( frequency->position ( string->number ( attribute self spinvalue: )))))))

每个函数的第一个参数是一个小部件，第二个参数是一个属性。 设置属性时，您还可以为该属性提供一个带有新值的附加参数。 属性值始终以字符串形式返回。 我们的对数转换函数采用数字，因此我们必须使用 string- string->number将属性字符串转换为数字。

将按钮的action回调连接到称为decrease-octave和increase-octave的函数。 八度音程是“一个音高与另一个音高之间的间隔，其频率是两倍”。

 ; Set frequency slider and display ( define ( set-frequency freq ) ( when ( and ( >= freq *min-frequency* ) ( <= freq *max-frequency* )) ( attribute-set! slider value: ( frequency->position freq )) ( attribute-set! frequency-field value: freq ))) ; Buttons increase and decrease frequency by one octave ( define ( adjust-octave modifier ) ( set-frequency ( * ( string->number ( attribute frequency-field spinvalue: )) modifier ))) ( define ( decrease-octave self ) ( adjust-octave 0.5 )) ( define ( increase-octave self ) ( adjust-octave 2 ))

让我们使用另一个旋转框来指定哔声的持续时间（以毫秒为单位）：

 ( define duration-field ( textbox spin: 'Yes spinmin: 1 spinmax: 600000 ; 10 minutes spinvalue: 200 )) ( define general-controls ( hbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 25 margin: '0x0 ( hbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 5 duration-field ( label "ms" )))) ; Main window ( define dlg ( dialog title: "Bleep" ( vbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 25 margin: '25x25 slider frequency-controls general-controls )))

频率相当抽象。 让我们也让用户能够选择一个音符。 我们可以将 A4-G4 的相应频率存储在关联列表中。

 ; Notes -> frequency (middle AG [A4-G4]) ; http://pages.mtu.edu/~suits/notefreqs.html ( define notes ' (( "A" 440.00 ) ( "B" 493.88 ) ( "C" 261.63 ) ( "D" 293.66 ) ( "E" 329.63 ) ( "F" 349.23 ) ( "G" 292.00 )))

我们将为用户提供一个下拉菜单。 每当从下拉菜单中选择一个音符时，我们将在关联列表中查找频率，并使用我们为八度音程按钮创建的set-frequency辅助函数进行设置。

 ( define general-controls ( hbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 25 margin: '0x0 ( hbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 5 duration-field ( label "ms" )) ( hbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 5 ( label "♪" ) ( listbox # :1 "A" # :2 "B" # :3 "C" # :4 "D" # :5 "E" # :6 "F" # :7 "G" value: 1 dropdown: 'Yes action: ( lambda ( self text item state ) ( set-frequency ( cadr ( assoc text notes ))))))))

现在，让我们发出一些声音。 我们感兴趣的是可用于生成正弦波音调的音频插件。 您需要安装 Allegro 库。 确保您还安装了头文件。 在一些 Linux 发行版中，它们被分成一个单独的包（例如 Debian 上liballegro5-dev ）。 另外，安装 Allegro egg ( chicken-install -sudo allegro )。 我在顶部附近添加了以下几行来导入 Allegro 绑定（以及我们还将使用的小鸡内存模块）。

 ( import ( prefix allegro "al:" )) ( import ( chicken memory )) ( define +pi+ 3.141592 )

您必须在show主 IUP 对话框和启动 IUP main-loop的函数之间放置初始化 Allegro 的代码。 如果它出现在show之前，程序将因segmentation violation而终止，并且它不能在main-loop之后出现，因为它在窗口关闭之前永远不会被执行。

 ; Display GUI ( show dlg ) ; Initialize Allegro and audio addon ; Must be initialized afer showing the main dialog but before starting the main ; loop or else program will die with a segmentation violation. ( unless ( al:init ) ( print "Could not initialize Allegro." )) ( unless ( al:audio-addon-install ) ( print "Could not initialize sound." )) ( al:reserve-samples 0 ) ; Start IUP main loop ( main-loop ) ( destroy! dlg ) ( exit 0 )

我会将该示例回馈给 Allegro egg，但该存储库在 GitHub 上被标记为“由所有者存档”并且是只读的。 我已将示例与本教程的其余代码一起包含在 repo 中，以防有人发现它有用。

您创建一个音频stream 。 在这种情况下，流以 44,100 Hz（音频 CD 的采样率）的频率（通常称为采样率）缓冲 1,024 个样本的八个片段，这意味着每秒有 44,100 个样本。 每个样本都是一个 32 位的浮点数（称为音频的位深度），我们只有一个通道来让事情尽可能简单。

 ; Generate a tone using Allegro ( define ( generate-tone frequency duration ) ( let* (( samples-per-buffer 1024 ) ( stream-frequency 44100 ) ( amplitude 0.5 ) ( stream ( al:make-audio-stream 8 samples-per-buffer stream-frequency 'float32 'one )) ( queue ( al:make-event-queue )) ( event ( al:make-event ))) ( unless ( al:audio-stream-attach-to-mixer! stream ( al:default-mixer )) ( print "Could not attach stream to mixer." )) ( al:event-queue-register-source! queue ( al:audio-stream-event-source stream )) ( let event-loop (( n 0 )) ; Grab and handle events ( when ( and ( < n ( / ( * ( / duration 1000 ) stream-frequency ) samples-per-buffer )) ( al:event-queue-wait! queue event )) ( case ( al:event-type event ) ( 'audio-stream-fragment ( let (( buffer ( al:audio-stream-fragment stream ))) ; If the stream is not ready for new data, buffer will be null. ( if ( not buffer ) ( event-loop n ) ( begin ( fill-buffer buffer n ) ; Placeholder ; Repeat ( event-loop ( + n 1 ))))))))) ( al:audio-stream-drain stream )))

一个事件循环等待音频流请求另一个缓冲区。 我们的工作是一次用 1,024 个 32 位浮点数填充该缓冲区。 在上面的代码清单中，这是由fill-buffer完成的。 那只是一个占位符，所以我可以将代码分成更短、更容易解释的块。 这是代替(fill-buffer buffer n)内容：

 ( let (( adr ( pointer->address buffer ))) ( let loop (( i 0 )) ( when ( < i samples-per-buffer ) ( let (( time ( / ( + ( * samples-per-buffer n ) i ) stream-frequency ))) ; al:audio-stream-fragment returns a C pointer. Use (chicken ; memory) module to operate on foreign pointer objects. ; Iterate over array four bytes at a time since 32-bit depth. ( pointer-f32-set! ( address->pointer ( + adr ( * i 4 ))) ( * amplitude ( sin ( * 2 +pi+ frequency time )))) ( loop ( + i 1 ))))) ( unless ( al:audio-stream-fragment-set! stream buffer ) ( print "Error setting stream fragment" )))

如果 egg 能够在 Allegro 上提供一些 Scheme 便利，比如允许我们将 Scheme 列表或数组传递给 Allegro 以提供样本缓冲区，那就太好了。 既然没有，我们将使用 chicken 内存模块从audio-stream-fragment返回的 C 指针开始填充 C 数组。 我们使用pointer->address来获取指针的地址。 指针引用一个字节的内存。 我们可以通过在地址上减或加 1 来引用前面或后面的字节。 由于我们用 32 位浮点数填充数组，而 32 位是 4 个字节，我们希望每次将地址增加 4。 然后我们可以用pointer-f32-set! 然后您只需要一次输入 1,024 个样本的 Allegro 缓冲区。 正弦波的基本公式是 A sin(2πft)，其中A是幅度， f是频率， t是时间。

 ( * amplitude ( sin ( * 2 +pi+ frequency time )))

将其连接到播放按钮，您就可以发出一些声音了。

 ( button title: "Play" action: ( lambda ( self ) ( generate-tone ( attribute frequency-field spinvalue: ) ( attribute duration-field spinvalue: ))))

您可以在GitHub 上查看整个示例。 这开始是一个个人学习项目，旨在探索 Lisp 中的 GUI 编程状态，并已成为一系列关于使用各种 Lisp 方言构建 GUI 的教程。



原文:

IUP is a cross-platform GUI toolkit that uses native controls. It uses GTK on Linux and, well, GTK on macOS too. According to the roadmap, there is a macOS native driver in the works, but it looks like one of those perpetually "coming soon" features. There are bindings for various programming languages including a Chicken egg. Instead of building yet another calculator, let's build a GUI for generating a tone.

You'll need Chicken Scheme installed. It's available in the repositories of most Linux distros. You'll also need the IUP libraries installed before you can install the IUP egg. Unfortunately, IUP doesn't appear to be packaged for many Linux distros. Fortunately, there are pre-compiled binaries available for download on the IUP website. They are packaged as a tarball. Each tarball includes shell scripts for installing the libraries (moving them to system-wide locations for which sudo may be required depending on your distro). In addition to IUP, you also need to download two other libraries also developed by Tecgraf Institute at PUC-Rio called IM and CD. For Linux, there are different downloads for different kernel versions. These appear to correspond to versions of the Linux kernel that came with different Ubuntu releases. I use Debian Sid, which has a newer kernel than any Ubuntu release, so I just downloaded the package for the most recent kernel available, and it worked fine. Position yourself in the directory where you downloaded the tarballs and:

$ mkdir iup
$ tar -xpvf iup-*_lib.tar.gz -C iup
$ sudo iup/install
$ sudo iup/install_dev
$ sudo cp iup/ftgl/lib/Linux*/* /usr/lib64
$ mkdir im
$ tar -xpvf im-*_lib.tar.gz -C im
$ sudo im/install
$ sudo im/install_dev
$ mkdir cd
$ tar -xpvf cd-*_lib.tar.gz -C cd
$ sudo cd/install
$ sudo cd/install_dev

The above scripts install the libraries in non-standard paths on Debian, and chicken-install is unable to find them. You can use the CSC_OPTIONS environment variable to tell chicken-install where to find these files.

$ CSC_OPTIONS='-I/usr/include/iup -I/usr/include/im -I/usr/include/cd -L/usr/lib64' chicken-install -sudo iup

You'll also need to specify the dynamic library path when executing the program with Chicken.

$ LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/usr/lib64 csi bleep.scm

If using IUP from its native C, attribute setting is a separate step from object construction. The Scheme bindings add a little syntactic sugar that allow specifying your entire UI as an S-expression. Attribute names are keywords which can be passed directly to widget constructors along with child widgets. The attributes have a trailing colon. You can look up what attributes a widget accepts in the original C documentation.

(import iup) ; Main window
(define dlg (dialog title: "Bleep" (vbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 25 margin: '25x25))) (show dlg)
(main-loop)
(destroy! dlg)
(exit 0)

The vbox is an invisible widget that helps with layout. Let's start by placing a slider within this vbox.

(define slider (valuator min: 20 max: 20000 value: 440 expand: 'Yes)) ; Main window
(define dlg (dialog title: "Bleep" (vbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 25 margin: '25x25 slider)))

In IUP parlance, a slider is a valuator. IUP convention is to use "Yes" and "No" as boolean values. In Scheme you can use the 'Yes and 'No symbols. To help with organization, you can store the widget in a variable. You can add child widgets to the vbox by passing them to the vbox constructor after any other attributes (actually, the child widgets can be passed before other attributes or even mixed in with the other attributes, but I find it clearest to keep them grouped at the end).

The range of frequencies audible by humans is typically between 20 Hz and 20 KHz (we lose the ability to hear some of those higher frequencies as we age). The musical note A above middle C is 440 Hz. Since A4 serves as a general tuning standard, it seems like a sensible default, but if you run the above in Chicken, this is what you'll see:

The scale of 20 to 20,000 is so large that 440 doesn't appear to move the slider at all. Ideally, 440 would fall about the middle of the slider. To achieve this, let's use a logarithmic scale.

I found a Stack Overflow answer on how to map a slider to a logarithmic scale. The code given in the answer is JavaScript, but it was easy enough to port to Scheme.

; Scale used by slider
(define *min-position* 0)
(define *max-position* 2000)
; Range of frequencies
(define *min-frequency* 20)
(define *max-frequency* 20000) ; Logarithmic scale for frequency (so middle A [440] falls about in the middle)
; Adapted from https://stackoverflow.com/questions/846221/logarithmic-slider (define min-freq (log *min-frequency*))
(define max-freq (log *max-frequency*))
(define frequency-scale (/ (- max-freq min-freq) (- *max-position* *min-position*)))
; Convert slider position to frequency
(define (position->frequency position) (round (exp (+ min-freq (* frequency-scale (- position *min-position*))))))
; Convert frequency to slider position
(define (frequency->position freq) (round (/ (- (log freq) min-freq) (+ frequency-scale *min-position*))))

I added some global parameters to the top of the script. The variable name *min-position* is just a Lisp naming convention for global parameters. I came up with the range of 0-2,000 by trial and error. It seemed to strike the best balance between each step of the slider making a noticeable change to the frequency while still allowing the user to narrow in on a specific frequency with just the slider.

Then we create two functions: one that takes the position on the slider and returns the frequency (position->frequency) and another that takes a frequency and returns the position on the slider (frequency-position). Now let's set the initial position of our slider with the frequency->position function:

(define slider (valuator min: *min-position* max: *max-position* value: (frequency->position 440) expand: 'Yes))

Underneath the slider is a spin box showing the current frequency and buttons to increase/decrease the frequency by one octave.

(define frequency-field (textbox spin: 'Yes spinmin: *min-frequency* spinmax: *max-frequency* spinvalue: 440))
(define frequency-controls (hbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 25 margin: '0x0 (button title: '<) (hbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 5 frequency-field (label "Hz")) (button title: '>))) ; Main window
(define dlg (dialog title: "Bleep" (vbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 25 margin: '25x25 slider frequency-controls)))

In IUP a spin box is just a text box that has the spin attribute set to "Yes." At this point, we are starting to have a nice looking interface, but it doesn't do anything. If you click the buttons or slide the slider, nothing happens. Widgets also take callbacks. Callbacks are specified just like attributes and are functions that take one or more arguments, usually self, which is the widget to which the callback belongs, and return a symbol: usually 'default but also 'close to close a dialog or others such as 'ignore and 'continue. The names of callbacks that a widget accepts and their arguments can be looked up in the original C documentation. Both the slider/valuator and the spin box have a valuechanged-cb callback (converted from valuechanged_cb in the original C API).

(define slider (valuator min: *min-position* max: *max-position* value: (frequency->position 440) expand: 'Yes valuechanged-cb: (lambda (self) (attribute-set! frequency-field spinvalue: (position->frequency (string->number (attribute self value:)))) 'default)))
(define frequency-field (textbox spin: 'Yes spinmin: *min-frequency* spinmax: *max-frequency* spinvalue: 440 valuechanged-cb: (lambda (self) (attribute-set! slider value: (frequency->position (string->number (attribute self spinvalue:)))))))

The attribute and attribute-set functions get the value of an attribute and set the value of an attribute, respectively. The first argument to each function is a widget, the second an attribute. When setting an attribute, you also provide an additional argument with the new value for the attribute. Attribute values are always returned as strings. Our logarithmic conversion functions take numbers, so we have to convert the attribute string to a number with string->number.

Wire the action callbacks of the buttons up to functions called decrease-octave and increase-octave. An octave is "the interval between one musical pitch and another with double its frequency."

; Set frequency slider and display
(define (set-frequency freq) (when (and (>= freq *min-frequency*) (<= freq *max-frequency*)) (attribute-set! slider value: (frequency->position freq)) (attribute-set! frequency-field value: freq)))
; Buttons increase and decrease frequency by one octave
(define (adjust-octave modifier) (set-frequency (* (string->number (attribute frequency-field spinvalue:)) modifier)))
(define (decrease-octave self) (adjust-octave 0.5))
(define (increase-octave self) (adjust-octave 2))

Let's use another spin box to specify the duration of the beep in milliseconds.:

(define duration-field (textbox spin: 'Yes spinmin: 1 spinmax: 600000 ; 10 minutes spinvalue: 200))
(define general-controls (hbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 25 margin: '0x0 (hbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 5 duration-field (label "ms")))) ; Main window
(define dlg (dialog title: "Bleep" (vbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 25 margin: '25x25 slider frequency-controls general-controls)))

Frequency is rather abstract. Let's also give the user the ability to select a musical note. We can store the corresponding frequencies for A4-G4 in an association list.

; Notes -> frequency (middle A-G [A4-G4])
; http://pages.mtu.edu/~suits/notefreqs.html
(define notes '(("A" 440.00) ("B" 493.88) ("C" 261.63) ("D" 293.66) ("E" 329.63) ("F" 349.23) ("G" 292.00)))

We'll give the user a drop-down menu. Whenever a note is selected from the drop-down menu, we'll look up the frequency in the association list and set it using the set-frequency helper function we created for the octave buttons.

(define general-controls (hbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 25 margin: '0x0 (hbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 5 duration-field (label "ms")) (hbox alignment: 'ACENTER gap: 5 (label "♪") (listbox #:1 "A" #:2 "B" #:3 "C" #:4 "D" #:5 "E" #:6 "F" #:7 "G" value: 1 dropdown: 'Yes action: (lambda (self text item state) (set-frequency (cadr (assoc text notes))))))))

Now, let's make some noise. There are Chicken Scheme bindings to the Allegro library. Allegro is a library primarily used by games for cross-platform graphics, input devices, and more. What we're interested in is the audio addon that can be used to generate a tone with a sine wave. You'll need to install the Allegro library. Make sure you also install the header files. In some Linux distros, these are split into a separate package (e.g. liballegro5-dev on Debian). Also, install the Allegro egg (chicken-install -sudo allegro). I added the following lines near the top to import the Allegro bindings (and the chicken memory module, which we'll also use).

(import (prefix allegro "al:"))
(import (chicken memory)) (define +pi+ 3.141592)

You must place the code to initialize Allegro between the function to show the main IUP dialog and starting the IUP main-loop. If it comes before show, the program will die with a segmentation violation, and it can't come after the main-loop, because it would never get executed until the window closed.

; Display GUI
(show dlg) ; Initialize Allegro and audio addon
; Must be initialized afer showing the main dialog but before starting the main
; loop or else program will die with a segmentation violation.
(unless (al:init) (print "Could not initialize Allegro."))
(unless (al:audio-addon-install) (print "Could not initialize sound."))
(al:reserve-samples 0) ; Start IUP main loop
(main-loop)
(destroy! dlg)
(exit 0)

The Allegro egg is accompanied by a couple of examples but no examples showing the use of the audio addon. The Allegro library itself comes with an example showing how to generate a saw wave, but being a C library, the example is, of course, in C. I ported that example to Scheme. I would have contributed the example back to the Allegro egg, but the repo is marked as "archived by the owner" and read-only on GitHub. I've included the example in the repo alongside the rest of the code for this tutorial in case someone finds it useful.

Allegro is very low-level. You create an audio stream. In this case, the stream buffers eight fragments of 1,024 samples each at a frequency (often called sampling rate) of 44,100 Hz (the sampling rate of an audio CD), which means there are 44,100 samples per second. Each sample is a 32-bit float (what is called the bit depth of the audio), and we only have one channel to keep things as simple as possible.

; Generate a tone using Allegro
(define (generate-tone frequency duration) (let* ((samples-per-buffer 1024) (stream-frequency 44100) (amplitude 0.5) (stream (al:make-audio-stream 8 samples-per-buffer stream-frequency 'float32 'one)) (queue (al:make-event-queue)) (event (al:make-event))) (unless (al:audio-stream-attach-to-mixer! stream (al:default-mixer)) (print "Could not attach stream to mixer.")) (al:event-queue-register-source! queue (al:audio-stream-event-source stream)) (let event-loop ((n 0)) ; Grab and handle events (when (and (< n (/ (* (/ duration 1000) stream-frequency) samples-per-buffer)) (al:event-queue-wait! queue event)) (case (al:event-type event) ('audio-stream-fragment (let ((buffer (al:audio-stream-fragment stream))) ; If the stream is not ready for new data, buffer will be null. (if (not buffer) (event-loop n) (begin (fill-buffer buffer n) ; Placeholder ; Repeat (event-loop (+ n 1))))))))) (al:audio-stream-drain stream)))

An event loop waits for the audio stream to ask for another buffer. Our job is to fill that buffer with 1,024 32-bit floats at a time. In the code listing above, this is done by fill-buffer. That was just a placeholder, so I could break the code up into shorter, more easily explainable chunks. This is what goes in the place of (fill-buffer buffer n):

(let ((adr (pointer->address buffer))) (let loop ((i 0)) (when (< i samples-per-buffer) (let ((time (/ (+ (* samples-per-buffer n) i) stream-frequency))) ; al:audio-stream-fragment returns a C pointer. Use (chicken ; memory) module to operate on foreign pointer objects. ; Iterate over array four bytes at a time since 32-bit depth. (pointer-f32-set! (address->pointer (+ adr (* i 4))) (* amplitude (sin (* 2 +pi+ frequency time)))) (loop (+ i 1))))) (unless (al:audio-stream-fragment-set! stream buffer) (print "Error setting stream fragment")))

The Allegro egg is a pretty thin wrapper of the Allegro library. The audio-stream-fragment procedure in the egg just passes along the C pointer that the corresponding al_get_audio_stream_fragment function from the C library returns. It would have been nice if the egg had offered some Scheme conveniences atop Allegro like allowing us to pass a Scheme list or array to Allegro to provide the buffer of samples. Since it doesn't, we'll use the chicken memory module to fill the C array starting at the C pointer returned by audio-stream-fragment. We use pointer->address to get the address of the pointer. A pointer references a byte of memory. We can reference the preceding or following byte by subtracting or adding 1 to the address. Since we are filling the array with 32-bit floats, and 32 bits is 4 bytes, we want to increment the address by 4 each time. Then we can set the value of the current location with pointer-f32-set!.

Then you just need to feed Allegro buffers of 1,024 samples at a time. The basic formula for a sine wave is A sin(2πft) where A is amplitude, f is frequency, and t is time.

(* amplitude (sin (* 2 +pi+ frequency time)))

Wire this up to a play button, and you're ready to make some noise.

(button title: "Play" action: (lambda (self) (generate-tone (attribute frequency-field spinvalue:) (attribute duration-field spinvalue:))))

The documentation for the iup egg is pretty sparse, but this tutorial on using the iup egg was indispensable in writing the above tutorial.

You can check out the entire example on GitHub. This started as a personal learning project to explore the state of GUI programming in Lisp and has become a series of tutorials on building GUIs with various dialects of Lisp.
Items.Author: goober99

Items.Title: 在 Go 中连接到数据库 | Connecting to Database in Go
Items.Description:
欢迎回到Golang Web 开发的简单介绍。 上次，我们学习了如何使用gorilla/mux 。 这次，我们将学习如何设置数据库并连接到它。 让我们开始吧！

从为什么开始

了解我们为什么需要数据库很重要。 大多数 Web 应用程序都需要数据库连接，因为需要一种方法来可靠地保存数据。

我们可以创建一个结构并将数据存储在那里。 像这样的东西：

 type BookData struct { title string author string isbn string } books := [] BookData { { "Tools of Titans" , "Tim Ferriss" , "978-13-28683-78-6" }, { "Siddhartha" , "Herman Hesse" , "978-1529024043" }, }

当然，这可能会存储书籍数据，但在我们的 RAM 中。 这是一个坏主意，因为内存存储是易失的。 当您关闭数据库时，存储在 RAM 中的数据将被销毁。

想象一下去法国旅行。 您可以参观埃菲尔铁塔，在最好的餐厅享用高级美食，并结交新朋友。 将其存储到 RAM 将试图在您的脑海中记住所有这些珍贵的记忆。 使用数据库就像拍照并将它们保存到您的计算机。

对于我们的游戏玩家朋友（比如我！），想象一下每次服务器维护时您的帐户数据都会重置。 哎呀。

使用适合存储数据的工具是个好主意。 如果你想使用 NoSQL 数据库，你很可能需要第三方包。 对于本教程，我们将使用 PostgreSQL。

创建数据库

创建数据库的简单方法是使用 Docker 映像。 容器化服务允许它们在隔离的环境中运行，使其易于运行、停止和删除。

首先，让我们安装 Docker。 我强烈建议您按照本教程使用简单的 Docker 命令来动手操作。 本教程将安装 Docker，并帮助您了解 Docker 是什么以及它是如何工作的。

完成本教程后，我们就可以开始处理我们的容器了。 创建一个名为docker-compose.yml的文件并像这样编辑它：

 version: "3.7" services: database: container_name: database image: postgres ports: - "5432:5432" volumes: - ./database:/var/lib/postgresql/data environment: - POSTGRES_DB=bookstoreDB - POSTGRES_USER=jacob - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=password

上面的文件做了一些事情：

  • 它从 Docker Hub 中提取postgres映像。

  • 容器打开 5432 端口供我们连接。

  • 它设置连接到数据库时要使用的环境变量。

  • 对于本教程，我使用jacob和password 。

  • 由于体积，数据在/var/lib/postgresql/data的容器内持久存在。

我们可以使用以下命令运行它：

我们可以使用以下命令来阻止它：

容器运行后，我们可以使用名为 pgAdmin 的工具连接到数据库。 继续并在此处为您的操作系统安装 pgAdmin。 安装后，打开应用程序。 在左侧，右键单击Servers ，然后转到Create > Server 。

  • 在“常规”选项卡中，随意命名。 转到连接选项卡。

  • 主机名/地址是localhost 。

  • 端口是5432 。

  • 维护数据库是bookstoreDB 。

  • 用户名和密码是您在docker-compose.yml文件中设置的。

保存后，pgAdmin 将创建一个新连接。 一旦我们下拉到bookstoreDB ，我们可以单击顶部工具栏上的Tools > Query Tool 。 这将打开一个窗口，我们可以在其中键入和执行 SQL 查询。

好的，设置过程很长，但是之后我们不必经常触摸它。 让我们继续 Go 代码。

休斯顿，你抄吗？

我们知道为什么我们需要一个数据库并创建了一个 PostgreSQL 实例。 现在我们需要从我们的 Go 程序建立一个连接。

 // main.go package main import ( "database/sql" "fmt" "log" "github.com/gorilla/mux" _ "github.com/lib/pq" ) var DB * sql . DB const ( HOST = "localhost" PORT = 5432 USER = "jacob" PASSWORD = "password" DBNAME = "bookstoreDB" ) func main () { connString := fmt . Sprintf ( "host=%s port=%d user=%s password=%s dbname=%s sslmode=disable" , HOST , PORT , USER , PASSWORD , DBNAME , ) DB , err := sql . Open ( "postgres" , connString ) if err != nil { log . Fatal ( err ) } defer DB . Close () // mux definition and route registration (from last tutorial) r := mux . NewRouter () r . HandleFunc ( "/" , homeHandler ) booksSubR := r . PathPrefix ( "/books" ) . Subrouter () booksSubR . HandleFunc ( "/all" , AllHandler ) . Methods ( http . MethodGet ) booksSubR . HandleFunc ( "/{isbn}" , IspnHandler ) . Methods ( http . MethodGet ) booksSubR . HandleFunc ( "/new" , NewHandler ) . Methods ( http . MethodPost ) booksSubR . HandleFunc ( "/update" , UpdateHandler ) . Methods ( http . MethodPut ) booksSubR . HandleFunc ( "/delete/{isbn}" , DeleteIspnHandler ) . Methods ( http . MethodDelete ) log . Fatal ( http . ListenAndServe ( ":8090" , r )) }

这是一种非常简单、易于理解的连接方式。

 import ( "database/sql" "fmt" "log" "github.com/gorilla/mux" _ "github.com/lib/pq" )

最后一个 import 语句看起来很可疑。 为什么开头有下划线？ 另外，既然 Go 已经提供了database/sql包，为什么还要下载这个包呢？ 这些都是很好的问题！

  • 它不包括所有数据库的驱动程序。 包开发人员认为将驱动程序与database/sql包分开会更有效。 把它想象成一个模块化的旅行适配器，电源砖保持不变，但插脚是可更换的。

  • 这个包有点不同，在我们使用这个驱动程序的代码中没有任何地方。 因此，我们在前面加上一个下划线，表示该驱动程序正在被使用，但没有被用户主动调用。

确保运行go mod tidy以更新依赖项列表。

  • 我们使用var DB *sql.DB将连接数据存储为全局变量。

  • 数据库表示为指向结构sql.DB的指针，因为 DB 本身是一个大结构。 取它的指针效率更高。

如果你不知道指针是什么，它是一个存储对象内存地址的变量。

  • 诸如具有多个属性的大型结构之类的对象会占用大量空间。 传递它很麻烦，因为程序每次都需要复制大量数据。

  • 如果我们改用指针，我们只是传递对对象的引用，这要轻得多。

  • 当我们组织文件时，我们倾向于创建某些文件夹的快捷方式，对吗？ 我们不必将文件复制到我们的桌面。 我们只需要创建一个快捷方式，它可以节省大量宝贵的千兆字节。

总有一天我会写一篇关于指针的指南，让更多的人得到帮助。

 const ( HOST = "localhost" PORT = 5432 USER = "jacob" PASSWORD = "password" DBNAME = "bookstoreDB" ) func main () { connString := fmt . Sprintf ( "host=%s port=%d user=%s password=%s dbname=%s sslmode=disable" , HOST , PORT , USER , PASSWORD , DBNAME , ) DB , err := sql . Open ( "postgres" , connString ) if err != nil { log . Fatal ( err ) } defer DB . Close () }

  • 要连接到我们的数据库，我们需要定义主机、端口、用户名、密码、dbname 和 SSL 模式。 这些不会改变，因此最好将它们声明为常量。

  • 这些值遵循我们在docker-compse.yml文件中定义的环境变量。

  • 它的返回值存储在我们的DB变量中。

  • 我们需要在应用未运行时关闭连接，因此我们使用defer关键字在应用停止之前关闭连接。

使用全局变量适用于像这样的简单应用程序，但不建议在生产环境中使用，因为全局变量对变异有多么不安全。 简而言之，我们无法控制全局变量会发生什么。 随着您的继续，依赖注入将被更频繁地使用。 但是，这个概念超出了今天教程的范围。

结论

感谢您的阅读！ 我希望今天涵盖查询，但这会使教程太长。 此外，引入 Docker 和 PostgreSQL 等新工具已经让人难以接受。 下一个教程将是关于查询数据库的，敬请期待！

您也可以在Medium和我的个人网站上阅读这篇文章。



原文:

Welcome back to A Gentle Intro to Golang Web Development. Last time, we learned how to use gorilla/mux. This time, we will learn how to set up a database and connect to it. Let's get started!

Start with why

It's important to understand why we need a database. A database connection is necessary for most web apps because there needs to be a way to persist data reliably.

We could create a struct and store the data there. Something like this:

type BookData struct { title string author string isbn string
} books := []BookData{ {"Tools of Titans", "Tim Ferriss", "978-13-28683-78-6"}, {"Siddhartha", "Herman Hesse", "978-1529024043"},
}

Sure, this might store the book data, but in our RAM. This is a bad idea because in-memory storage is volatile. The data stored in RAM will be destroyed when you turn off your database.

Imagine going on a trip to France. You see the Eiffel Tower, eat haute-cuisine from the best restaurants, and make new friends. Storing it to RAM would be trying to remember all these cherished memories in your mind. Using a database is like taking pictures and saving them to your computer.

For our gamer friends (like me!), imagine your account data resetting every time there is server maintenance. Yikes.

It's a good idea to use tools suited for storing data. database/sql package provides support for SQL databases. If you want to use NoSQL databases, you will most likely require third-party packages. For this tutorial, we will use PostgreSQL.

Create the database

The easy way to create a database is to use a Docker image. Docker is a tool that lets us run services inside a container. Containerizing services allows them to run in an isolated environment, making it easy to run, stop and delete.

First, let's install Docker. I highly recommend you follow this tutorial to get your hands dirty with simple Docker commands. This tutorial will get Docker installed, and help you understand what Docker is and how it works.

Once you're done with the tutorial, we can start working on our containers. Create a file named docker-compose.yml and edit it like this:

version: "3.7"

services:
  database:
    container_name: database
    image: postgres
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - ./database:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_DB=bookstoreDB
      - POSTGRES_USER=jacob
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=password

The file above does a few things:

  • It pulls the postgres image from the Docker Hub.

  • The container opens port 5432 for us to connect.

  • It sets the environment variables to use when connecting to the database.

  • POSTGRES_USER and POSTGRES_PASSWORD can be anything. For this tutorial, I'm using jacob and password.

  • Thanks to volume, data persists inside the container at /var/lib/postgresql/data.

We can run this by using the following command:

We can stop this by using the following command:

Once the container is running, we can connect to the database by using a tool called pgAdmin. Go ahead and install pgAdmin for your OS here. Once installed, open the app. To the left, right-click on Servers, and go to Create > Server.

  • In the General tab, name this whatever you want. Go to the Connection tab.

  • The hostname/address is localhost.

  • Port is 5432.

  • The maintenance database is bookstoreDB.

  • Username and password are what you set in the docker-compose.yml file.

Once you save, pgAdmin will create a new connection. Once we drop down to bookstoreDB, we can click on Tools > Query Tool at the top toolbar. This opens a window where we can type and execute SQL queries.

Ok, the setup process was long, but we don't have to touch it often afterwards. Let's move on to the Go code.

Houston, do you copy?

We know why we need a database and have created an instance of PostgreSQL. Now we need to make a connection from our Go program.

// main.go
package main import ( "database/sql" "fmt" "log" "github.com/gorilla/mux" _"github.com/lib/pq"
) var DB *sql.DB const ( HOST = "localhost" PORT = 5432 USER = "jacob" PASSWORD = "password" DBNAME = "bookstoreDB"
) func main() { connString := fmt.Sprintf( "host=%s port=%d user=%s password=%s dbname=%s sslmode=disable", HOST, PORT, USER, PASSWORD, DBNAME, ) DB, err := sql.Open("postgres", connString) if err != nil { log.Fatal(err) } defer DB.Close() // mux definition and route registration (from last tutorial) r := mux.NewRouter() r.HandleFunc("/", homeHandler) booksSubR := r.PathPrefix("/books").Subrouter() booksSubR.HandleFunc("/all", AllHandler).Methods(http.MethodGet) booksSubR.HandleFunc("/{isbn}", IspnHandler).Methods(http.MethodGet) booksSubR.HandleFunc("/new", NewHandler).Methods(http.MethodPost) booksSubR.HandleFunc("/update", UpdateHandler).Methods(http.MethodPut) booksSubR.HandleFunc("/delete/{isbn}", DeleteIspnHandler).Methods(http.MethodDelete) log.Fatal(http.ListenAndServe(":8090", r))
}

This is a very simple, easy-to-understand way to create a connection.

import ( "database/sql" "fmt" "log" "github.com/gorilla/mux" _"github.com/lib/pq" )

That last import statement looks suspicious. Why is there an underscore at the beginning? Also, why even download this package when Go already provides the database/sql package? These are all great questions!

  • database/sql is designed to support many SQL and SQL-like databases. It does not include drivers for all the databases out there. The package developers thought that it is more efficient to separate the drivers from the database/sql package. Think of it as a modular travel adapter where the power brick stays the same, but the prongs are swappable.

  • github.com/lib/pq is the driver for PostgreSQL. This package is a little different in that there is nowhere in the code where we use this driver. Therefore, we put an underscore at the front to denote that the driver is being used but not actively called by the user.

Make sure to run go mod tidy to update the dependencies list.

  • We store connection data as a global variable using var DB *sql.DB.

  • The database is represented as a pointer to the struct sql.DB, because DB itself is a large struct. It is more efficient to take the pointer of it.

If you don't know what a pointer is, it's a variable that stores the memory address of an object.

  • An object such as a large struct with multiple attributes takes up a lot of space. Passing it around is cumbersome, because the program needs to copy a lot of data every time.

  • If we use a pointer instead, we are just passing around a reference to the object, which is a lot lighter.

  • We tend to create shortcuts to certain folders when we organize our files, right? We don't have to copy the file to our desktop. We just have to create a shortcut, which saves a lot of precious gigabytes.

I will write a guide on pointers some day, so that more people can get help.

const ( HOST = "localhost" PORT = 5432 USER = "jacob" PASSWORD = "password" DBNAME = "bookstoreDB"
) func main() { connString := fmt.Sprintf( "host=%s port=%d user=%s password=%s dbname=%s sslmode=disable", HOST, PORT, USER, PASSWORD, DBNAME, ) DB, err := sql.Open("postgres", connString) if err != nil { log.Fatal(err) } defer DB.Close()
}

  • To connect to our database, we need to define the host, port, username, password, dbname, and SSL mode. These won't change, so it's a good idea to declare them as constants.

  • The values follow the environment variables we defined in our docker-compse.yml file.

  • connString is a string that holds all these variables, passed to sql.Open() as an option.

  • sql.Open() connects our program to the database. Its return value is stored in our DB variable.

  • We need to close our connection when the app is not running, so we use the defer keyword to close the connection right before the app stops.

Using global variables works for simple apps like this, but it is not recommended in production because of how unsafe global variables are to mutation. Simply put, we have no control over what happens to the global variable. As you move on, dependency injection will be used more often. However, this concept is out of the scope of today's tutorial.

Conclusion

Thank you for reading! I hoped to cover querying today, but that would make the tutorial too long. Plus, introducing new tools such as Docker and PostgreSQL is already lot to swallow. The next tutorial will be all about querying the database, so stay tuned!

You can also read this post on Medium and my personal site.
Items.Author: jpoly1219

Items.Title: 使用 Sagemaker SDK 进行 REST 端点推理的不同方法 | Different ways of making REST Endpoint inference with Sagemaker SDK
Items.Description:
在本文中，我们将看到两种使用 Sagemaker SDK 从机器学习模型端点进行推理的方法。 机器学习模型在“亚马逊客户评论数据集”上进行了训练，可以预测 1-5 级的客户评论评级。 作为迁移学习的一部分，我使用了通过 DistilBERT 进行预训练的 BERT 模型，使用了提供双向注意力的 Hugging face 转换器和 TensorFlow。 有关模型实现的详细信息如下所示。

推理脚本的第一步是包含样板详细信息，例如会话、存储桶、角色、区域、账户 ID 和 Sagemaker 对象。

第二步是包括在模型部署阶段完成的细节。 这将包括训练作业名称、模型名称和端点详细信息。

使用部署阶段详细信息，需要创建一个预测器对象，其中包括会话详细信息和端点名称。 我们使用 JSONserializer 和 JSONdeserializer 来格式化输入，解释它，然后将其反序列化为对象类型。

第 1 类：实时/临时预测
创建此预测器后，它可用于通过提供输入作为特征来进行临时预测，如下所示。 此处函数 predictor.predict() 用于使用 python 对象对 Sagemaker 端点进行实时预测。 此处展示了四个亚马逊评论示例。

类别 2：批量预测
评论存储在 .tsv 文件（制表符分隔值）中，可以一次性处理。 根据所使用的计算，预测了 102,084 条评论中的 1000 条样本评论，持续时间为 5 分 45 秒。 通过在 df_sample_reviews.head() 调用中提供 10 的参数，我们可以检索小数字并验证预测。 作为输入的列是“review body”，预测与“star rating”进行比较。

也可以使用以下 HTTP 请求/响应语法直接调用预测模型作为 SageMaker 端点。 这种方法主要用于将模型部署为生产环境中的微服务时。

参考：



原文:

In this article we are going to see two ways of making inference from a machine learning model endpoint using Sagemaker SDK. The machine learning model is trained on 'Amazon Customer Reviews Dataset' and can predict customer review rating on a scale of 1-5. As a part of transfer learning, I have used BERT model with pre-training carried out through DistilBERT, using Hugging face transformer, which provides bi-directional attention, and TensorFlow. Details regarding model implementation are shown below.

The first step for the inference script is to include the boiler plate details like session, bucket, role, region, account ID and Sagemaker object.

The second step is to include the details completed during the model deployment stage. This will include the training job name, model name, and endpoint details.

Using the deployment stage details, a predictor object needs to be created which includes session details and the endpoint name. We are using JSONserializer and JSONdeserializer to format the input, interpret it and then deserialize it into an object type.

Category 1: Real-time/Ad-hoc predictions
Once this predictor is created, it can be used to make ad-hoc predictions by providing inputs as features, shown below. Here function predictor.predict() is being used to make real-time predictions against Sagemaker endpoints with python objects. Four examples of Amazon reviews have been shown here.

Category 2: Batch predictions
The reviews are stored in a .tsv file (tab separated value) and can be processed all at once. Predictions of 1000 sample reviews out of 102,084 reviews, were made which had a duration of 5 mins 45 seconds based on the compute being used. By providing the argument of 10, on the df_sample_reviews.head() call, we can retrieve the small number and validate the predictions. The column which serves as an input is 'review body' and predictions are compared against 'star rating'.

Predictions can also be invoked model as a SageMaker Endpoint directly using the following HTTP request/response syntax. This approach is primarily being used when models are deployed as microservices in a production environment.

Items.Title: 试验 API 微服务 | Experimenting with API microservices
Items.Description:
就几乎所有学科的知识而言，维基百科都是一块巨石。 在我最近的项目中，用户的位置是通过 Google Maps 使用Free Geo IP根据他们的 IP 地址显示的。

由于我们已经能够确定用户所在的城市和州，因此该项目已扩展为包含wikipedia-query source 。 这个 Web 组件调用 Wikipedia API，接收返回的 JSON 数据，并能够显示有关用户所在城市或州的信息。

安装/设置

  1. 导入项目： import '@lrnwebcomponents/wikipedia-query';

位置的使用

//finding wiki based on city and state <wikipedia-query search="${this.city}, ${this.state}"></wikipedia-query> //finding wiki based on city <wikipedia-query search="${this.city}"></wikipedia-query> //finding wiki based on state <wikipedia-query search="${this.state}"></wikipedia-query>

请访问wikipedia-query 文档和Wikipedia 官方 API 文档



原文:

Wikipedia is a monolith in terms of knowledge of almost any subject. In my recent project, a user's location is displayed via Google Maps based off their IP address using Free Geo IP.

Since we are able to determine a user's city and state already, the project has expanded to incorporate wikipedia-query source. This web-component calls the Wikipedia API, receives JSON data back and is able to display information about a user's city or state.

Install/Setup

  1. Wikipedia-query needs to be added as a dependency in package.json.
  2. Import into the project: import '@lrnwebcomponents/wikipedia-query';

Usage of location 

//finding wiki based on city and state
<wikipedia-query search="${this.city}, ${this.state}"></wikipedia-query>

//finding wiki based on city
<wikipedia-query search="${this.city}"></wikipedia-query>

//finding wiki based on state
<wikipedia-query search="${this.state}"></wikipedia-query>

Wikipedia-query can display more than location information and can be included in your project today. Please visit wikipedia-query documentation and Wikipedia Offical API documentation
Items.Author: mnmartinelli

Items.Title: 编程就像蒲公英 | Programing is Like a Dandelion
Items.Description:
你可能在想，猎户座，你到底在说什么？

不过听我说！ 作为一个新的开发者，但一个非常熟练的觅食者和植物书呆子，我通过在这两个截然不同的领域的经验建立了一些有趣的联系。

你可能认出这种植物是蒲公英，但不是因为它是这篇文章的主题。 这是因为你见过它们，听说过它们，也许是把它们从你的草坪或花园里拉出来的。 你的大脑知道蒲公英的图案，从深裂的叶子到阳光明媚的黄色花朵，再到蓬松的种子头。 所有这些小特征结合在一起“制造”了蒲公英。

模式是那里的关键词，也是这篇文章背后的整个想法。

模式识别

我们的大脑是为模式识别而生的。 大多数时候，这个过程是自动无意识地发生的。 例如，当您看到蒲公英时，您的大脑会很快处理图像的每个部分：

  • 细长的茎上开着黄色的花。
  • 露齿的、裂的叶子低到地面。
  • 在蒲公英最爱的市区。 你可能不知道，但你经常在城市环境中看到它们，所以这种模式存在于你的大脑中！

不停地，几乎以瞬间的速度完成。 每天，这种内置能力都在帮助您做出决定、度过一天、避免危险、学习新信息以及无数其他事情。

我作为觅食者和植物书呆子的背景和能力 100% 依赖于模式识别。 我知道哪些植物是可食用的，我知道如何从它们潜在的有毒外观中辨别它们，我知道一种蘑菇和另一种相似的蘑菇，我知道一棵云杉树和一棵枞树，这一切都是因为我知道它们的模式。 我提高了能力，因为我意识到模式识别在我们学习中的作用，并且因为我一生中的大部分时间都在用它来研究自然。 我已经看到一些相同的能力转移到学习编程上。

我注意到我可以挑选出构成编程语言不同方面的模式，理解它们存在的原因，并看到相似概念中的细微差别。 与大多数事情一样，学习和编写代码都是关于模式的。 在 JavaScript 中，您知道箭头函数是因为您已经了解了它的模式。 由于它们的差异，您从 forEach 中知道 for 循环。 那就是模式识别！ 而且，您可以变得更好，这可以帮助您更有效地学习。

如何提高你的模式识别

第一步只是意识到它的存在。 你的大脑几乎一直在经历这个例行功能。 一旦你意识到了，你就可以开始关注事物的模式并理解它们为什么会这样。 寻找并尝试将模式以某种方式概念化，而不是让你的大脑无意识地将模式与事物联系起来。 这确实需要练习，你们中的一些人会比其他人做得更好。

上面链接的文章还提到，玩游戏，无论是物理游戏还是电脑游戏，也有帮助，因为当你分解游戏时，它们都是模式！ 我也是一名游戏玩家（视频和棋盘），并且绝对注意到我的模式识别有助于在与朋友竞争时给我一点优势！

不过，也许更有趣的是，这篇文章提到了一些神经递质（多巴胺、去甲肾上腺素和 anandamide）与模式识别之间的潜在联系。 它继续说，这些是与“流动状态”等相关的神经递质。 我知道，当我在写作、编码、阅读等方面进行了一次非常好的、迷失时间的会话时，我对自己所取得的成就感觉很好，并且我对这些材料的记忆力更好。

您越喜欢并被某个工作主题所吸引，您就会越专注于它，您就越能将其模式记忆并理解它们！

您是否知道模式识别在我们日常生活中的作用？ 我觉得很迷人！

如果你做到了这一步，感谢你阅读我关于植物和编程之间联系的奇怪的小思考。 这是我关于 Dev 的第一篇文章，我很想知道你的想法！ 我经常写关于觅食的文章，但这是我第一次接触科技相关的东西！

猎户座



原文:

You might be thinking, Orion, what the heck are you talking about?

Hear me out though! As a new dev, but a very practiced forager and plant nerd I've made some interesting connections through experience with these two very different fields.

You probably recognized this plant as a dandelion, but not because it's the subject of this post. It's because you've seen them, heard about them, maybe pulled them from your lawn or garden. Your brain knows the pattern of a dandelion, from its deeply lobed leaves to its sunny yellow flowers and later its fluffy seed head. All these little traits come together to 'make' a dandelion.

Pattern is the keyword there, and the whole idea behind this post.

Pattern Recognition

Our brains were built for pattern recognition. Most of the time this process is happening automatically and unconsciously. For example, when you saw that dandelion your brain very quickly processed each part of the image:

  • Yellow flower on a long skinny stalk.
  • Toothy, lobed leaves growing low to the ground.
  • In an urban area which is a favorite of dandelions. You may not know that, but you're regularly seeing them in urban environments, so that pattern exists in your brain!

On and on, and done at almost instant speed. Every day this built-in ability is helping you make decisions, move through the day, avoid dangers, learn new information, and a myriad of other things.

My background and abilities as a forager and plant nerd are 100% reliant on pattern recognition. I know which plants are edible, I know how to tell them from their potentially toxic look-a-likes, I know one mushroom species from another similar one, I know a spruce tree from a fir tree, all because I know their patterns. I have heightened abilities because I'm aware of the role of pattern recognition in our learning and because I've spent the majority of my life using it to study nature. I've seen some of the same abilities transfer over to learning programming.

I've noticed that I can pick out the patterns that make up different aspects of a programming language, understand why they're there, and see the small differences in similar concepts. Learning and writing code, as with most things, is all about patterns. In JavaScript, you know an arrow function because you've learned its pattern. You know a for loop from a forEach because of their differences. That's pattern recognition! And, you can become better at it which could help you learn a little more efficiently.

How to Improve Your Pattern Recognition

The first step is just to be aware of its existence. This routine function that your brain is going through nearly constantly. Once you're aware, you can start paying attention to the patterns of things and understanding why they're like that. Look for and try to conceptualize why the patterns are a certain way, instead of just letting your brain associate a pattern with a thing unconsciously. This does take practice, and some of you will be better at it than others.

The above-linked article also mentions that playing games, physical or computer, can help too because games are all patterns when you break them down! I'm also a gamer (video and board) and definitely notice that my pattern recognition helps give me a little edge when competing with my friends!

Maybe more interestingly though, the article mentions a potential link between some neurotransmitters (dopamine, norepinephrine, and anandamide) and pattern recognition. It goes on to say that these are the neurotransmitters associated with the 'flow state' among other things. I know when I have a really good, lose-track-of-time session of writing, coding, reading, whatever, I feel great about what I accomplished and I have a better recall of that material.

The more you enjoy and are enthralled by a topic of work, the most focus you'll have on it, and the better you'll commit its patterns to memory and understand them!

Were you aware of the role pattern recognition has on our day-to-day lives? I think it's fascinating!

If you made it this far, thank you for reading my weird little musing on connections between plants and programming. This is my first article on Dev and I would love to know what you think! I write regularly about foraging, but this is my first shot at anything tech-related!

Orion
Items.Author: orionaon

Items.Title: 使用 Imgur API 上传美杜莎图片 | Medusa images uploading using Imgur API
Items.Description:
但默认情况下，它不提供任何形式的免费在线空间来存储产品图片。 官方文档描述了如何使用digitalOcean和AWS S3来放置您上传的产品图片，但这些服务是付费使用的。

最终，我找到了一种使用Imgur API 完全免费存储图像的方法。 以下是您可以执行的步骤。

 const headers = { "Content-Type": "multipart/form-data", Authorization: "Client-ID " + IMGUR_CLIENT_ID, }
 export const imgurUploadPromise = (formData) => { const apiUrl = "https://api.imgur.com/3/image" return axios .post(apiUrl, formData, { headers, }) .then((res) => { console.log(res) return res.data.data.link }) .catch((err) => console.log(err)) }

确保响应格式的链接应该是res.data.data.link 。

  1. 使用 promise.all 一次操作上传多张图片。
 uploads: { create(files) { const promiseArray = [] for (const f of files) { const formData = new FormData() formData.append("image", f) promiseArray.push(imgurUploadPromise(formData)) } return Promise.all(promiseArray).then((values) => { const uploads = values.map((value) => { return { url: value } }) return { data: { uploads, }, } }) },

请记住，每个 promise 的返回数据格式必须为{ url: value } ，并且所有的返回数据格式必须为{ uploads } 。

然后它将成功完成。 (don't upload images each over than 5MB ) （请勿上传超过5MB的图片）



原文:

Medusa is very powerful, useful, open-source and easily-revised e-commerce project.

But in default, it doesn't provide any kind free online space to store products' pictures. The offical documents describe how to use digitalOcean and AWS S3 to put your uploaded images of products but those services are used with paid.

Eventually, I found one way to use Imgur API to store our images for free totally. Following are the steps you can go with.

const headers = {
  "Content-Type": "multipart/form-data",
  Authorization: "Client-ID " + IMGUR_CLIENT_ID,
}

export const imgurUploadPromise = (formData) => {
  const apiUrl = "https://api.imgur.com/3/image"
  return axios
    .post(apiUrl, formData, {
      headers,
    })
    .then((res) => {
      console.log(res)
      return res.data.data.link
    })
    .catch((err) => console.log(err))
}

Make sure the link of response format should be res.data.data.link.

  1. Using promise.all to upload multiple images in one operation. 
  uploads: {
    create(files) {
      const promiseArray = []
      for (const f of files) {
        const formData = new FormData()
        formData.append("image", f)
        promiseArray.push(imgurUploadPromise(formData))
      }

      return Promise.all(promiseArray).then((values) => {
        const uploads = values.map((value) => {
          return { url: value }
        })
        return {
          data: {
            uploads,
          },
        }
      })
    },

Remember the return data format must be { url: value } for each promise and return data: { uploads } for all.

Then it will be done successfully. (don't upload images each over than 5MB)

在本文中，我想分享我私下为创建新互联网所做的工作。 这听起来很有野心，这就是我公开开放我的项目的原因。

这就是 web 2.0当前的工作方式

新互联网需要什么？

  2. 一种采用“”的编程语言

这个搜索引擎，直到现在我已经用 go 和 rust 开发了，它的目的是通过 api 向区块链发出请求，以接收 web 的所有必要数据。 迄今为止（5/1/2022）它是部分有用的。

我现在在这里。 尝试设置一个参数，以便 html 中的必要引号在我们在区块链中看到的 msg 中可见。 在 rust 中不能这样做，因为这意味着语法错误。 一旦我有了语言，我就可以用浏览器组装它并向你展示最终结果

开源

区块链上的第一个 100% 网页。

马特奥·拉法尔塞

Items.Title: Svelte：带标签的语句 | Svelte: Labeled Statements
Items.Description:
艾莉亚克里希纳

发表于任何顶级语句（即不在块或函数内）都可以通过在其前面加上 $: JS 标签语法来进行反应。

在 vanilla JavaScript 中可以看到标签语句与循环以及 break 和 continue 关键字并附加在一起。

这里 name.toUpperCase 是一个内置的 JavaScript 方法，而 upperCaseName 是一个变量。

每当名称更改时，Svelte 都会为我们重新计算值。

阿斯米特马拉卡纳瓦尔 - May 1 5月1日



原文:

Arya Krishna

Posted on May 2

What is the $: in Svelte?

Any top-level statement (i.e. not inside a block or a function) can be made reactive by prefixing it with the $: JS label syntax.

Label statements can be seen in vanilla JavaScript in conjunction with loops and the break and continue keyword and attached.

What svelte does is it grabs this $: statement and we can add a dynamic calculation based on some other value.
$: uppercaseName = name.toUpperCase();

Here name.toUpperCase is a built in JavaScript Method and upperCaseName is a variable.

Svelte will recalculate the value for us whenever the name changes.

Muthu Annamalai Venkatachalam - May 1

Asmit Malakannawar - May 1

Items.Title: Axios 一些信息 | Axios some info
Items.Description:
发表于在服务器上它使用本地节点http模块，而在客户端它使用 XMLHttpRequest 系统。

使用您喜欢的任何包管理器将axios添加到您的依赖项列表并安装。

要发出 GET 请求，在发出请求的文件中需要一些样板。

 const axios = require ( ' axios ' ); axios . get ( ' /user ' , { params : { id : 1 , } }) . then ( response => { console . log ( response ); // do more with response }); . catch ( error => { console . error ( error ); // handle error });

可以在单个函数中对 GET 和 POST 请求使用单个样板：

 const request = ( var ) => { const options = { method : " VERB " , url : " API-ENDPOINT " , params : { ` ${ var } ` }, headers : { " API-HOST " : " HOST-NAME " , " API-KEY " : " KEY-VALUE " , }, }, axios . request ( options ) . then ( response => { // handle response }) . catch ( error => { // handle error }); };

Axios allows for easy client-side requests this way and will offer protection against Cross-site Request Forgery as well. Axios 允许以这种方式轻松进行客户端请求，并且还将提供针对跨站点请求伪造的保护。



原文:

Swislok-Dev

Posted on May 2

Axios is a promise base HTTP client for node.js. On the server it uses the native node http module while on the client side it uses XMLHttpRequest system.

Using whatever package manager you like add axios to your list of dependencies and install.

To make a GET request some boilerplate is required in the file the request is made.

const axios = require('axios'); axios.get('/user', { params: { id: 1, }
}) .then(response => { console.log(response); // do more with response
});
.catch(error => { console.error(error); // handle error
});

POST requests are made in a similar fashion:

axios.post('/user', { firstName: 'Bob', lastName: 'Ross'
})
.then(response => { // handle response
})
.catch(error => { // handle error
});

It is possible to use a single boilerplate for both GET and POST requests within a single function:

const request = (var) => { const options = { method: "VERB", url: "API-ENDPOINT", params: { `${var}` }, headers: { "API-HOST": "HOST-NAME", "API-KEY": "KEY-VALUE", }, }, axios.request(options) .then(response => { // handle response }) .catch(error => { // handle error });
};

Axios allows for easy client-side requests this way and will offer protection against Cross-site Request Forgery as well.
Items.Author: swislokdev

Items.Title: 如何准备 GCP 专业数据工程师认证 | How to prepare for the GCP Professional Data Engineer certification
Items.Description:
你好！

我最近通过了 GCP 专业数据工程师认证考试，有人问我考试技巧和学习资料，所以我决定写这篇文章来解释我所走的路。

这当然不是准备这次测试的唯一可能方法，但它对我有用。 所以要心胸开阔，因为每个人都有不同的学习方式。

期望对齐

与其他一些认证不同，专业数据工程师 (PDE) 绝对不是一个简单的考试，只需学习 8 小时，您就可以做好准备。 谷歌以一种只有具有实践经验并了解其服务的人才能理解的方式编写问题。

重要的是要注意，认证是对您获得的知识的验证。 目标不是让您记住问题，而是真正了解云提供的服务，以便能够将它们应用到您的日常工作中。 准备时请记住这一点。

对于此考试，Google 推荐以下背景：3 年以上的行业经验，包括 1 年以上使用 Google Cloud 设计和管理解决方案的经验。 在我参加考试的时候，我在数据工程领域有 3 年的经验，但我没有面向数据的云服务的背景。 重要的是，我在之前的工作中使用 AWS 6 个月时已经拥有云知识库，但它是在 devops 环境中而不是数据工程中。 无论哪种方式，我认为一个人在尝试像 GCP PDE 这样的特定考试之前具备云架构的基本知识很重要。 因此，对于 GCP 专业数据工程师考试，我需要 4 个月的密集学习才能对参加考试有最低限度的信心。

考试本身

在开始准备之前，值得探索官方网站。 它包含有关测试及其内容的所有最新信息。 值得一提的是，官网上的考试费用是200美元，但是因为我住在巴西，所以只付了120美元。 谷歌提供了一个名为考试指南的网站部分来解释考试内容。 所以在开始学习之前值得一读，当你觉得你已经学习了大量的材料时，也可以看看，看看你对要求的立场。

如果您的公司是 Google Cloud 合作伙伴，它很可能会为其员工提供优惠券。 这些代金券通常可供完成 Google 在 Qwiklabs 平台上提供的知识路径的任何人使用。

考试共有 50 道题，时长 2 小时。 我认为这段时间足以回答所有问题，甚至可以回顾那些留下的疑问。

以及关于测试的最后一条评论。 由于 COVID-19 大流行，可以在线参加考试。 虽然这种方式有点无聊，因为谷歌要求你有一个准备好的测试环境。 就个人而言，我更喜欢亲自到认可的中心进行考试，因为它消除了在家中可能出现的计算机和互联网问题的风险，并且可以让考试更加集中。

准备

有许多在线和面对面的课程可以帮助您为 GCP 专业数据工程师考试做好准备。 我会在这里注册我接触过的人，我可以证明质量。

云大师

这是我准备考试的主要课程。 这可能是云认证的主要学习平台。 本课程几乎涵盖了您通过考试所需的所有内容，并且非常详细。 虽然更昂贵，但该平台还包括实验室，用于练习课堂所教内容。

丹·沙利文的Udemy 课程

这是我参加的另一门课程，我真的很喜欢。 讲师可以以非常具有指导意义的方式在服务之间建立联系。 还值得一提的是，Udemy 一直在推广课程。 特别是我设法以 25 雷亚尔的价格购买了这个。 课程结构随着考试内容不断发展。

这是谷歌提供自己培训的平台。 作为 Google Cloud 合作伙伴的公司通常可以访问此平台。 我参加了数据工程轨道上的大部分课程，但我认为它们的质量一般。 这个平台最好的地方是有众多的实验室可供练习。 实践经验

正如我一开始提到的，在学习认证时，实践经验非常重要。 虽然 Google 需要一年的云工作才能通过考试，但您可以通过其他方式取得成功。 第一个也是最推荐的是在所谓的免费层中创建一个帐户。 此帐户将允许您使用 Google Cloud Platform 资源来练习您在课堂上学到的内容。 提到的所有课程都教如何创建此帐户。 但如果你想看看，你可以看看这个视频教程。

另一种实践方式是通过提供实验室的平台。 正如我在上一节中提到的，我使用的提供此功能的两个平台是 Cloud Guru 和 Qwiklabs，Qwiklabs 有更多的实验室可供练习。

我明确表示，我更喜欢创建免费套餐帐户的方法，因为它比使用现成的环境提供更完整的学习体验。

额外的学习资源

除了课程之外，其他资源对于通过考试也是必不可少的。

邓沙利文的专业数据工程师学习指南书

这本由 Dan Sullivan 撰写的优秀书籍（是的，与列出的第二门课程是同一作者）包含了通过考试所需的几乎所有信息。 它有关于 GCP 服务的组织良好的章节，并提供了许多要记住的关键点来解决考试。 在每个部分的末尾，都会对呈现的内容进行测试。 说实话，读完这本书后，我的知识提高了大约40-50%，而且我在模拟测试中的成绩也有了很大的提高。

她设法通过精美的插图来解释 GCP 的产品和服务。 这本书和频道都是很好的教学资源，在理解考试的重要概念方面帮助了我很多。

谷歌文档

我认为阅读每个 GCP 服务和产品的文档不仅对于测试非常重要，而且对于在日常工作中扮演 GCP 数据工程师的角色也非常重要。 当对 Google Cloud 服务有任何疑问时，我始终建议您查阅文档，也就是说，它是您从准备考试的最初时刻到结束都应该使用的资源。

数据工程师实践考试

准备过程中极其重要的一步是参加实践考试。 这是经典，越多越好。 查看哪些研究领域你做得不太正确，然后再次阅读相应的文档。 为许多业务场景问题做好准备，询问要使用哪种技术以及有多种选择的问题。 在我的考试中，我发现了几个问题来选择两个备选方案。 下面我列出了我在整个准备过程中使用的平台。 一个重要的细节是，在某些平台上可以暂停模拟，因此您不必一次完成所有 50 个问题。 尽管我建议您至少进行三门实践考试，而不会被打断。

我还观看了Sathish VJ在AwesomeGCP YouTube 频道上的视频。 这是一个很好的学习资源，因为除了提示和内容本身之外，它还有几个带有评论问题的视频。

最后提示

这些是我测试最多的主要服务，也就是说，它们有两个或多个问题：

  • 云 SQL。
  • 数据流。
  • 云存储（主要主题是存储类）。

其他服务出现特定问题，每个服务最多两个问题：

  • 云扳手
  • 作曲家
  • 计算引擎
  • 数据丢失防护
  • 数据准备
  • 火库

熟悉诸如 IAM 之类的云概念也非常重要，它与云中的资源许可有关。

结论

在这篇文章中，我介绍了准备 GCP 专业数据工程师考试的步骤。 正如开头所解释的，没有“灵丹妙药”可以在测试中取得成功。 有些事情对一个人来说比对另一个人更有效，我相信还有其他（更好的）方法来准备。

老实说，即使是非常密集地学习了 4 个月，在测试的第三个问题之后，我的感觉是我对所有事情都只了解一点，而且还不够通过，因为有几个问题涉及工具的小细节。 在测试的几个时刻，我确信我不会接受测试，在那些时刻，焦虑和绝望占据了我的心。 我浪费了至少 5 分钟的考试时间，什么都不看，并后悔自己没有做好答案。 但我努力保持冷静和专注。 在考试结束时，我收到了“通过”的结果。 得知所有的努力和牺牲都得到了回报，我感到非常高兴和宽慰。 如果您对准备工作有任何疑问，请随时给我留言。 我希望我能够向您传达有用的信息，并祝您考试好运！



原文:

Hi!

I recently passed the GCP Professional Data Engineer certification exam and some people asked me for tips for the exam and study materials, so I decided to write this post to explain the path I took.

This is certainly not the only possible way to prepare for this test, but it was the one that worked for me. So be open-minded as each person has different ways of studying and learning.

Expectations alignment

Unlike some other certifications, the Professional Data Engineer (PDE) is definitely not a simple exam, where just study for 8 hours and you'll be ready. Google writes its questions in a way that only someone with hands-on experience and an understanding of their services can get across.

It is important to note that a certification is the validation of the knowledge you gain. The goal is not for you to memorize questions, but to actually understand the services that the cloud offers to be able to apply them in your day to day work. Keep this in mind as you prepare.

For this exam, Google recommends the following background: 3+ years of industry experience including 1+ years designing and managing solutions using Google Cloud. At the time I took the test I had 3 years of experience in the field of data engineering but I had no background with data-oriented cloud services. It is important to say that I already had a knowledge base in Cloud as I used AWS for 6 months in my previous work, but it was in a devops context and not data engineering. Either way, I think it's important for a person to have basic knowledge of Cloud architecture before attempting a specific exam like the GCP PDE. Therefore, for the GCP Professional Data Engineer exam I needed 4 months of intense studies to feel minimally confident to take the exam.

The exam itself

Before starting your preparation, it is worth exploring the official website. It contains all up-to-date information about the test and its content. It is worth mentioning that on the official website the cost of the exam is 200 dollars, but because I live in Brazil, I only paid 120 dollars. Google provides a section of the site, called Exam Guide, to explain what is on the test. So it's worth reading before starting your study and also taking a look when you feel you've studied a considerable amount of material, to see where you stand on the requirements.

If your company is a Google Cloud partner, it is very likely that it has the benefit of vouchers for its employees. These vouchers are usually available to anyone who completes a knowledge path made available by Google on the Qwiklabs platform.

The exam has a total of 50 questions and is 2 hours long. I consider this time enough to answer all the questions and even review the ones that were left as doubts.

And the last comment about the test. Due to the COVID-19 pandemic, it is possible to take the exam online. Although this way is a bit boring because Google requires you to have a well-prepared environment for the test. Personally, I prefer to do it in person at an accredited center as it eliminates the risk of possible computer and internet problems that may occur at home as well as allowing a greater level of concentration for the test.

Preparation

There are many online and face-to-face courses that prepare you for the GCP Professional Data Engineer exam. I will register here the ones I had contact with and I can attest to the quality.

A Cloud Guru

This was the main course I used to prepare for the exam. This is probably the main study platform for Cloud certifications. This course pretty much covers everything you need to pass the exam and is very detailed. Although more expensive, this platform also includes labs to practice what is taught in class.

Udemy Course by Dan Sullivan

This was another course I took and I really enjoyed it. The instructor can make links between services in a very didactic way. It is also worth mentioning that Udemy always promotes courses. This one in particular I managed to buy for 25 reais. The course structure is constantly evolving along with the test content.

Qwiklabs

This is the platform where Google makes its own trainings available. Companies that are Google Cloud partners typically have access to this platform. I took most of the courses on the data engineering track but I consider them to be of average quality. The best thing about this platform are the numerous labs to practice. Cloud Guru also has labs but Qwiklabs has a lot more.

Practice experience

As I mentioned at the outset, practical experience is very important when studying for certification. While Google requires a year of cloud work to pass the exam, you can succeed in other ways. The first and most recommended is to create an account in the so-called Free Tier. This account will allow you to use Google Cloud Platform resources to practice what you learn in class. All the courses mentioned teach how to create this account. But if you want to check it out, you can take a look at this video tutorial.

The other way to practice is through platforms that offer labs. As I mentioned in the last section, the two platforms I've used that offer this feature are Cloud Guru and Qwiklabs, with Qwiklabs having a larger amount of labs to practice with.

I make it clear that I prefer the approach of creating a Free Tier account as it allows for a much more complete learning experience than taking a ready-to-use environment.

Extra study resources

In addition to the courses, other resources were essential for passing the exam.

Professional Data Engineer Study Guide Book by Dun Sullivan

This excellent book written by Dan Sullivan (Yes, the same author as the second course listed) contains almost all the information needed to pass the exam. It has well-organized chapters on GCP services and provides many key points to remember to solve the exam. At the end of each section, there are tests on the presented content. To tell you the truth, after reading this book, I was able to improve my knowledge by about 40-50%, and my results in the mock test improved significantly.

The Cloud Girl and Visualizing Google Cloud

The YouTube channel The Cloud Girl and the book Visualizing Google Cloud are produced by Priyanka Vergadia, a Google Cloud Developer Advocate. She manages to explain GCP's products and services through beautifully crafted illustrations. Both the book and the channel are great teaching resources and helped me a lot in understanding important concepts for the exam.

Google documentation

I consider reading the documentation of each GCP service and product very important not only for the test but also for playing the role of GCP Data Engineer in the day to day work. I always recommend consulting the documentation when any point of doubt about Google Cloud services arises, that is, it is a resource that you should use from the initial moment of preparation for the exam until the end.

Data Engineer Practice Exam

An extremely important step in your preparation is taking practical exams. This is the classic, the more the better. See which are the areas of study where you are getting less right and read the corresponding documentation again. Prepare yourself for many business scenario questions, asking which technology to use and for questions with multiple alternatives. In my exam, I found several questions to select two alternatives. Below I have listed the platforms I used throughout my preparation. An important detail is that on some of the platforms it is possible to pause the simulations, so you don't have to do all 50 questions at once. Although I recommend that you do at least three practical exams without interruptions.

I also watched the videos on the AwesomeGCP YouTube channel by Sathish VJ. It is an excellent source of study because in addition to tips and content itself, it also has several videos with commented questions.

Final tips

These are the main services that most fell on my test, that is, that had two or more questions about them:

  • ML concepts + AI at GCP (Vertex AI, Vision, NLP etc APIs).
  • BigQuery (some questions covering details about the tool like backup and SQL structures like window functions).
  • BigTable (the main detail is design of tables to avoid hot spots).
  • Cloud SQL.
  • Dataflow.
  • Dataproc (some issues addressing Dataproc + HDFS).
  • Pub/sub (especially used for scenarios where there needs to be decoupling between systems).
  • Cloud Storage (main topic was storage classes).

Other services appeared with specific questions, with a maximum of two questions each:

  • Cloud Spanner
  • Composer
  • Compute Engine
  • Data Loss Prevention
  • Dataprep
  • Firestore

It is also very important to be familiar with cloud concepts such as IAM, which pertains to permissioning resources in the Cloud.

Conclusion

In this post I presented the steps I followed to prepare for the GCP Professional Data Engineer exam. As explained at the beginning, there is no “silver bullet” to succeed in the test. Some things work better with one person than another, and I'm sure there are other (and better) ways to prepare.

Honestly, even studying very intensively for 4 months, after the third question on the test my feeling was that I only knew a little bit about everything and that it wouldn't be enough to pass, since several questions asked small details about the tools. For several moments in the test I was sure I wouldn't be tested and in those moments, anxiety and despair took over me. I wasted at least 5 minutes of exam time staring at nothing and regretting that I wasn't doing well with the answers. But I tried to stay calm and concentrate. At the end of the exam I received the result of "Pass". I was extremely happy and relieved to know that all the effort and sacrifices paid off. After 3 days Google finally sent me the result confirmation and the GCP Professional Data Engineer certificate.

If you have any questions about the preparation, feel free to message me. I hope I was able to convey useful information to you, and I wish you good luck in the exam!
Items.Author: gabrielosluz

Items.Title: 韦尔塞尔 | Vercel
Items.Description:
什么是 Vercel？ 它是一种云计算服务，可以免费使用，但需要付费才能获得高级会员资格。


您可以将其直接链接到 GitHub 以将其用于 git 项目。

一旦您选择并加载了一个项目，Vercel 就会为您完成剩下的工作并为您部署整个项目。



原文:

what is Vercel? Vercel is a programing tool that is used for building and deploying projects. it is a cloud computing service that can be used free but cost money for a premium membership.


You can link it directly to GitHub to use it for git projects.

once you have a project selected and loaded it Vercel does the rest and deploys the entirety of your project for you.
Items.Author: mct5330

Items.Title: 使用 Docker | Using Docker
Items.Description:
该工具非常强大，甚至允许两个容器从同一主机运行。

开始使用 docker 就像下载桌面应用程序或使用他们网站上的教程实验室一样简单。 下载应用程序后，您可以运行命令docker run -dp 80:80 docker/getting-started as shown below,然后在浏览器中转到本地主机。

您可以在 docker 桌面应用程序中查看最近的容器。

为了能够将 nasa 项目集成到 docker 中，我必须创建一个新实例，并且 docker 会自动为您创建文件设置。 要运行程序，您可以使用dockercompose up 。



原文:

Docker is a web container tool used for developing deploying, testing, and controlling containers. the tool is extremely powerful and even allows two containers to run from the same host.

Getting started with docker is as simple as downloading the desktop app, or playing with the tutorial labs on their website. Once the app is downloaded you can run the command docker run -dp 80:80 docker/getting-started as shown below, then go to your local host in your browser.

You can see the recent containers in the docker desktop app.

to be able to integrate the nasa project into docker I had to create a new instance and docker somewhat automatically creates the file setup for you. to run the program you can use dockercompose up.
Items.Author: mct5330

Items.Title: 第 21 - 40 天：#100DaysofCode | Days 21 - 40: #100DaysofCode
Items.Description:
我阅读并研究了许多方法来结合不同的颜色、排版、空白和形状，以正确地代表我脑海中想象的品牌。

然后，它在名片、小册子、PowerPoint 和其他想到的产品中保持一致性。 毕竟，当您创建应用程序或网站时，您需要生成的绝不是一个单一的实体。 和往常一样，我的脑子里一直在纠结下一步该做什么。

由于需要在产品之间保持一致性，同时在每个设计之间提供新鲜空气，我继续研究设计响应元素的方法。 看到这个品牌在我眼前栩栩如生，真是令人兴奋的几周。

遗憾的是，这个项目已经停止。 但尽管如此，通过我所做的所有研究，这是一个很好的学习机会。 我迫不及待地想把我学到的所有东西带到我投资的下一个项目中。

快乐编码！



原文:

This section of the #100daysofcode was more focused on UX/UI principles and color theory. I read and researched about many ways to incorporate different colors, typography, white space, and shapes in order to correctly represent the brand I imagined in my head.

Then it was maintaining consistency throughout products from business cards, pamphlets, PowerPoints, and others that came to mind. After all, when you're creating an application or website, it is never one single entity that you need to produce. My head was jumbled with what to do next, as always.

Since there needed to be a consistency between products while still offering a breathe of fresh air between each design, I continued to research ways to design responsive elements. It was a very exciting couple weeks seeing this brand come to life right before my eyes.

Sadly, this project has come to a halt. But nonetheless, it was a great learning opportunity through all the research I did. I can't wait to take everything I learned to the next project I invest in.

Happy coding!
Items.Author: tmchuynh

Items.Title: 在 OSX 中快速轻松地找到屏幕上任何颜色的 RGB 值 | Find the RGB value of any color on the screen in OSX quickly and easily
Items.Description:
我今天发现自己想从图像中检索颜色。 这通常涉及在某种图像编辑软件中打开图像并使用称为吸管的工具。 这是一个多步骤的过程，有点痛苦。

所以我想知道，有没有更简单的方法？ 事实证明，在 Apple 计算机上，有一个名为Digital Color Meter的内置工具，它包含在 OSX 的应用程序/实用程序中。

它跟踪您的光标并在一个小窗口上显示该像素的 RGB 值。 用于在尝试创建某些东西时模拟冷色！

您还可以使用组合键 ⇧ + ⌘ + C 轻松地将 RGB 复制为字符串



原文:

I found myself today wanting to retrieve a color from an image. This normally involves opening the image in some sort of image editing software and using a tool called the eyedropper. It's a multi-step process and kind of a pain.

So I wondered, is there an easier way? It turns out that on Apple computers there is a built in tool called Digital Color Meter that is included in the Apps/Utilities in OSX.

It tracks your cursor and displays the RGB value of that pixel or pixels on a little window. Useful for emulating a cool color when trying to create something!

You can also easily copy the RGBs as a string using the keycombo ⇧ + ⌘ + C
Items.Author: aretecore

Items.Title: 第 2 天 - java 中已检查和未检查异常的区别！ | Day 2 - Differences between checked & unchecked exceptions in java!
Items.Description:
这些是在编译时检查的。 应处理检查的异常或使用 throws 关键字通知相同的异常。

示例 - SQLException、IOException。

未经检查的异常

在运行时检查这些异常。 继承 RuntimeException 的类属于未经检查的异常。 没有必要处理这些异常。

示例 - NullPointerException、ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException。



原文:

These are checked during compile time itself. Checked Exceptions should be either handled or the same should be notified with the throws keyword.

Example - SQLException, IOException.

Unchecked Exceptions

These exceptions are checked during runtime. The classes that inherit the RuntimeException fall under unchecked exceptions. It's not necessary to handle these exceptions.

Example - NullPointerException, ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.
Items.Author: sanjaybabu

Items.Title: 终端故障 | Terminal Trouble
Items.Description:
所以我最近在我的 Macbook 上更新了 macOS 12.3。 如果你和我一样。 我们通常只运行更新而不查看更新包含的内容及其影响。 嗯……吸取的教训

新的更新有效地删除了通过code . 如果您像我这样的东西不会飞，在航站楼再一次。

修复

当我第一次遇到这个问题时。 我虽然做错了什么。 所以我重新启动了我的 Macbook，检查了我的拼写和许多其他事情，然后我决定用谷歌搜索它。 事实证明，随着这个更新，苹果删除了它对 python 2 的支持 🙄 并且 python 3 没有随 Mac 一起提供，这导致了code . 命令不再工作。

在做了一些研究之后……又名谷歌搜索。
我遇到了这个修复。

要从终端打开文件，请使用关键字“open”和文件路径/名称。

要打开 VScode 文件，请按照以下步骤操作 1) cd /usr/local/bin 2) nano code

3) change python => python3 on the following line 3) 在以下行更改 python => python3



原文:

So I recently did an update on my Macbook to the macOS 12.3. If you are anything like me. We usually just run the update without looking into what the update includes and what it will effect. 🤷🏿🤷🏿🤷🏿

Welp...lesson learned

The new update effectively removed the ability to open files in VScode via the code . in the terminal and again if your anything like me that's not going to fly.

The Fix

When I first came across this issue. I though I was doing something wrong. So I rebooted my Macbook, checked my spelling and a host of others thing before I decided to Google it. (Google is out friend).

It turns out that with this update apple removed it's support of python 2 🙄 and python 3 doesn't come with Mac which causes the code . command to not work anymore.

🤔🤔 Now what? After doing some research ...aka Googling.
I came across this fix.

To open a file from the terminal use the keyword "open" and the file path/name.

To open a VScode file follow these steps 1) cd /usr/local/bin 2) nano code

3) change python => python3 on the following line

Items.Title: 我的编码之旅的第 5 个月 | Month 5 of my Coding Journey
Items.Description:
你好开发社区，

只是想我会提供到目前为止我的旅程的最新信息。

在我学习编码的第 4 个月的过去两周里，我承认我一直在懈怠。 我没有完成我想要的那么多。 但我确实完成了前端项目。 现场直播在这里。 对于这个挑战，我主要关注移动优先的工作流程。 虽然不完美，但与我完成的其他项目相比，它仍然是一个进步。 我仍然需要提高我的移动优先技能，以及学习如何让网页在不同的浏览器中显示相同。

我还了解了 Sass。 我真的很喜欢它，而且理解起来并不复杂。 但是仍然需要在现实世界的项目中练习使用它。

我的五月目标

这个月，我会做一些不同的事情。 我将开始建立我的作品集并开始为自己做准备，这样我就可以开始申请工作了（不管需要多长时间）。 我将查找 Web 开发人员面试问题并开始练习和准备。 我还需要继续练习在项目中使用 JavaScript。 这就是我完成作品集后要做的事情。 我不想一次做太多事情而使自己不知所措。 完成我的作品集后，我将继续完成前端项目，当然还要使用 JavaScript。 这个月，我还想编写更长的时间。 通常我每天编码大约 2-3 个小时。 但与每周 5 天 8 小时轮班相比，这算不了什么。 我的目标是每天编写大约 5-6 个小时的代码。 祝我好运！🤞🏾

"Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished" -Lao Tau “自然不急，一切都已成定局”——老头



原文:

Hello Dev community,

Just thought I'd give an update on my journey thus far.

For the past two weeks during my 4th month of learning to code, I will admit that I have been slacking. I did not accomplish as much as I'd like to. But I did complete the frontendmentor project. The live site is here. For this challenge, I mainly focused on mobile-first workflow. Although not perfect, it's still an improvement compared to the other projects I've completed. I still need to work on my mobile-first skills as well as learning how to get the web page to be displayed the same in different browsers.

I also learned about Sass. I actually like it and it's not too complicated to understand. But still need to practice using it in real world projects.

My goals for May

This month, I will be doing things a bit differently. I will begin building my portfolio and start preparing myself so I can start applying for work (however long that will take). I will look up web developer interview questions and start practicing and preparing. I will also need to continue practicing working with JavaScript in projects. So that is what I will do after I've completed my portfolio. I don't want to overwhelm myself by doing too many things at once. After I've completed my portfolio, I will go back to completing frontendmentor projects and of course using JavaScript. This month, I also want to code longer hours. Usually I code for about 2-3 hours a day. But that's nothing compared to an 8 hour shift 5 days a week. My goal is to code for about 5-6 hours a day. Wish me luck guys!🤞🏾

"Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished" -Lao Tau
